message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_38.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 38: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[38]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_38.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 38: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""Cross-section repetition guard (I-deepfix-001 FIX 5, GH #1344).
2	
3	THE DEFECT (composition-collapse audit, drb_72): the composer recycled ~8 findings across 6
4	sections (e.g. Goldman's 2.5% GDP estimate appeared in 4 sections, the robot-count estimate in
5	4, the "15% of tasks" figure in 3, the Frey-Osborne method in 4). ``fact_dedup`` consolidates
6	near-restatements WITHIN a single section only, so a finding restated VERBATIM in a DIFFERENT
7	section survives — burning section space that DISTINCT findings should occupy.
8	
9	THE GUARD (this module): after per-section compose, detect a finding (a cited sentence) that
10	recurs VERBATIM across DIFFERENT sections and CONSOLIDATE it to its richest instance plus a short
11	citation-preserving back-reference, freeing section space for distinct findings.
12	
13	§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL (never a drop / cap / thin): every recycled instance KEEPS its own
14	numeric citation marker(s) inline on the back-reference — no citation is ever dropped, and NO
15	citation is moved between sections (so the per-section-local citation numbering the downstream
16	``_remap_section_markers_to_global`` relies on stays valid). Repetition is consolidated, not
17	deleted; the finding's full prose remains in its richest section.
18	
19	EXACT-RECYCLE ONLY — NOT a paraphrase-collapse (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #2): equivalence is an
20	EXACT normalized-text match (NUMERIC-citation-stripped, whitespace-collapsed, lowercased,
21	trailing-punctuation-stripped). Two sentences that differ in ANY content — a year, a figure, an
22	entity, directionality, an extra clause — have DIFFERENT normalized text and are NEVER clustered.
23	Crucially, ONLY renderer-guaranteed numeric citation markers (``[12]``) are stripped from the
24	signature; NON-citation bracketed content — a bracketed entity/label such as ``[Alpha]`` — is
25	PRESERVED in the signature (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1). So ``The model [Alpha] cut error 10% [1]``
26	and ``The model [Beta] cut error 10% [2]`` have DIFFERENT signatures and are never clustered:
27	distinct verified content can NEVER be replaced by a back-reference. A loose token-overlap
28	threshold is deliberately NOT used — it would let near-identical-but-distinct sentences cluster,
29	turning a render-only consolidation into a silent DROP (a §-1.3 violation).
30	
31	MARKER-REQUIRED — never rewrites an honest gap disclosure (Fable diff-gate iter-1 P1): a unit is an
32	eligible finding ONLY if it carries at least one NUMERIC ``[N]`` citation marker. Every
33	strict_verify-kept claim sentence carries markers (§9.1 invariant 2); the production gap / degraded
34	disclosures (``_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE`` / ``_NO_EVIDENCE_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE`` /
35	``_SECTION_FAILED_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE``) and back-references are marker-less BY DESIGN. So two starved
36	sections carrying the same gap stub can NEVER cluster, and the honest per-section gap disclosure is
37	never deleted or replaced by a false "See ... for this finding" pointer. Belt-and-braces: a section
38	flagged ``is_gap_stub=True`` is skipped entirely.
39	
40	LAYOUT-PRESERVING (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #3): a recycled instance is replaced by an in-place
41	single-occurrence substring swap on the ORIGINAL ``verified_text`` — every other byte (markdown
42	``### slot`` sub-headings, blank lines, adjacent sentences) is preserved unchanged. The guard NEVER
43	re-splits + ``" ".join``-rejoins a body (which would flatten ``### Heading`` layout into inline
44	prose and corrupt contract-section slot headings). A unit whose text contains a markdown heading
45	line (``#``-prefixed) is not eligible, and a unit that does not occur EXACTLY once in its body is
46	left untouched — precision-first: never risk corrupting real prose on a splitter/matching miss.
47	
48	FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL (constraint 1, never a relax): RENDER-ONLY. It edits ONLY the rendered
49	``verified_text`` string, and ONLY AFTER the frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI
50	entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) has already run per section. It does not touch
51	``kept_sentences_pre_resolve`` (the SentenceVerification objects the 4-role D8 gate judges), the
52	per-section verified/dropped counts, or any evidence row.
53	
54	KILL-SWITCH (LAW VI / constraint 3): ``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD`` — DEFAULT OFF. When OFF
55	(or unset) the guard is a no-op and the assembled report is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the legacy path.
56	Tunable: ``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_MIN_WORDS`` (min distinct content words per eligible finding,
57	default 6) — read from the environment (no magic numbers).
58	"""
59	from __future__ import annotations
60	
61	import logging
62	import os
63	import re
64	from typing import Any
65	
66	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.cross_section_repetition_guard")
67	
68	# ── Environment knobs (LAW VI — every threshold/flag is env-tunable, no magic numbers) ──────────
69	_ENV_ENABLED = "PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD"
70	_ENV_MIN_WORDS = "PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_MIN_WORDS"
71	
72	_MIN_WORDS_DEFAULT = 6
73	
74	_OFF_TOKENS = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
75	
76	# NUMERIC citation marker ONLY (``[1]``, ``[12]``). This is the renderer-guaranteed citation syntax
77	# at this pre-global-remap stage (§9.1 invariant 2). It is the ONLY thing stripped from the
78	# equivalence signature and is what a unit's eligibility ("is this a strict_verify-kept CLAIM
79	# sentence?") is keyed on. NON-citation bracketed content (a bracketed entity/label such as
80	# ``[Alpha]``) is deliberately NOT matched here, so it stays IN the signature and two units that
81	# differ only by such an entity are never clustered (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1 — no faithfulness leak).
82	_CITATION_NUM_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
83	
84	# Content-word tokenizer for the min-content-words eligibility gate ONLY (never the equivalence
85	# signature). Unicode-aware (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P2): ``[^\W_]+`` matches runs of Unicode letters
86	# and digits (excluding underscore), so a repeated cited finding written in a non-Latin script is
87	# still measured by real content-word count instead of silently underfiring an ASCII-only ``[a-z0-9]``.
88	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_]+", re.UNICODE)
89	
90	# Function words removed ONLY for the min-content-words eligibility count (never for the equivalence
91	# signature, which is an EXACT match). Intentionally small + generic.
92	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
93	    "a", "an", "and", "are", "as", "at", "be", "been", "but", "by", "for", "from", "had", "has",
94	    "have", "in", "into", "is", "it", "its", "of", "on", "or", "that", "the", "their", "them",
95	    "these", "they", "this", "to", "was", "were", "which", "will", "with", "would",
96	})
97	
98	# The back-reference that replaces a recycled instance. The recycled instance's own numeric
99	# citation marker(s) are appended so no citation is dropped (§-1.3 keep-all).
100	_BACKREF_TEMPLATE = 'See "{title}" for this finding.'
101	_BACKREF_FALLBACK_TITLE = "the section above"
102	
103	
104	def guard_enabled() -> bool:
105	    """Kill-switch ``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD`` — DEFAULT OFF.
106	
107	    Unset or an off token (``0`` / ``false`` / ``off`` / ``no`` / empty) => the guard is a no-op and
108	    the assembled report is byte-identical to the legacy path."""
109	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_ENABLED)
110	    if raw is None:
111	        return False
112	    return str(raw).strip().lower() not in _OFF_TOKENS
113	
114	
115	def _min_content_words() -> int:
116	    """Minimum distinct content words for a sentence to be an eligible finding (default 6). A
117	    malformed or non-positive value falls back to the default."""
118	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_MIN_WORDS, "")
119	    if not raw.strip():
120	        return _MIN_WORDS_DEFAULT
121	    try:
122	        val = int(raw.strip())
123	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
124	        return _MIN_WORDS_DEFAULT
125	    if val <= 0:
126	        return _MIN_WORDS_DEFAULT
127	    return val
128	
129	
130	def _has_numeric_citation(sentence: str) -> bool:
131	    """True iff the sentence carries at least one numeric ``[N]`` citation marker (the eligibility
132	    signal for a strict_verify-kept claim; marker-less disclosures/back-refs are excluded)."""
133	    return bool(_CITATION_NUM_RE.search(sentence))
134	
135	
136	def _contains_heading(sentence: str) -> bool:
137	    """True iff any line of the unit is a markdown heading (``#``-prefixed). Such a unit is not
138	    eligible — replacing it would delete a contract-section ``### slot`` sub-heading (layout defect)."""
139	    for line in sentence.splitlines():
140	        if line.lstrip().startswith("#"):
141	            return True
142	    return False
143	
144	
145	def _signature(sentence: str) -> str:
146	    """EXACT-recycle equivalence signature: ONLY numeric ``[N]`` citation markers removed, whitespace
147	    collapsed, lowercased, trailing sentence punctuation stripped. Two units are equivalent iff their
148	    signatures are EQUAL — any difference in content (year / figure / entity / a bracketed label such
149	    as ``[Alpha]`` / direction / extra clause) yields a different signature, so distinct verified
150	    content can NEVER be clustered (no faithfulness leak). Non-citation bracketed content is preserved
151	    verbatim in the signature (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1)."""
152	    no_cite = _CITATION_NUM_RE.sub(" ", sentence)
153	    collapsed = " ".join(no_cite.split()).lower()
154	    return collapsed.rstrip(".!?;:, ")
155	
156	
157	def _content_word_count(sentence: str) -> int:
158	    """Number of distinct content-word tokens (stopwords + numeric citations removed) — the min-words
159	    gate. Bracketed entity words (e.g. ``Alpha`` in ``[Alpha]``) count as content."""
160	    no_cite = _CITATION_NUM_RE.sub(" ", sentence)
161	    return len({t for t in _WORD_RE.findall(no_cite.lower()) if t not in _STOPWORDS})
162	
163	
164	def _citation_markers(sentence: str) -> list[str]:
165	    """Ordered list of the unit's numeric ``[N]`` citation marker(s), kept verbatim for the
166	    back-reference so no citation is ever dropped (§-1.3 keep-all)."""
167	    return _CITATION_NUM_RE.findall(sentence)
168	
169	
170	def _backref_sentence(title: str, citations: list[str]) -> str:
171	    """Build the back-reference that replaces a recycled instance, preserving its citations."""
172	    clean_title = (title or "").strip() or _BACKREF_FALLBACK_TITLE
173	    base = _BACKREF_TEMPLATE.format(title=clean_title)
174	    if citations:
175	        return f"{base} {''.join(citations)}"
176	    return base
177	
178	
179	def consolidate_cross_section_repetition(section_results: list[Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
180	    """Consolidate findings that recur VERBATIM across DIFFERENT sections down to a richest instance
181	    plus a citation-preserving back-reference. Mutates each affected ``SectionResult.verified_text``
182	    IN PLACE via a single-occurrence substring swap (layout-preserving). Returns a telemetry dict
183	    ``{clusters, consolidated}`` (empty dict when the guard is OFF).
184	
185	    Contract:
186	      * OFF (flag unset / off token) -> ``{}`` and NO mutation (byte-identical legacy output).
187	      * Eligible finding = a sentence that (a) carries >= 1 numeric ``[N]`` marker, (b) has
188	        >= min_content_words distinct content words, (c) contains NO markdown heading line, in a
189	        section that is not dropped and not ``is_gap_stub``.
190	      * Equivalence = EXACT normalized-text match (NOT a loose overlap threshold); ONLY numeric
191	        citations are stripped, so bracketed non-citation content stays distinguishing.
192	      * A cluster consolidates ONLY when it spans >= 2 DISTINCT sections.
193	      * The richest instance (most citation markers; earliest section/sentence on a tie) is KEPT
194	        verbatim; every OTHER instance in a DIFFERENT section is replaced IN PLACE by a
195	        back-reference carrying that instance's OWN marker(s) — no citation dropped, none moved.
196	      * A unit that does not occur EXACTLY once in its body is left untouched (precision fail-safe).
197	      * Same-section duplicates are left to ``fact_dedup`` (never consolidated here).
198	      * A ``dropped_due_to_failure`` / ``is_gap_stub`` / empty section is EXCLUDED from the unit set,
199	        so it is never a cluster member, never the richest instance, and never a back-reference
200	        TARGET. The ``dropped_due_to_failure`` predicate is the SAME one the downstream render filter
201	        uses (``if not sr.dropped_due_to_failure``), so a rendered section can NEVER be collapsed into
202	        a back-reference to a non-rendered section (Codex diff-gate P1: no final-output content loss).
203	    """
204	    if not guard_enabled():
205	        return {}
206	
207	    # Lazy import to avoid any generator-package import-order coupling.
208	    from .verified_compose import split_into_sentences  # noqa: PLC0415
209	
210	    min_words = _min_content_words()
211	
212	    # Per-section eligible-unit lists. ``sections[i]`` is None for a section that is dropped, empty,
213	    # or a gap stub (left completely untouched).
214	    sections: list[dict[str, Any] | None] = []
215	    for sr in section_results:
216	        # Codex diff-gate P1 (dropped-section safety): a dropped_due_to_failure section (the EXACT
217	        # predicate the downstream render filter uses — ``if not sr.dropped_due_to_failure``), a gap
218	        # stub, or an empty section is set to None and NEVER contributes a unit. So it can never be a
219	        # cluster member, the richest instance, or a back-reference TARGET — a rendered section is
220	        # never collapsed into a back-reference to a non-rendered section (no final-output content loss).
221	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False) or getattr(sr, "is_gap_stub", False):
222	            sections.append(None)
223	            continue
224	        text = getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or ""
225	        if not text.strip():
226	            sections.append(None)
227	            continue
228	        sections.append({"sr": sr, "text": text})
229	
230	    # Flat list of eligible finding-units across all in-scope sections. Each unit keeps the RAW
231	    # sentence text (for the in-place substring swap) and its normalized signature (for clustering).
232	    units: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
233	    for sec_pos, sec in enumerate(sections):
234	        if sec is None:
235	            continue
236	        for sent_pos, sent in enumerate(split_into_sentences(sec["text"])):
237	            if _contains_heading(sent):
238	                continue
239	            if not _has_numeric_citation(sent):
240	                continue
241	            if _content_word_count(sent) < min_words:
242	                continue
243	            units.append({
244	                "sec_pos": sec_pos,
245	                "sent_pos": sent_pos,
246	                "raw": sent,
247	                "sig": _signature(sent),
248	                "citations": _citation_markers(sent),
249	            })
250	
251	    if len(units) < 2:
252	        return {"clusters": 0, "consolidated": 0}
253	
254	    # Cluster by EXACT signature (deterministic; insertion-ordered members).
255	    groups: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
256	    for i, unit in enumerate(units):
257	        groups.setdefault(unit["sig"], []).append(i)
258	
259	    # Plan the in-place swaps (do not mutate while clustering).
260	    replacements: dict[int, list[dict[str, str]]] = {}
261	    clusters = 0
262	    consolidated = 0
263	    for members in groups.values():
264	        distinct_secs = {units[m]["sec_pos"] for m in members}
265	        if len(distinct_secs) < 2:
266	            continue
267	        clusters += 1
268	        # Richest = most citation markers; earliest (section, sentence) breaks ties deterministically
269	        # (negated positions so ``max`` prefers the earliest instance).
270	        richest = max(
271	            members,
272	            key=lambda m: (
273	                len(units[m]["citations"]),
274	                -units[m]["sec_pos"],
275	                -units[m]["sent_pos"],
276	            ),
277	        )
278	        richest_sec = units[richest]["sec_pos"]
279	        richest_title = getattr(sections[richest_sec]["sr"], "title", "") or ""  # type: ignore[index]
280	        for m in members:
281	            if m == richest or units[m]["sec_pos"] == richest_sec:
282	                # The kept instance, or a same-section twin (that is fact_dedup's job) — never here.
283	                continue
284	            replacements.setdefault(units[m]["sec_pos"], []).append({
285	                "old": units[m]["raw"],
286	                "new": _backref_sentence(richest_title, units[m]["citations"]),
287	            })
288	
289	    # Apply the swaps in place, one section at a time. Each swap targets EXACTLY one occurrence; a
290	    # unit that is absent or ambiguous (count != 1) in the current body is skipped (fail-safe).
291	    for sec_pos, swaps in replacements.items():
292	        sec = sections[sec_pos]
293	        if sec is None:
294	            continue
295	        text = sec["sr"].verified_text
296	        changed = False
297	        for swap in swaps:
298	            old = swap["old"]
299	            if text.count(old) != 1:
300	                continue
301	            text = text.replace(old, swap["new"], 1)
302	            changed = True
303	            consolidated += 1
304	        if changed:
305	            sec["sr"].verified_text = text
306	
307	    if consolidated:
308	        logger.info(
309	            "[cross_section_repetition_guard] consolidated %d recycled finding instance(s) across "
310	            "sections into %d cross-section cluster(s); every recycled citation preserved as a "
311	            "back-reference (§-1.3 consolidate-keep-all; frozen faithfulness engine untouched)",
312	            consolidated, clusters,
313	        )
314	
315	    return {"clusters": clusters, "consolidated": consolidated}
316

1	"""I-deepfix-001 M6 (Layer 1) — the VERIFIED cross-source analytical-synthesis composer.
2	
3	POLARIS had NO verified analytical-synthesis path, so DRB-II analysis (18% weight) scored near-zero
4	and "Comparative Assessment" rendered a gap stub. The within-basket multi-cited producer
5	(``verified_compose.compose_basket_multicited_sentence``) consolidates corroborators WITHIN one
6	basket and joins them with a SEMANTICALLY-NEUTRAL connective; it never spans TWO baskets and the
7	``relational_quantifier_guard`` actively STRIPS any relational predicate. There was no producer that
8	emits a sentence relating TWO distinct baskets.
9	
10	THE M6 ARCHITECTURE (the only faithfulness-safe way to add analysis depth):
11	
12	    An analytical sentence = ``[verified clause A][LICENSED relation connective][verified clause B]``
13	
14	where (1) each clause is an existing ``strict_verify``-PASSED unit carrying its OWN
15	``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` token (built by the UNCHANGED ``verified_compose`` per-basket contract),
16	and (2) the connective is from a CLOSED set and is LICENSED by an existing cross-basket relation
17	engine (``claim_graph.ClaimGraph.edges`` ContradictionEdge from the three certified detectors, or the
18	``consolidation_nli`` / equivalence agreement map). The synthesis asserts NO new free-standing fact —
19	it asserts a RELATION between two already-verified facts, and that relation is gated by the engine.
20	
21	``strict_verify`` still passes iff BOTH atoms pass (the connective carries no provenance token), so the
22	FROZEN faithfulness engine remains the only hard gate. This module CALLS the production
23	``verify_sentence_provenance`` (through the per-basket ``verify_fn`` it is given) but NEVER modifies it.
24	It is the C2 atomic-entailment family from I-faith-001, applied at composition time: *synthesize the
25	RELATION, verify the ATOMS.*
26	
27	FAIL-CLOSED (the §-1.3 + brief boundary contract, "under-relax is safe; over-relax is lethal"):
28	  * when the licensing engine does NOT fire for a pair, the connective DEFAULTS to ``neutral`` pure
29	    juxtaposition — a wrong "in contrast" / "consistent with this" can NEVER render;
30	  * when either clause fails to build / re-verify, the analytical unit is DROPPED and the two atoms
31	    survive as independent single-source sentences (the existing per-basket path emits them) — KEEP-ALL;
32	  * "extension" (D2, I-deepfix-001) is now licensed — but ONLY by a CERTIFIED DIRECTIONAL-entailment
33	    verdict from ``consolidation_nli.entails_directional`` (clause B entails-and-extends clause A). It
34	    FAILS CLOSED to ``neutral`` on any absent/negative/error signal, is gated by ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_EXTENSION``
35	    (default-ON), and NEVER outranks a real ``conflict``. No free-form judge, no LLM guess for the word.
36	  * "agreement" (L3, I-deepfix-001 COVERAGE) is licensed on the LIVE edges-only path by an EXPLICIT
37	    BIDIRECTIONAL-equivalence verdict — clause A entails clause B AND clause B entails clause A (BOTH
38	    directions entail), the symmetric consolidation-merge predicate, asked of the SAME certified engine
39	    ``consolidation_nli.entails_directional`` in both directions. Before L3 the live composer passed only
40	    ``edges`` (never an ``agree_map``/``equiv_clusters``), so agreement could NEVER fire live; L3 wires the
41	    explicit signal so two clauses that are the SAME claim corroborated render "; consistent with this, ".
42	    It FAILS CLOSED to ``neutral`` on any one-way / absent / negative / error signal, is gated by
43	    ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_AGREEMENT`` (default-ON), and NEVER outranks a real ``conflict``. No free-form judge,
44	    no LLM guess for the word.
45	
46	LAW VI: gated by ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS`` (read by the caller in ``verified_compose``); when the
47	flag is off this module is never invoked and the section producer is byte-identical. PURE: no network,
48	no model, no faithfulness-file import, no input mutation. snake_case.
49	"""
50	from __future__ import annotations
51	
52	import logging
53	import os
54	import re
55	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
56	
57	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
58	
59	# The CLOSED set of cross-source connectives. The VALUE is inserted between the two verified clauses;
60	# it carries NO factual content and NO provenance token, so ``strict_verify`` still gates iff both atoms
61	# pass. ``neutral`` is pure juxtaposition (the fail-closed default). The ``agreement`` / ``conflict`` /
62	# ``extension`` phrases are kept in sync with ``relational_quantifier_guard._ANALYTICAL_CONNECTIVE_RELATION``
63	# (the guard neutralizes an UNLICENSED one back to ``neutral``). Wave-2a NOTE (deliberate, brief decision 4):
64	# ``comparison`` (``"; for comparison, "``) is DELIBERATELY NOT added to the guard's regex — this composer
65	# is the SOLE emitter of it and licenses "comparison" FIRST (the fail-closed numeric comparator), so there
66	# is no unlicensed "for comparison" for the guard to neutralize; adding it to the guard would risk mutating
67	# a verbatim-atom source-prose "for comparison" on the DEFAULT-ON ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS`` path.
68	LICENSED_CONNECTIVES: dict[str, str] = {
69	    "agreement": "; consistent with this, ",
70	    "conflict": "; in contrast, ",
71	    "extension": "; extending this, ",  # D2: licensed by a certified directional-entailment verdict
72	    "comparison": "; for comparison, ",  # Wave-2a: licensed by the deterministic FAIL-CLOSED numeric comparator (non-directional)
73	    "neutral": "; separately, ",
74	}
75	
76	# A provenance token: ``[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]`` (the shape strict_verify parses).
77	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
78	_EV_SPAN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:(?P<ev_id>[A-Za-z0-9_]+):(?P<start>\d+)-(?P<end>\d+)\]")
79

81	def _norm_anchor(text: str) -> str:
82	    """The conservative normalized anchor key for a basket's subject (or predicate). Lower-cased,
83	    whitespace-collapsed, punctuation-trimmed. An empty / whitespace string is NOT an anchor (returns
84	    "") so empty-subject baskets are never paired (random juxtaposition is forbidden, brief risk #3)."""
85	    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", str(text or "").strip().lower())
86	    s = s.strip(" \t\r\n.,;:-—–")
87	    return s
88	
89	
90	def _basket_anchor(basket: Any) -> str:
91	    """A basket's pairing anchor = normalized ``subject`` + "|" + normalized ``predicate``. Two baskets
92	    are pairing CANDIDATES iff they share this anchor (same subject AND predicate); the RELATION between
93	    them is then decided by the licensing engine, never by the anchor. "" when subject is blank (the
94	    basket is not eligible for analytical pairing — it has no anchor to compare against)."""
95	    subj = _norm_anchor(getattr(basket, "subject", "") or "")
96	    if not subj:
97	        return ""
98	    pred = _norm_anchor(getattr(basket, "predicate", "") or "")
99	    return f"{subj}|{pred}"
100	
101	
102	def _cluster_id(basket: Any) -> str:
103	    return str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
104	
105	
106	def _edge_between(edges: Any, cluster_a_id: str, cluster_b_id: str) -> bool:
107	    """True iff a ContradictionEdge joins the two claim clusters (``claim_graph.ClaimGraph.edges`` /
108	    ``CredibilityAnalysis.edges``). An edge's ``claim_cluster_ids`` is the sorted pair of endpoints; we
109	    accept any edge whose endpoint set CONTAINS both cluster ids (recall-first, mirrors the recall-first
110	    edge sourcing). Conservative on missing/garbage data -> False (no conflict licensed)."""
111	    if not edges or not cluster_a_id or not cluster_b_id or cluster_a_id == cluster_b_id:
112	        return False
113	    want = {cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id}
114	    for e in edges:
115	        ids = getattr(e, "claim_cluster_ids", None)
116	        if ids is None and isinstance(e, dict):
117	            ids = e.get("claim_cluster_ids")
118	        try:
119	            id_set = {str(x) for x in (ids or ())}
120	        except TypeError:
121	            continue
122	        if want <= id_set:
123	            return True
124	    return False
125	
126	
127	def _agree(agree_map: Any, equiv_clusters: Any, cluster_a_id: str, cluster_b_id: str) -> bool:
128	    """True iff the consolidation/equivalence engine says cluster A and cluster B AGREE (A entails/equiv
129	    B). The signal is OPTIONAL: the run threads it only when an inter-basket agreement map was computed.
130	
131	    ``agree_map`` may be a dict ``cluster_id -> iterable[cluster_id]`` (or set of frozenset/tuple pairs);
132	    ``equiv_clusters`` may be a list of clusters (each an iterable of cluster_ids) that were merged as
133	    equivalent. Conservative: empty / unreadable => False (NO agreement licensed -> neutral). Never a
134	    network/model call (the licensing already happened upstream; this is a pure lookup)."""
135	    if not cluster_a_id or not cluster_b_id or cluster_a_id == cluster_b_id:
136	        return False
137	    pair = {cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id}
138	    # (1) dict form: cluster -> neighbours that it entails/agrees-with (either direction).
139	    if isinstance(agree_map, dict):
140	        try:
141	            if cluster_b_id in (agree_map.get(cluster_a_id) or ()):
142	                return True
143	            if cluster_a_id in (agree_map.get(cluster_b_id) or ()):
144	                return True
145	        except TypeError:
146	            pass
147	    # (2) set/list of pairs form.
148	    elif agree_map:
149	        for entry in agree_map:
150	            try:
151	                if pair <= {str(x) for x in entry}:
152	                    return True
153	            except TypeError:
154	                continue
155	    # (3) equivalence groups: both ids inside the SAME merged group => they agree.
156	    for group in (equiv_clusters or ()):
157	        try:
158	            members = {str(x) for x in group}
159	        except TypeError:
160	            continue
161	        if pair <= members:
162	            return True
163	    return False
164

166	def license_relation(
167	    cluster_a_id: str,
168	    cluster_b_id: str,
169	    *,
170	    edges: Any = None,
171	    equiv_clusters: Any = None,
172	    agree_map: Any = None,
173	    directional_entails: Optional[bool] = None,
174	    bidirectional_entails: Optional[bool] = None,
175	) -> str:
176	    """Decide the LICENSED relation between two claim clusters from the EXISTING certified engines.
177	
178	    Returns one of ``conflict`` / ``agreement`` / ``extension`` / ``neutral``:
179	      * ``conflict``   iff a ContradictionEdge joins the pair (the certified semantic/qualitative/rule
180	        contradiction detectors) — surfaces the disagreement ("in contrast"), the DeepTRACE
181	        one-sidedness win, ONLY ever from a real verified edge;
182	      * ``agreement``  iff EITHER (i) an EXPLICIT BIDIRECTIONAL-equivalence verdict positively confirms
183	        that clause A entails clause B AND clause B entails clause A (``bidirectional_entails is True`` —
184	        the L3 COVERAGE signal the caller computes from the certified engine
185	        ``consolidation_nli.entails_directional`` asked in BOTH directions, the symmetric consolidation
186	        merge predicate) OR (ii) the consolidation/equivalence lookup (``agree_map`` / ``equiv_clusters``)
187	        says A entails/equiv B — either way the two clauses are the SAME claim corroborated ("consistent
188	        with this"). ``bidirectional_entails`` licenses agreement ONLY when EXACTLY ``True``; ``None`` /
189	        ``False`` (no signal, a one-way entailment, a confident non-equivalence, or an infra fault) adds
190	        NOTHING here and FAILS CLOSED (the ``agree_map`` lookup or neutral decides). The live edges-only
191	        path carries no ``agree_map``, so before L3 agreement could never fire; the explicit signal is
192	        what wires it. A WRONG "consistent with this" is a relation fabrication, so it is gated by the
193	        engine and NEVER by a free-form / LLM guess for the word;
194	      * ``extension``  iff a CERTIFIED DIRECTIONAL-entailment signal positively confirms that clause B
195	        (the SECOND, continuation clause the "; extending this, " connective points at) entails-and-adds
196	        to clause A — i.e. B is a proper superset/elaboration of A, so "A; extending this, B" is faithful.
197	        The signal is ``directional_entails`` (computed by the caller from the certified engine
198	        ``consolidation_nli.entails_directional``, the ONE-directional NLI counterpart to the bidirectional
199	        consolidation merge). The caller sets it ``True`` ONLY on a PROPER one-way entailment — B entails A
200	        AND A does NOT entail B (a confident reverse non-entailment), so a bidirectional PARAPHRASE
201	        (equivalence) is excluded at the signal and can never reach the extension branch here even when the
202	        agreement-precedence inputs (``agree_map`` / ``equiv_clusters``) are absent on the live edges-only
203	        path. It licenses ``extension`` ONLY when EXACTLY ``True``; ``None`` / ``False``
204	        (no signal, a confident non-entailment, an equivalence, or an infra fault) FAIL-CLOSED to neutral.
205	        This is a NEW RELATION WORD between two already strict_verify-PASSED atoms; a WRONG "extension" is
206	        a relation fabrication, so it is gated by the engine and NEVER by a free-form / LLM guess;
207	      * ``neutral``    otherwise — pure juxtaposition. NEVER fabricate a relation the engines did not
208	        license (over-relax is the lethal direction).
209	
210	    Precedence (most-specific-faithful first): ``conflict`` ALWAYS wins (a contested pair is reported as a
211	    conflict, never smoothed into agreement/extension). Then ``agreement`` (a bidirectional equivalence is
212	    "consistent with", not an "extension" — a truly equivalent B adds no information); the explicit
213	    ``bidirectional_entails`` signal and the ``agree_map``/``equiv_clusters`` lookup are the two agreement
214	    sources and are checked BEFORE extension so an equivalence is never mislabeled an extension. Then
215	    ``extension`` (a PROPER one-way directional entailment, which a bidirectional equivalence never
216	    contains — the two are mutually exclusive: extension needs ``a_entails_b`` False, agreement needs it
217	    True). Then ``neutral``. ``bidirectional_entails`` / ``directional_entails`` are WEIGHTED signals
218	    surfaced as a connective word; neither is ever a DROP / CAP / FILTER (§-1.3)."""
219	    if _edge_between(edges, cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id):
220	        return "conflict"
221	    if bidirectional_entails is True or _agree(
222	        agree_map, equiv_clusters, cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id
223	    ):
224	        return "agreement"
225	    if directional_entails is True:
226	        return "extension"
227	    return "neutral"
228

230	def _distinct_ev_ids(text: str) -> set[str]:
231	    """The distinct evidence_ids cited by the provenance tokens in ``text``."""
232	    return {m.group("ev_id") for m in _EV_SPAN_RE.finditer(text or "")}
233	
234	
235	# LAW VI — the fine flag for the D2 "extension" relation. DEFAULT-ON (the whole cross-source path is
236	# gated by ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS`` upstream in ``verified_compose``; this only lets an operator
237	# switch OFF the directional-extension word without disabling conflict/agreement). Even when ON,
238	# ``extension`` is FAIL-CLOSED: it renders ONLY on a positive certified directional-entailment verdict.
239	_ENV_EXTENSION_FLAG = "PG_CROSS_SOURCE_EXTENSION"
240	
241	
242	def cross_source_extension_enabled() -> bool:
243	    """``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_EXTENSION`` — the D2 extension-relation switch. DEFAULT-ON. OFF => the composer
244	    never asks the directional engine and ``license_relation`` never sees ``directional_entails=True``,
245	    so the connective can only ever be conflict / agreement / neutral (the pre-D2 behavior). This is a
246	    WEIGHT switch, never a hard cap on breadth (§-1.3)."""
247	    return os.getenv(_ENV_EXTENSION_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
248	
249	
250	# LAW VI — the fine flag for the L3 COVERAGE "agreement" relation. DEFAULT-ON (the whole cross-source path
251	# is gated by ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS`` upstream in ``verified_compose``; this only lets an operator
252	# switch OFF the explicit bidirectional-equivalence agreement word without disabling conflict/extension).
253	# Even when ON, ``agreement`` is FAIL-CLOSED: it renders ONLY on a positive certified BIDIRECTIONAL
254	# (both-directions-entail) verdict (or a real ``agree_map``/``equiv_clusters`` lookup).
255	_ENV_AGREEMENT_FLAG = "PG_CROSS_SOURCE_AGREEMENT"
256	
257	
258	def cross_source_agreement_enabled() -> bool:
259	    """``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_AGREEMENT`` — the L3 explicit bidirectional-equivalence agreement switch.
260	    DEFAULT-ON. OFF => the composer never asks the engine for the bidirectional signal and
261	    ``license_relation`` never sees ``bidirectional_entails=True``, so agreement can then only be licensed
262	    by an ``agree_map``/``equiv_clusters`` lookup (which the LIVE edges-only path does not carry — i.e. the
263	    pre-L3 behavior where agreement never fired live). This is a WEIGHT switch, never a hard cap on breadth
264	    (§-1.3)."""
265	    return os.getenv(_ENV_AGREEMENT_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
266	
267	
268	def _strip_ev_tokens(clause: str) -> str:
269	    """The clause prose with its ``[#ev:...]`` provenance tokens removed and whitespace collapsed — the
270	    CLEAN natural-language text handed to the NLI cross-encoder. The rendered clause KEEPS its token
271	    (this only affects the signal input, never the emitted sentence)."""
272	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", clause or "")).strip()
273	
274	
275	# ── I-deepfix-001 Wave-2 (#1370): COMPOSE-STAGE ANTI-GLUE for topically-unrelated NEUTRAL pairs ──────
276	# LAW VI: DEFAULT-OFF (``PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE``). The cross-source composer joins two verified
277	# single-source clauses with ``LICENSED_CONNECTIVES[rel]``. When the relation is NEUTRAL (no engine
278	# licensed a conflict/agreement/extension/comparison), the connective is ``"; separately, "`` — pure
279	# juxtaposition. box2 showed that this GLUES two TOPICALLY-UNRELATED spans into one run-on sentence
280	# (e.g. "...a Gaussian distribution; separately, Informant 17 summarized..."), which reads as incoherent
281	# glue. This lever renders such a NEUTRAL + topically-unrelated pair as SEPARATE sentences (a period
282	# break) instead of gluing them. FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: the SAME two clauses, the SAME provenance tokens,
283	# the SAME citations survive — only the JOIN character changes from ``"; separately, "`` to a sentence
284	# break. It never touches strict_verify / NLI / provenance and only ever acts on the ``neutral`` branch
285	# (a licensed conflict/agreement/extension/comparison connective is NEVER split). OFF => byte-identical.
286	_ENV_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE = "PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE"
287	_ENV_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP = "PG_COMPOSE_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP"
288	_DEFAULT_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP = 2
289	
290	# The grammatical connective-tissue words that do NOT signal a shared TOPIC (mirrors the strict_verify
291	# grounding-stopword intent, kept LOCAL so this module imports NO faithfulness file — its PURE contract).
292	_GLUE_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
293	    "the", "and", "are", "was", "were", "for", "from", "with", "that", "this",
294	    "these", "those", "its", "has", "have", "had", "will", "would", "can",
295	    "could", "been", "being", "also", "more", "most", "other", "any", "all",
296	    "each", "both", "only", "just", "even", "new", "old", "one", "two", "three",
297	    "four", "five", "such", "which", "their", "there", "than", "then", "over",
298	    "under", "into", "onto", "very", "used", "using", "some", "many",
299	})
300	
301	
302	def compose_no_unrelated_glue_enabled() -> bool:
303	    """``PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE`` gate (default OFF, LAW VI). OFF => the composer glues a NEUTRAL
304	    pair with ``"; separately, "`` exactly as before (byte-identical). ON => a NEUTRAL pair whose two
305	    clauses share FEWER than the min content-word overlap is rendered as SEPARATE sentences. A rendering
306	    (readability) lever, never a drop / cap / thinner (§-1.3) — both clauses + all citations survive."""
307	    return os.getenv(_ENV_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
308	
309	
310	def _no_glue_min_overlap() -> int:
311	    """The minimum shared content-word count for two NEUTRAL clauses to be treated as TOPICALLY RELATED
312	    (and therefore kept glued). Default 2 (mirrors the strict_verify >=2 content-word grounding floor); a
313	    pair sharing FEWER is topically UNRELATED and split. LAW VI env-tunable; a non-positive / unparseable
314	    value falls back to the default (never 0, which would split even a related pair)."""
315	    raw = os.getenv(_ENV_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP, "").strip()
316	    if not raw:
317	        return _DEFAULT_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP
318	    try:
319	        val = int(raw)
320	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
321	        return _DEFAULT_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP
322	    return val if val >= 1 else _DEFAULT_NO_GLUE_MIN_OVERLAP
323	
324	
325	def _glue_content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
326	    """The lower-cased content words (alphabetic, length >=3, minus ``_GLUE_STOPWORDS``) of a clause with
327	    its ``[#ev:...]`` provenance tokens removed — the topical-overlap signal. Pure; deterministic."""
328	    clean = _strip_ev_tokens(text).lower()
329	    return {w for w in re.findall(r"[a-z][a-z\-]{2,}", clean) if w not in _GLUE_STOPWORDS}
330	
331	
332	def _clauses_topically_related(clause_a: str, clause_b: str) -> bool:
333	    """True iff the two clauses share at least ``_no_glue_min_overlap()`` content words (topically
334	    related => keep glued). An empty content-word set on EITHER clause reads as UNRELATED (returns
335	    False => split) — never glue when there is no shared topic to justify the co-location. Pure."""
336	    words_a = _glue_content_words(clause_a)
337	    words_b = _glue_content_words(clause_b)
338	    if not words_a or not words_b:
339	        return False
340	    return len(words_a & words_b) >= _no_glue_min_overlap()
341	
342	
343	def _ensure_sentence_terminal(clause: str) -> str:
344	    """The clause with a single sentence terminal guaranteed at the end: unchanged when it already ends in
345	    a terminal (``. ! ?``) or a provenance ``]`` (which the production sentence splitter treats as a
346	    terminal), else a ``.`` is appended. Pure; never touches a provenance token."""
347	    c = (clause or "").rstrip()
348	    if not c:
349	        return c
350	    return c if c[-1] in ".!?]" else c + "."
351	
352	
353	def _render_separate_sentences(clause_a: str, clause_b: str) -> Optional[str]:
354	    """Render two verified clauses as TWO SEPARATE sentences (a terminal break + a space), each KEEPING
355	    its OWN leading capital and its OWN ``[#ev:...]`` token(s). This is the anti-glue alternative to the
356	    ``"; separately, "`` connective: the SAME spans, the SAME citations survive — only the join becomes a
357	    sentence break. Returns None when EITHER clause is empty (the caller then drops the unit)."""
358	    first = _ensure_sentence_terminal(clause_a)
359	    second = _ensure_sentence_terminal((clause_b or "").strip())
360	    if not first or not second:
361	        return None
362	    return f"{first} {second}"
363

365	def _join_analytical_pair(clause_a: str, clause_b: str, rel: str) -> Optional[str]:
366	    """Join two already-verified single-source clauses into ONE analytical unit for the licensed relation
367	    ``rel``, returning the guarded final string (or None if the join collapses).
368	
369	    DEFAULT (byte-identical): glue ``clause_a`` + ``LICENSED_CONNECTIVES[rel]`` + ``clause_b`` via the
370	    shared ``_join_verified_clauses`` contract, then run ``guard_relational_quantifier`` (defense-in-depth
371	    against an unlicensed connective).
372	
373	    ``PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE`` (default OFF, LAW VI): when ``rel`` is ``neutral`` (no engine licensed
374	    a relation) AND the two clauses are TOPICALLY UNRELATED (share fewer than ``_no_glue_min_overlap()``
375	    content words), render them as SEPARATE sentences instead of the ``"; separately, "`` glue. The guard
376	    still runs (a no-op on a connective-free two-sentence string). FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: the SAME two
377	    clauses, the SAME tokens, the SAME citations — ONLY the JOIN becomes a sentence break. This never
378	    touches strict_verify / NLI / provenance and never acts on a licensed connective."""
379	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
380	        _join_verified_clauses,
381	        _strip_terminal_punct,
382	    )
383	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.relational_quantifier_guard import (  # noqa: PLC0415
384	        guard_relational_quantifier,
385	    )
386	    if (
387	        rel == "neutral"
388	        and compose_no_unrelated_glue_enabled()
389	        and not _clauses_topically_related(clause_a, clause_b)
390	    ):
391	        joined = _render_separate_sentences(clause_a, clause_b)
392	    else:
393	        connective = LICENSED_CONNECTIVES.get(rel, LICENSED_CONNECTIVES["neutral"])
394	        # Strip clause_A's terminal so the join reads as one flowing sentence "[clause A]<connective>[clause B]".
395	        joined = _join_verified_clauses(
396	            [_strip_terminal_punct(clause_a), clause_b], connective=connective,
397	        )
398	    if not joined:
399	        return None
400	    # The guard neutralizes an UNLICENSED connective (defense-in-depth: our composer only ever emits a
401	    # licensed one; a two-sentence separate rendering carries no connective, so the guard is a no-op).
402	    # ``licensed_relations`` is the SINGLE relation we licensed for this sentence.
403	    guarded = guard_relational_quantifier(joined, None, licensed_relations={rel})
404	    return (guarded or joined).strip()

408	    """The CERTIFIED directional-entailment engine — ``consolidation_nli.entails_directional`` (the
409	    one-directional NLI counterpart to the bidirectional consolidation merge, reading ONLY the forward
410	    logits at the SAME entailment-argmax threshold ``_entails`` the consolidation leg uses). It reuses
411	    the RESIDENT cross-encoder the consolidation leg already loads (zero extra OpenRouter/GPU spend) and
412	    FAIL-CLOSES to ``None`` on any infra fault. Returns None only if the module cannot be imported at
413	    all (then the extension signal is unavailable -> fail-closed to no-extension)."""
414	    try:
415	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import (  # noqa: PLC0415
416	            entails_directional,
417	        )
418	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — signal unavailable => fail-closed (no extension)
419	        logger.warning(
420	            "[cross_source_synthesis] certified directional-entailment engine unavailable (%s); "
421	            "extension relation fail-closed to neutral", exc,
422	        )
423	        return None
424	    return entails_directional

427	def _safe_entail(
428	    fn: Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]], premise: str, hypothesis: str,
429	) -> Optional[bool]:
430	    """ONE certified directional-entailment verdict, FAIL-CLOSED to ``None`` on any engine fault. Never
431	    raises into the composer and never fabricates a relation — an infra fault reads as "no signal", so the
432	    connective falls to ``neutral`` (over-relax is the lethal direction)."""
433	    try:
434	        return fn(premise, hypothesis)
435	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — an engine fault FAIL-CLOSES to neutral (never a relation)
436	        logger.warning(

438	            "relation signal fail-closed to neutral", exc,

443	def _pair_entailment_verdicts(
444	    clause_a: str,
445	    clause_b: str,
446	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]],
447	) -> tuple[Optional[bool], Optional[bool]]:
448	    """The TWO certified directional NLI verdicts for the ordered clause pair, SHARED by both the
449	    ``extension`` (directional) and ``agreement`` (bidirectional-equivalence) relation signals so the
450	    resident cross-encoder is asked at most ONCE per direction per pair (no duplicate forward passes on
451	    the hot path). Reads ONLY the CLEAN token-stripped clause prose (the rendered clause keeps its token).
452	
453	    Returns ``(a_entails_b, b_entails_a)`` where each element is the ``consolidation_nli.entails_directional``
454	    verdict — ``True`` (entails), ``False`` (a CONFIDENT non-entailment), or ``None`` (empty text /
455	    unavailable engine / infra fault — the FAIL-CLOSED sentinel). Each direction fails closed INDEPENDENTLY;
456	    a fault on one never fabricates a verdict for the other. Never an LLM/judge call — the SAME resident
457	    cross-encoder the consolidation leg already loads (zero extra OpenRouter/GPU spend)."""
458	    fn = entail_fn if entail_fn is not None else _default_entail_fn()
459	    if fn is None:
460	        return (None, None)
461	    clause_a_text = _strip_ev_tokens(clause_a)
462	    clause_b_text = _strip_ev_tokens(clause_b)
463	    if not clause_a_text or not clause_b_text:
464	        return (None, None)
465	    a_entails_b = _safe_entail(fn, clause_a_text, clause_b_text)  # does clause_a entail clause_b?
466	    b_entails_a = _safe_entail(fn, clause_b_text, clause_a_text)  # does clause_b entail clause_a?
467	    return (a_entails_b, b_entails_a)

470	def _extension_from_verdicts(
471	    a_entails_b: Optional[bool], b_entails_a: Optional[bool],
472	) -> Optional[bool]:
473	    """The ``extension`` licence derived from the two shared verdicts: clause_b entails clause_a (the
474	    SUPPORT direction) AND clause_a does NOT entail clause_b (a CONFIDENT reverse non-entailment => clause_b
475	    is a PROPER superset, not an equivalence). ``True`` ONLY on that proper one-way entailment; any other
476	    combination — including a reverse ``True`` (equivalence), a reverse ``None`` (unavailable), or a forward
477	    that is not ``True`` — returns ``None`` (FAIL-CLOSED to neutral, never a wrong "extending this")."""
478	    if b_entails_a is True and a_entails_b is False:
479	        return True
480	    return None

483	def _bidirectional_agreement_from_verdicts(
484	    a_entails_b: Optional[bool], b_entails_a: Optional[bool],
485	) -> Optional[bool]:
486	    """The ``agreement`` licence derived from the two shared verdicts: a BIDIRECTIONAL equivalence — clause_a
487	    entails clause_b AND clause_b entails clause_a (BOTH directions entail, the symmetric consolidation-merge
488	    predicate the L3 COVERAGE lever wires to the LIVE edges-only path). ``True`` ONLY when BOTH are ``True``;
489	    any ``False`` / ``None`` (a one-way entailment, a confident non-equivalence, an unavailable engine, or an
490	    infra fault) returns ``None`` (FAIL-CLOSED to neutral, never a wrong "consistent with this"). Mutually
491	    exclusive with ``_extension_from_verdicts`` by construction (extension needs ``a_entails_b`` False)."""
492	    if a_entails_b is True and b_entails_a is True:
493	        return True
494	    return None

497	def _directional_extension_signal(
498	    clause_a: str,
499	    clause_b: str,
500	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]],
501	) -> Optional[bool]:
502	    """Compute the CERTIFIED directional-entailment verdict that licenses ``extension`` for the ordered
503	    pair ``(clause_a, clause_b)``.
504	
505	    The rendered sentence is ``[clause_a]; extending this, [clause_b]`` — the "; extending this, "
506	    connective points BACK at clause_a, so clause_b is the clause that EXTENDS/elaborates it. For that to
507	    be faithful, clause_b must be a PROPER superset of clause_a: clause_b must ADD information, not merely
508	    restate it. A mere bidirectional PARAPHRASE (clause_b entails clause_a AND clause_a entails clause_b)
509	    adds NOTHING — the faithful word there is "consistent with" (agreement), not "extending this". So we
510	    ask the certified engine BOTH directions and license ``extension`` ONLY on a PROPER one-way entailment:
511	
512	      * FORWARD  ``fn(clause_b, clause_a)`` must be ``True``  — clause_b entails clause_a (the SUPPORT
513	        direction the engine was built for);
514	      * REVERSE  ``fn(clause_a, clause_b)`` must be ``False`` — a CONFIDENT non-entailment, i.e. clause_a
515	        does NOT entail clause_b, proving clause_b carries strictly MORE than clause_a (a proper superset,
516	        not an equivalence).
517	
518	    Returns:
519	      * ``True``  only when FORWARD is ``True`` AND REVERSE is ``False`` (a proper directional extension);
520	      * ``None`` (the FAIL-CLOSED sentinel — ``license_relation`` treats anything but ``True`` as "no
521	        extension") when the feature is OFF, no engine is available, the FORWARD direction is not a
522	        positive entailment, OR the REVERSE direction is anything other than a confident ``False``
523	        (``True`` = equivalence, ``None`` = unavailable, or an engine fault). We never guess the relation
524	        and never call an LLM here; the equivalence guard fails CLOSED to neutral, never open to a wrong
525	        relation word between two verified facts.
526	
527	    L3 refactor: the two directional NLI passes are now computed by the SHARED ``_pair_entailment_verdicts``
528	    (so the extension and agreement signals ask the resident cross-encoder at most once per direction), and
529	    the extension decision is the pure ``_extension_from_verdicts``. The RESULT is byte-identical to the
530	    pre-L3 forward/reverse logic (FORWARD = ``b_entails_a`` must be ``True``; REVERSE = ``a_entails_b`` must
531	    be ``False``); only the internal wiring is shared."""
532	    if not cross_source_extension_enabled():
533	        return None
534	    a_entails_b, b_entails_a = _pair_entailment_verdicts(clause_a, clause_b, entail_fn)
535	    return _extension_from_verdicts(a_entails_b, b_entails_a)
536

538	def _bidirectional_agreement_signal(
539	    clause_a: str,
540	    clause_b: str,
541	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]],
542	) -> Optional[bool]:
543	    """Compute the CERTIFIED BIDIRECTIONAL-equivalence verdict that licenses ``agreement`` for the pair
544	    ``(clause_a, clause_b)`` — the L3 COVERAGE lever.
545	
546	    The rendered sentence is ``[clause_a]; consistent with this, [clause_b]``. For that to be faithful the
547	    two clauses must be the SAME claim corroborated — a BIDIRECTIONAL equivalence: clause_a entails clause_b
548	    AND clause_b entails clause_a (BOTH directions entail), the symmetric predicate the consolidation merge
549	    already uses. We ask the SAME certified engine (``consolidation_nli.entails_directional``) in BOTH
550	    directions and license ``agreement`` ONLY on both-True.

555	        bidirectional agreement") when the feature is OFF, no engine is available, EITHER direction is not a

567	    clause_b: str,

569	) -> tuple[Optional[bool], Optional[bool]]:

571	    pair from ONE shared pair of NLI forward passes (the hot-path entry the composer uses).
572	
573	    Each signal is INDEPENDENTLY flag-gated (``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_EXTENSION`` / ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_AGREEMENT``,
574	    both DEFAULT-ON) and FAIL-CLOSED to ``None``. When BOTH flags are off, NO engine call is made
575	    (``(None, None)``). Extension and agreement are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE by construction (extension needs
576	    ``a_entails_b`` False; agreement needs it True), and ``license_relation`` checks agreement ahead of
577	    extension as the backstop. Sharing the two verdicts keeps this FAST (§ operator FAST DNA): the resident
578	    cross-encoder is asked at most twice per eligible pair regardless of how many relation words we derive."""

584	    directional = _extension_from_verdicts(a_entails_b, b_entails_a) if ext_on else None
585	    bidirectional = _bidirectional_agreement_from_verdicts(a_entails_b, b_entails_a) if agr_on else None
586	    return (directional, bidirectional)

589	def _first_verified_clause(
590	    basket: Any,
591	    evidence_pool: dict,
592	    *,
593	    writer_fn: Callable[[Any, dict], str],
594	    verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
595	) -> Optional[str]:
596	    """ONE single-sentence verified clause for a basket, suitable for analytical co-location.
597	
598	    Reuses the UNCHANGED ``verified_compose._per_basket_verified_clause`` (the existing P1-2/P1-1
599	    per-basket contract: writer drafts -> ``strict_verify`` each sentence against THIS basket's scoped
600	    pool -> own-region gate -> verbatim K-span fallback). The returned clause is therefore an EXISTING
601	    strict_verify-PASSED (or faithful-by-construction verbatim-span) unit carrying its OWN ``[#ev]``
602	    token. We then reduce it to its FIRST sentence-unit so the analytical join yields ONE clean sentence
603	    carrying exactly one token per clause. Returns None when the basket yields no verified clause (the
604	    pair is then skipped; the basket's own single-source unit is surfaced by the per-basket path)."""
605	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
606	        _per_basket_verified_clause,
607	        split_into_sentences,
608	    )
609	    clause = _per_basket_verified_clause(
610	        basket, evidence_pool, writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
611	    )
612	    if not clause or not clause.strip():
613	        return None
614	    units = [u for u in (split_into_sentences(clause) or [clause]) if u and u.strip()]
615	    if not units:
616	        return None
617	    first = units[0].strip()
618	    # The clause MUST carry exactly one provenance token (a clean single-source atom for the join).
619	    if not _EV_TOKEN_RE.search(first):
620	        return None
621	    return first

624	# ── I-deepfix-001 Wave-2a (#1344): PLAN-DRIVEN pairing (replaces near-self-annulling anchor equality) ──
625	# LAW VI: DEFAULT-OFF (``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY``). OFF => the legacy ``_basket_anchor`` (subject|predicate)
626	# grouping runs UNCHANGED (byte-identical). ON => a pair of section baskets is a CANDIDATE when they are
627	# PLAN-related — same section facet (proxied by the same normalized subject, since baskets carry no facet
628	# field and consolidation already merged same-subject-same-predicate claims into ONE cluster, so the old
629	# subject|predicate anchor near-self-annuls), OR joined by a certified ContradictionEdge / refuter
630	# reference, OR bidirectionally/directionally agreeing per the threaded agree-map. The RELATION word is
631	# still decided ONLY by ``license_relation`` from the certified engines; candidacy just admits the pair to
632	# be analyzed. FAIL-CLOSED: an unlicensed pair renders the neutral connective, never a fabricated relation.

636	def cross_source_body_enabled() -> bool:
637	    """``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY`` gate (default OFF, LAW VI). OFF => anchor-equality pairing (byte-identical);
638	    ON => plan-driven candidate pairing (same-facet / contradiction / agreement). A WEIGHT/CONSOLIDATE
639	    lever, never a cap / target / thinner (§-1.3)."""

643	# ── I-deepfix-001 FIX 3 (#1344): thread the consolidation AGREEMENT MAP into the composer ─────────────
644	# LAW VI: DEFAULT-OFF (``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_THREAD_CONSOLIDATION``). Before FIX 3 the caller
645	# (``verified_compose._compose_section_per_basket``) threaded ONLY ``edges`` (the ContradictionEdge list)
646	# and never an ``agree_map`` / ``equiv_clusters``, so ``compose_cross_source_analytical_units`` logged
647	# ``input_threaded=False degraded=True`` and cross-basket CORROBORATION could never admit a plan-driven
648	# candidate (only same-facet / edge / refuter did). ON => the caller builds a per-section agree_map from
649	# the SAME certified bidirectional-NLI merge predicate the consolidation leg uses (both directions entail),

651	# ``agreement`` connective. It is a CONSOLIDATE lever (adds candidacy + a relation WORD), never a
652	# drop / cap / thinner (§-1.3), and it is FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: strict_verify is byte-untouched; each
653	# emitted connective is STILL independently re-gated per BUILT clause inside ``_process_pair`` and each

660	    agree_map (byte-identical: ``input_threaded=False`` as before). ON => the caller builds a per-section
661	    bidirectional-equivalence agree_map (``build_basket_agreement_map``) and threads it. A CONSOLIDATE
662	    lever, never a cap / target / thinner (§-1.3)."""

666	def _basket_claim_text(basket: Any) -> str:
667	    """The basket's representative claim text — the SAME cluster-representative text the consolidation leg
668	    compares. '' (never an agreement candidate) when absent, mirroring ``_basket_anchor``'s blank guard."""

672	def build_basket_agreement_map(
673	    baskets: list,
674	    *,
675	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]] = None,
676	) -> dict[str, set]:
677	    """Build the per-section consolidation AGREEMENT MAP threaded into the cross-source composer (FIX 3).
678	
679	    Two DISTINCT-cluster section baskets AGREE iff the certified engine confirms a BIDIRECTIONAL
680	    equivalence over their representative claim texts — clause A entails clause B AND clause B entails
681	    clause A (BOTH directions entail), the SAME symmetric merge predicate ``consolidation_nli`` uses. The
682	    result is a dict ``cluster_id -> set(cluster_id)`` in the shape ``_agree`` reads; an empty dict when no
683	    pair agrees (then ``input_threaded`` stays False — honest, never forced).
684	
685	    FAIL-CLOSED (Codex #1344 iter-1 P1): each pair's bidirectional NLI check is wrapped in try/except so a
686	    RAISED exception from ``entail_fn(ta, tb)`` OR ``entail_fn(tb, ta)`` NEVER propagates and aborts the
687	    composer — the pair simply contributes NO map entry (an infra fault licenses NOTHING, the §-1.3 +
688	    module "under-relax is safe; over-relax is lethal" contract). A one-way / absent / non-``True`` verdict
689	    likewise adds no entry. Never mutates the baskets. Reuses the RESIDENT cross-encoder the consolidation
690	    leg already loads (``_default_entail_fn``) — zero extra OpenRouter / GPU spend.
691	
692	    Faithfulness-neutral: the map only ADMITS candidacy + drives the ``input_threaded`` telemetry; the
693	    emitted ``agreement`` connective is STILL independently re-gated per BUILT clause inside
694	    ``_process_pair`` (``_bidirectional_agreement_signal``) and each atom re-passes the UNCHANGED
695	    strict_verify. It NEVER relaxes the frozen engine."""
696	    if not cross_source_thread_consolidation_enabled():
697	        return {}
698	    items: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
699	    for b in (baskets or []):
700	        cid = _cluster_id(b)
701	        text = _basket_claim_text(b)
702	        if cid and text:
703	            items.append((cid, text))
704	    if len(items) < 2:
705	        return {}
706	    fn = entail_fn if entail_fn is not None else _default_entail_fn()
707	    if fn is None:
708	        return {}
709	    agree_map: dict[str, set] = {}
710	    n = len(items)
711	    for i in range(n):
712	        cluster_a_id, text_a = items[i]
713	        for j in range(i + 1, n):
714	            cluster_b_id, text_b = items[j]
715	            if not cluster_a_id or not cluster_b_id or cluster_a_id == cluster_b_id:
716	                continue
717	            # FAIL-CLOSED: an ``entail_fn`` fault on EITHER direction contributes no map entry and never
718	            # aborts the composer (Codex #1344 iter-1 P1). The bidirectional-equivalence decision reuses
719	            # the shared pure ``_bidirectional_agreement_from_verdicts`` (both-True), so the "agreement"
720	            # licence here is byte-identical to the composer's own ``_bidirectional_agreement_signal``.
721	            try:
722	                a_entails_b = fn(text_a, text_b)
723	                b_entails_a = fn(text_b, text_a)
724	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — an NLI fault licenses NOTHING (fail-closed)
725	                logger.warning(
726	                    "[cross_source_synthesis] agreement-map bidirectional NLI raised (%s); pair "
727	                    "fails closed (no map entry)", exc,
728	                )
729	                continue
730	            if _bidirectional_agreement_from_verdicts(a_entails_b, b_entails_a) is True:
731	                agree_map.setdefault(cluster_a_id, set()).add(cluster_b_id)
732	                agree_map.setdefault(cluster_b_id, set()).add(cluster_a_id)
733	    return agree_map

742	    """Two baskets are on the SAME section facet iff they share a non-empty normalized subject. This is
743	    strictly BROADER than the legacy subject|predicate anchor (which self-annuls after consolidation) yet
744	    bounded to the same entity — never an arbitrary cross-subject juxtaposition (brief risk #3)."""
745	    sa = _basket_subject(a)

749	def _refuter_cross_ref(a: Any, b: Any, cluster_a_id: str, cluster_b_id: str) -> bool:
750	    """True iff either basket's durable ``refuter_cluster_ids`` references the other's cluster — the
751	    consolidation-layer conflict signal that complements ``_edge_between`` (both read only certified
752	    contradiction references, never a free-form guess). Conservative on missing data -> False."""
753	    def _refs(basket: Any) -> set:
754	        try:
755	            return {str(x) for x in (getattr(basket, "refuter_cluster_ids", None) or ())}
756	        except TypeError:
757	            return set()
758	    return cluster_b_id in _refs(a) or cluster_a_id in _refs(b)
