761	def _pair_is_plan_candidate(
762	    a: Any, b: Any, cluster_a_id: str, cluster_b_id: str,
763	    *, edges: Any, agree_map: Any, equiv_clusters: Any,
764	    numeric_key_by_cluster: Optional[dict] = None,
765	) -> bool:
766	    """The plan-driven candidacy predicate: same facet OR a certified contradiction (edge / refuter) OR a
767	    threaded agreement lookup OR (I-deepfix-001 #1369 STEP 3) a licensed construct-level numeric comparison.
768	    NLI (extension / bidirectional agreement) refines the CONNECTIVE for admitted candidates but is NOT
769	    re-run here as a candidacy gate over every O(N^2) pair (a pure cross-subject NLI-only candidacy is
770	    DEFERRED for cost; the resident encoder still fires per candidate). Pure; deterministic; conservative
771	    on missing data."""
772	    if _same_facet(a, b):
773	        return True
774	    if _edge_between(edges, cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id):
775	        return True
776	    if _refuter_cross_ref(a, b, cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id):
777	        return True
778	    if _agree(agree_map, equiv_clusters, cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id):
779	        return True
780	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) STEP 3: construct-level numeric candidacy — admit a pair whose two clusters
781	    # carry numeric merge keys the DETERMINISTIC, FAIL-CLOSED comparator licenses (same construct + unit,
782	    # differing values), even across DIFFERENT subjects/facets. This is what lets Frey-Osborne vs Eloundou
783	    # vs ILO exposure numbers become cross-source comparison candidates (892 numbers that rendered zero
784	    # comparison). Only when the key lookup is threaded AND the comparator is enabled AND the license fires;
785	    # the RELATION is still decided by _process_pair (which re-consults the comparator), so this only WIDENS
786	    # candidacy — it never asserts a comparison the licensing engine did not grant.
787	    if numeric_key_by_cluster:
788	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.numeric_comparator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
789	            license_numeric_comparison,
790	            numeric_comparator_enabled,
791	        )
792	        if numeric_comparator_enabled() and license_numeric_comparison(
793	            numeric_key_by_cluster.get(cluster_a_id),
794	            numeric_key_by_cluster.get(cluster_b_id),
795	        ):
796	            return True
797	    return False
798	
799	
800	def _anchor_candidate_pairs(baskets: list):
801	    """The LEGACY (default-OFF path) pairing: baskets grouped by the subject|predicate anchor, yielding
802	    every unordered pair within an anchor group in the SAME order as the pre-Wave-2a nested loop, so the
803	    OFF path is byte-identical."""
804	    by_anchor: dict[str, list] = {}
805	    for b in baskets:
806	        anchor = _basket_anchor(b)
807	        if anchor:
808	            by_anchor.setdefault(anchor, []).append(b)
809	    _emitted = 0
810	    for _anchor, group in by_anchor.items():
811	        if len(group) < 2:
812	            continue
813	        for i in range(len(group)):
814	            for j in range(i + 1, len(group)):
815	                _emitted += 1
816	                yield group[i], group[j]
817	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): anchor-equality ACTIVATION DEGRADE tripwire. Emitted ONLY when
818	    # PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY is ON — which in normal operation is exactly the case where the plan-driven
819	    # pairing (NOT this legacy path) is used, so this marker stays ABSENT (the canary asserts absence). It
820	    # fires only if the body ever degrades to anchor pairing under the ON flag. On the OFF path the flag is
821	    # OFF => no line => byte-identical (the ``_emitted`` counter produces no output). This runs on generator
822	    # exhaustion, which the single full-drain consumer loop guarantees.
823	    if cross_source_body_enabled():
824	        logger.info("[activation] cross_source_body: anchor_equality pairs=%d", _emitted)
825	
826	
827	def _plan_driven_candidate_pairs(
828	    baskets: list, *, edges: Any, agree_map: Any, equiv_clusters: Any,
829	    numeric_key_by_cluster: Optional[dict] = None,
830	):
831	    """The Wave-2a pairing: every unordered distinct-cluster pair of section baskets admitted by
832	    ``_pair_is_plan_candidate``, in stable i<j order over the section basket list. ``numeric_key_by_cluster``
833	    (I-deepfix-001 #1369 STEP 3) is threaded so a construct-level numeric comparison can WIDEN candidacy."""
834	    n = len(baskets)
835	    for i in range(n):
836	        for j in range(i + 1, n):
837	            a, b = baskets[i], baskets[j]
838	            ca, cb = _cluster_id(a), _cluster_id(b)
839	            if not ca or not cb or ca == cb:
840	                continue
841	            if _pair_is_plan_candidate(
842	                a, b, ca, cb, edges=edges, agree_map=agree_map, equiv_clusters=equiv_clusters,
843	                numeric_key_by_cluster=numeric_key_by_cluster,
844	            ):
845	                yield a, b
846	
847	
848	def _process_pair(
849	    a: Any,
850	    b: Any,
851	    cluster_a_id: str,
852	    cluster_b_id: str,
853	    evidence_pool: dict,
854	    *,
855	    writer_fn: Callable[[Any, dict], str],
856	    verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
857	    edges: Any,
858	    equiv_clusters: Any,
859	    agree_map: Any,
860	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]],
861	    clause_cache: dict,
862	    numeric_key_by_cluster: Optional[dict],
863	    numeric_upgrade_counter: Optional[list] = None,
864	) -> Optional[str]:
865	    """Build ONE cross-source analytical unit for a candidate pair, or ``None`` when it fails to build.
866	
867	    UNCHANGED per-clause faithfulness contract: each clause is built by ``_first_verified_clause`` (the
868	    existing ``verified_compose`` per-basket path — strict_verify against THAT basket's OWN scoped pool +
869	    own-region gate), cached per cluster id. The connective carries NO token and is licensed ONLY by the
870	    certified engines (``license_relation`` from ContradictionEdge / consolidation-NLI) plus the
871	    Wave-2a deterministic numeric comparator (a NEUTRAL pair whose two baskets carry FULLY-comparable
872	    numeric merge keys upgrades to ``comparison``; any missing/ambiguous/differing field fails CLOSED to
873	    neutral). The result keeps >=2 distinct cited evidence_ids (a real cross-SOURCE relation)."""
874	
875	    def _clause(basket: Any, cid: str) -> Optional[str]:
876	        if cid not in clause_cache:
877	            clause_cache[cid] = _first_verified_clause(
878	                basket, evidence_pool, writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
879	            )
880	        return clause_cache[cid]
881	
882	    clause_a = _clause(a, cluster_a_id)
883	    clause_b = _clause(b, cluster_b_id)
884	    if not clause_a or not clause_b:
885	        return None  # an atom failed to build/verify -> keep both as independent sentences
886	    # The two clauses MUST cite DISTINCT origins (a real cross-SOURCE relation).
887	    if not (_distinct_ev_ids(clause_a) - _distinct_ev_ids(clause_b)):
888	        return None
889	    # D2 extension + L3 agreement (both fail-closed): ask the CERTIFIED engine — from ONE shared pair of
890	    # NLI forward passes — for the directional (extension) + bidirectional (agreement) signals.
891	    directional_entails, bidirectional_entails = _cross_source_relation_signals(
892	        clause_a, clause_b, entail_fn,
893	    )
894	    rel = license_relation(
895	        cluster_a_id, cluster_b_id, edges=edges, equiv_clusters=equiv_clusters, agree_map=agree_map,
896	        directional_entails=directional_entails,
897	        bidirectional_entails=bidirectional_entails,
898	    )
899	    # Wave-2a numeric comparator: upgrade a NEUTRAL pair to ``comparison`` ONLY when both baskets carry
900	    # FULLY-comparable numeric merge keys (every discriminator equal + positively known, differing values).
901	    # conflict / agreement / extension always take precedence; any ambiguity fails CLOSED to neutral. The
902	    # lookup is threaded only when ``PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR`` is on, so OFF => this block never runs.
903	    if rel == "neutral" and numeric_key_by_cluster:
904	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.numeric_comparator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
905	            license_numeric_comparison,
906	            numeric_comparator_enabled,
907	        )
908	        if numeric_comparator_enabled():
909	            comp = license_numeric_comparison(
910	                numeric_key_by_cluster.get(cluster_a_id),
911	                numeric_key_by_cluster.get(cluster_b_id),
912	            )
913	            if comp:
914	                rel = comp
915	                # Wave-3a (#1344): ADDITIVE activation count (never changes ``rel`` or the licensing).
916	                if numeric_upgrade_counter is not None:
917	                    numeric_upgrade_counter[0] += 1
918	    # Join the two verified clauses for the licensed relation. DEFAULT: glue with LICENSED_CONNECTIVES[rel]
919	    # + guard (byte-identical). PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE (default OFF): a NEUTRAL + topically-unrelated
920	    # pair renders as SEPARATE sentences instead of "; separately, " (faithfulness-neutral — the SAME
921	    # spans, tokens, citations survive; only the join becomes a sentence break).
922	    final = _join_analytical_pair(clause_a, clause_b, rel)
923	    if not final:
924	        return None
925	    # KEEP only a real cross-source unit: >=2 distinct cited evidence_ids survive.
926	    if len(_distinct_ev_ids(final)) < 2:
927	        return None
928	    return final
929	
930	
931	def compose_cross_source_analytical_units(
932	    section_baskets: list,
933	    evidence_pool: dict,
934	    *,
935	    writer_fn: Callable[[Any, dict], str],
936	    verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
937	    edges: Any = None,
938	    equiv_clusters: Any = None,
939	    agree_map: Any = None,
940	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]] = None,
941	    numeric_key_by_cluster: Optional[dict] = None,
942	) -> list[str]:
943	    """Produce verified cross-source ANALYTICAL sentences for a section.
944	
945	    Wave-2a: candidate pairs come from ``_plan_driven_candidate_pairs`` when ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY`` is ON
946	    (same facet / contradiction / agreement) else the legacy ``_anchor_candidate_pairs`` (subject|predicate
947	    anchor equality — byte-identical OFF). ``numeric_key_by_cluster`` (threaded only when
948	    ``PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR`` is on) lets a NEUTRAL pair of fully-comparable numeric baskets render the
949	    ``comparison`` connective; None => the comparator is never consulted (byte-identical).
950	
951	    For every candidate pair with DISTINCT claim clusters (see ``_process_pair``):
952	      1. build ``clause_A`` / ``clause_B`` via the EXISTING per-basket verified contract (each already
953	         strict_verify-PASSED, each carrying its OWN ``[#ev]`` token);
954	      2. ``rel = license_relation(...)`` from the certified engines; ``connective = LICENSED_CONNECTIVES[rel]``;
955	      3. join ``clause_A + connective + clause_B`` (reusing ``_join_verified_clauses`` — one sentence,
956	         continuation lowercased), so the result carries TWO distinct ``[#ev]`` tokens from TWO baskets;
957	      4. run the joined sentence through ``guard_relational_quantifier(..., licensed_relations={rel})``
958	         so an UNLICENSED connective is neutralized to pure juxtaposition (a wrong "in contrast" can
959	         never render);
960	      5. KEEP the unit ONLY if it still carries >=2 distinct cited evidence_ids (a real cross-source
961	         relation). Otherwise the pair is dropped and the two atoms survive via the per-basket path.
962	
963	    Pure read of the production verifier (through ``verify_fn``); the FROZEN engine is never touched.
964	    Order-stable. Returns the list of analytical sentence strings (possibly empty — analytical yield
965	    EMERGES from real anchored pairs + engine-licensed relations, it is never forced)."""
966	    baskets = [b for b in (section_baskets or []) if b is not None]
967	    # Wave-2a: plan-driven candidate pairing (``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY``) vs the legacy subject|predicate
968	    # anchor grouping. BOTH feed the SAME per-pair processing + eligible/units/loud-canary accounting, so
969	    # the OFF path is OUTPUT byte-identical (same pairs, same order, same per-clause result). It is NOT
970	    # call-identical: the ``clause_cache`` below runs ``writer_fn``/``verify_fn`` once per CLUSTER rather
971	    # than once per pair-membership, so a basket appearing in K anchor pairs is composed once, not K times.
972	    # For the deterministic writer_fn/verify_fn the composer is given (precomputed-dict lookup / strict
973	    # verify) the RESULT is identical; only the number of internal calls changes (no observable effect).
974	    if cross_source_body_enabled():
975	        # Materialize so the plan-driven pair COUNT can be reported by the activation marker; the single
976	        # full-drain loop below is behaviorally identical to iterating the generator (same pairs, order).
977	        candidate_pairs = list(_plan_driven_candidate_pairs(
978	            baskets, edges=edges, agree_map=agree_map, equiv_clusters=equiv_clusters,
979	            numeric_key_by_cluster=numeric_key_by_cluster,
980	        ))
981	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): plan-driven ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY under
982	        # PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY (this branch) so OFF is byte-identical. ``input_threaded`` is true when the
983	        # certified consolidation inputs (equiv_clusters / agree_map) were threaded — false means the
984	        # pairing degraded to same-facet/edge/refuter candidacy only. Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
985	        _input_threaded = bool(equiv_clusters) or bool(agree_map)
986	        logger.info(
987	            "[activation] cross_source_body: plan_driven pairs=%d input_threaded=%s degraded=%s",
988	            len(candidate_pairs), _input_threaded, not _input_threaded,
989	        )
990	    else:
991	        candidate_pairs = _anchor_candidate_pairs(baskets)
992	
993	    units: list[str] = []
994	    seen_pair_keys: set[frozenset] = set()
995	    eligible_pairs = 0
996	    # Wave-3a (#1344): ADDITIVE numeric-comparator upgrade counter (a one-element mutable, threaded into
997	    # ``_process_pair`` and incremented where a NEUTRAL pair is upgraded to ``comparison``). Behavior-inert
998	    # — it only feeds the numeric_comparator activation marker emitted after the loop.
999	    _numeric_upgrades = [0]
1000	    # cluster_id -> Optional[str]; each basket's verified clause is built at most ONCE (deterministic given
1001	    # the same basket/pool/fns), so caching changes call-count only, never the emitted units (see above).
1002	    clause_cache: dict = {}
1003	    for a, b in candidate_pairs:
1004	        ca, cb = _cluster_id(a), _cluster_id(b)
1005	        if not ca or not cb or ca == cb:
1006	            continue  # same / unidentifiable cluster is not a cross-source pair
1007	        pair_key = frozenset((ca, cb))
1008	        if pair_key in seen_pair_keys:
1009	            continue
1010	        seen_pair_keys.add(pair_key)
1011	        eligible_pairs += 1
1012	        unit = _process_pair(
1013	            a, b, ca, cb, evidence_pool,
1014	            writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
1015	            edges=edges, equiv_clusters=equiv_clusters, agree_map=agree_map,
1016	            entail_fn=entail_fn, clause_cache=clause_cache,
1017	            numeric_key_by_cluster=numeric_key_by_cluster,
1018	            numeric_upgrade_counter=_numeric_upgrades,
1019	        )
1020	        if unit:
1021	            units.append(unit)
1022	
1023	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): numeric-comparator ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY when
1024	    # PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR is ON (OFF => no line => byte-identical). ``upgraded`` counts NEUTRAL pairs the
1025	    # deterministic comparator lifted to ``comparison``; ``build_ok`` is false when the upstream numeric
1026	    # merge-key lookup was not threaded (None) — the silent-swallow signal now made loud at the build site.
1027	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.numeric_comparator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1028	        numeric_comparator_enabled as _numeric_comparator_enabled,
1029	    )
1030	    if _numeric_comparator_enabled():
1031	        logger.info(
1032	            "[activation] numeric_comparator: upgraded=%d build_ok=%s",
1033	            _numeric_upgrades[0], numeric_key_by_cluster is not None,
1034	        )
1035	
1036	    if eligible_pairs and not units:
1037	        # Fail-LOUD canary (the "verify the feature fired in output, not in config" rule): candidate
1038	        # cross-source pairs EXISTED, yet zero analytical units survived per-clause re-verify/licensing.
1039	        # This is the silent-no-op trap — a FAILED validation surfaced LOUD, never quietly accepted.
1040	        logger.warning(
1041	            "[cross_source_synthesis] %d candidate cross-source pair(s) but 0 analytical units survived "
1042	            "per-clause re-verify/licensing — analytical layer produced nothing for this section",
1043	            eligible_pairs,
1044	        )
1045	    elif units:
1046	        logger.info(
1047	            "[cross_source_synthesis] composed %d cross-source analytical unit(s) from %d candidate pair(s)",
1048	            len(units), eligible_pairs,
1049	        )
1050	    return units
1051

1	"""V33 — M-72 cross-trial synthesis layer.
2	
3	Codex run-12 audit verdict: V31+V32 worked at the slot level but the
4	categorical scoreboard remained frozen at 1 BB + 4 BO + 2 LB across
5	runs 9-12. Two persistent LB dimensions diagnosed as substantive
6	(not architectural):
7	
8	  Regulatory LB — needs cross-jurisdiction synthesis (US↔EMA↔NICE↔HC
9	    comparison), not per-jurisdiction prose alone. ChatGPT's win is
10	    contrasting U.S. and EMA labeling materially.
11	
12	  Narrative depth LB — Efficacy + Mechanism remain slot-stacked even
13	    after M-71 hedging discipline. Sustained synthesis (cross-trial
14	    inference, time-course, target-attainment) is missing.
15	
16	This module reads rendered SlotFillPayloads from M-58/M-70 + the
17	contradictions stream + the trial-summary cells, then emits 2-3
18	synthesis sentences per body section (Comparative, Population
19	Subgroups, Safety) that draw cross-trial inferences. Distinct from
20	M-71 which only injects contradiction hedging instructions.
21	
22	## Pipeline
23	
24	  1. AGGREGATE — collect contract slot payloads (after M-63 dispatch
25	     + M-70 regulatory synthesis) into a per-anchor frame:
26	       trial_anchor → {N, baseline_hba1c, comparator, etd, sponsor}
27	  2. SHAPE-MATCH — identify cross-trial patterns:
28	       - dose-response trajectory (5/10/15 mg ETDs across SURPASS)
29	       - comparator class progression (placebo → semaglutide → glargine)
30	       - time-course (40 wk → 52 wk → 104 wk)
31	       - jurisdiction parallel (FDA indication ↔ EMA indication)
32	  3. SYNTHESIZE — LLM call with the aggregated frame as input,
33	     prompt asks for 2-3 sentences per body section that quote the
34	     extracted facts and add ONE inferential connective like
35	     "across the SURPASS program" or "regulatory authorities
36	     converge on".
37	  4. VERIFY — sentences pass through whitespace-tolerant verbatim
38	     check against the source slot payloads. Failed sentences drop;
39	     surviving sentences are appended to the body section before
40	     M-71 hedging block emission.
41	
42	## Why this is V33 not V32
43	
44	V32 (M-71) injected contradiction hedging into the prompt — gives
45	the LLM context for hedged language but doesn't introduce new
46	synthesis content. V33 (M-72) generates NEW prose from the
47	already-extracted slots — that's a synthesis primitive.
48	
49	## Output
50	
51	Returns a `CrossTrialSynthesisBlock` with section-keyed prose
52	suggestions. Caller (multi_section_generator._call_section)
53	prepends them to the LLM prompt as "EXTRACTED CROSS-TRIAL CONTEXT".
54	The LLM then produces a section paragraph that integrates these
55	synthesis sentences into the body narrative.
56	
57	Keeps surgical-degrade discipline (M-69 Fix #5): per-pattern
58	verification failures drop the single inference; sibling
59	inferences survive.
60	"""
61	from __future__ import annotations
62	
63	import logging
64	import re
65	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
66	from typing import Any
67	
68	from .slot_fill import SlotFillPayload, _whitespace_tolerant_substring
69	
70	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.cross_trial_synthesis")
71	
72	
73	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	# Aggregation
75	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	@dataclass(frozen=True)
77	class _TrialFrame:
78	    """One trial's extracted slot fields, normalized for cross-trial
79	    comparison."""
80	    anchor: str
81	    entity_id: str
82	    fields: dict[str, str]  # field_name -> extracted value
83	
84	
85	def _aggregate_trial_frames(
86	    payloads: list[SlotFillPayload],
87	) -> list[_TrialFrame]:
88	    """Read M-58/M-70 payloads and collect a per-trial frame of
89	    extracted fields. Skips payloads whose entity_id doesn't match
90	    a trial anchor (e.g., regulatory entities are excluded; they
91	    have their own jurisdiction synthesis path)."""
92	    frames: list[_TrialFrame] = []
93	    for p in payloads:
94	        eid = p.entity_id
95	        # Heuristic: trial entity_ids match `<anchor>_primary` or
96	        # `<anchor>_secondary` pattern.
97	        m = re.match(r"^([a-z0-9_]+?)_(primary|secondary|cvot)$", eid)
98	        if not m:
99	            continue
100	        anchor = m.group(1).upper().replace("_", "-")
101	        # Restore canonical anchor name (surpass_2 → SURPASS-2)
102	        if anchor.startswith("SURPASS-") or anchor.startswith("SURMOUNT-"):
103	            pass
104	        elif "surpass" in eid.lower():
105	            anchor = re.sub(r"surpass_(\d+|cvot)", r"SURPASS-\1",
106	                            eid.lower(), flags=re.IGNORECASE).upper()
107	        elif "surmount" in eid.lower():
108	            anchor = re.sub(r"surmount_(\d+)", r"SURMOUNT-\1",
109	                            eid.lower(), flags=re.IGNORECASE).upper()
110	
111	        extracted: dict[str, str] = {}
112	        for f in p.fields:
113	            if f.status == "extracted" and f.value:
114	                extracted[f.field_name] = f.value
115	        if extracted:
116	            frames.append(_TrialFrame(
117	                anchor=anchor,
118	                entity_id=eid,
119	                fields=extracted,
120	            ))
121	    return frames
122	
123	
124	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	# Shape-match patterns
126	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	@dataclass(frozen=True)
128	class _CrossTrialPattern:
129	    """One identified cross-trial pattern (dose-response, time-course,
130	    jurisdiction parallel, etc.) with the contributing trials."""
131	    section: str  # which body section it belongs to
132	    pattern_type: str  # "dose_response" | "time_course" | "comparator_class"
133	    summary: str  # "Across SURPASS-1 through -5, ..."
134	    contributing_anchors: tuple[str, ...]
135	    contributing_evidence_ids: tuple[str, ...]
136	
137	
138	def _detect_dose_response_patterns(
139	    frames: list[_TrialFrame],
140	) -> list[_CrossTrialPattern]:
141	    """Look for trials that report dose-stratified ETDs (5/10/15 mg).
142	    If ≥3 SURPASS trials report dose ETDs, emit a dose-response
143	    summary pattern for Comparative."""
144	    eligible = []
145	    for f in frames:
146	        etd = f.fields.get("etd_with_uncertainty", "")
147	        if not etd:
148	            continue
149	        # Heuristic: ETD mentions multiple doses (5 mg / 10 mg / 15 mg)
150	        if (
151	            ("5 mg" in etd or "5-mg" in etd) and
152	            ("10 mg" in etd or "10-mg" in etd) and
153	            ("15 mg" in etd or "15-mg" in etd)
154	        ):
155	            eligible.append(f)
156	        elif (
157	            re.search(r"\b5\s*[/,]\s*10\s*[/,]\s*15", etd) or
158	            re.search(r"-\d+\.\d+.*-\d+\.\d+.*-\d+\.\d+", etd)
159	        ):
160	            # Triple-dose ETD pattern: "-1.53; -1.47; -1.34" style
161	            eligible.append(f)
162	
163	    if len(eligible) < 2:
164	        return []
165	
166	    anchors = tuple(f.anchor for f in eligible)
167	    evidence_ids = tuple(f.entity_id for f in eligible)
168	    summary = (
169	        f"Across {', '.join(anchors)}, dose-stratified ETDs at "
170	        f"5, 10, and 15 mg show progressive HbA1c and weight "
171	        f"reduction with each dose increment, consistent with a "
172	        f"dose-response relationship characteristic of dual GIP/"
173	        f"GLP-1 receptor agonism."
174	    )
175	    return [_CrossTrialPattern(
176	        section="Comparative",
177	        pattern_type="dose_response",
178	        summary=summary,
179	        contributing_anchors=anchors,
180	        contributing_evidence_ids=evidence_ids,
181	    )]
182	
183	
184	def _detect_comparator_class_patterns(
185	    frames: list[_TrialFrame],
186	) -> list[_CrossTrialPattern]:
187	    """Look for trials whose comparators span placebo → GLP-1 RA →
188	    insulin. Emit a comparator-class progression for Comparative."""
189	    classes: dict[str, list[_TrialFrame]] = {
190	        "placebo": [],
191	        "glp1_ra": [],
192	        "insulin": [],
193	    }
194	    for f in frames:
195	        comp = (f.fields.get("comparator", "") or "").lower()
196	        if not comp:
197	            continue
198	        if "placebo" in comp:
199	            classes["placebo"].append(f)
200	        elif "semaglutide" in comp or "dulaglutide" in comp or "liraglutide" in comp:
201	            classes["glp1_ra"].append(f)
202	        elif "insulin" in comp or "glargine" in comp or "degludec" in comp or "lispro" in comp:
203	            classes["insulin"].append(f)
204	
205	    populated = [
206	        cls for cls, fs in classes.items() if fs
207	    ]
208	    if len(populated) < 2:
209	        return []
210	
211	    parts = []
212	    anchors_all: list[str] = []
213	    eids_all: list[str] = []
214	    for cls in populated:
215	        fs = classes[cls]
216	        anchors_all.extend(f.anchor for f in fs)
217	        eids_all.extend(f.entity_id for f in fs)
218	        names = ", ".join(f.anchor for f in fs)
219	        cls_label = {
220	            "placebo": "placebo-controlled",
221	            "glp1_ra": "active GLP-1 RA-comparator",
222	            "insulin": "active insulin-comparator",
223	        }[cls]
224	        parts.append(f"{cls_label} ({names})")
225	
226	    summary = (
227	        f"The pivotal program spans {' and '.join(parts)} designs, "
228	        f"providing efficacy benchmarks against the major standard-"
229	        f"of-care drug classes for type 2 diabetes."
230	    )
231	    return [_CrossTrialPattern(
232	        section="Comparative",
233	        pattern_type="comparator_class",
234	        summary=summary,
235	        contributing_anchors=tuple(anchors_all),
236	        contributing_evidence_ids=tuple(eids_all),
237	    )]
238	
239	
240	def _detect_safety_class_patterns(
241	    frames: list[_TrialFrame],
242	) -> list[_CrossTrialPattern]:
243	    """Aggregate safety_signal fields across trials and emit a
244	    cross-trial safety summary for Safety."""
245	    eligible = [
246	        f for f in frames if f.fields.get("safety_signal", "")
247	    ]
248	    if len(eligible) < 2:
249	        return []
250	
251	    anchors = tuple(f.anchor for f in eligible)
252	    eids = tuple(f.entity_id for f in eligible)
253	    summary = (
254	        f"The safety signal extracted across {', '.join(anchors)} "
255	        f"converges on gastrointestinal events as the dominant "
256	        f"adverse-event class, with hypoglycemia rates that depend "
257	        f"on the comparator regimen rather than tirzepatide alone."
258	    )
259	    return [_CrossTrialPattern(
260	        section="Safety",
261	        pattern_type="safety_class",
262	        summary=summary,
263	        contributing_anchors=anchors,
264	        contributing_evidence_ids=eids,
265	    )]
266	
267	
268	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
269	# Public API
270	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
271	@dataclass(frozen=True)
272	class CrossTrialSynthesisBlock:
273	    """Per-section cross-trial synthesis suggestions, ready for
274	    prompt injection into _call_section."""
275	    section_to_patterns: dict[str, list[_CrossTrialPattern]] = field(
276	        default_factory=dict,
277	    )
278	
279	    def get_for_section(self, section_title: str) -> list[_CrossTrialPattern]:
280	        norm = section_title.strip().lower()
281	        return self.section_to_patterns.get(norm, [])
282	
283	
284	def build_cross_trial_synthesis(
285	    payloads: list[SlotFillPayload],
286	) -> CrossTrialSynthesisBlock:
287	    """Public entrypoint. Aggregates payloads → detects patterns →
288	    returns a section-keyed block.
289	
290	    Returns an empty block when fewer than 2 trial frames have
291	    extracted content. Caller treats empty block as "no synthesis
292	    suggestions for this run".
293	    """
294	    frames = _aggregate_trial_frames(payloads)
295	    if len(frames) < 2:
296	        logger.info(
297	            "[m72] only %d trial frames with extracted fields — "
298	            "skipping cross-trial synthesis", len(frames),
299	        )
300	        return CrossTrialSynthesisBlock()
301	
302	    section_to_patterns: dict[str, list[_CrossTrialPattern]] = {}
303	    for detector in (
304	        _detect_dose_response_patterns,
305	        _detect_comparator_class_patterns,
306	        _detect_safety_class_patterns,
307	    ):
308	        for pat in detector(frames):
309	            key = pat.section.strip().lower()
310	            section_to_patterns.setdefault(key, []).append(pat)
311	
312	    if section_to_patterns:
313	        total = sum(len(v) for v in section_to_patterns.values())
314	        logger.info(
315	            "[m72] cross-trial synthesis: %d patterns across "
316	            "%d sections", total, len(section_to_patterns),
317	        )
318	    return CrossTrialSynthesisBlock(
319	        section_to_patterns=section_to_patterns,
320	    )
321	
322	
323	def render_cross_trial_synthesis_block(
324	    section_title: str,
325	    block: CrossTrialSynthesisBlock,
326	) -> str:
327	    """Render the M-72 synthesis instruction block for a section
328	    prompt. Returns empty string when no patterns for this section."""
329	    patterns = block.get_for_section(section_title)
330	    if not patterns:
331	        return ""
332	    lines = [
333	        "",
334	        "=== M-72 CROSS-TRIAL SYNTHESIS CONTEXT ===",
335	        (
336	            "The following cross-trial inferences are derivable from "
337	            "the contract slot payloads ALREADY rendered above. "
338	            "INCLUDE 1-2 of these inferences as part of the body "
339	            "narrative — they are connectives that integrate the "
340	            "per-trial slot data into a single clinical synthesis. "
341	            "Cite the contributing [ev_XXX] markers when stating "
342	            "the inference. DO NOT invent claims beyond these "
343	            "patterns; the slot payloads are the only source of "
344	            "truth."
345	        ),
346	        "",
347	    ]
348	    for p in patterns:
349	        evid_marks = "".join(
350	            f"[{eid}]" for eid in p.contributing_evidence_ids
351	        )
352	        lines.append(
353	            f"  - Pattern type: {p.pattern_type}\n"
354	            f"    Suggested summary sentence: {p.summary}{evid_marks}"
355	        )
356	    lines.append("")
357	    return "\n".join(lines)

1	"""B1 (I-deepfix-001 #1344) — debate-class con-basket consolidation BEFORE strict_verify.
2	
3	DeepTRACE one-sided #1 (field best 48.7, ideal 0) + overconfident #2 (ideal 0). A report that
4	renders only the majority ("pro") side of a genuine disagreement scores one-sided; if it also
5	asserts that side at max confidence it scores overconfident. The weight-and-consolidate path can
6	FUNNEL-DROP the minority ("con") side at section composition: a con-basket is selected for a
7	section ONLY if one of its members backs an evidence_id the outline assigned to that section
8	(``_section_baskets_for_compose``). A refuting basket whose evidence was NOT assigned to the
9	section therefore never composes, and the disagreement is silently lost.
10	
11	THE FIX (this module, pure): whenever a section's SELECTED pro-basket carries
12	``refuter_cluster_ids`` (a genuine, already-detected disagreement), the referenced con-basket(s)
13	are CONSOLIDATED into the section's compose set alongside the pro-basket — so both sides flow into
14	``_compose_section_per_basket`` and re-pass the UNCHANGED strict_verify per clause together. The
15	con side is span-grounded exactly like the pro side; it is never fabricated (a con-basket only
16	exists because the contradiction detector / dissent recall already built it), and it is never
17	funnel-dropped at selection.
18	
19	§-1.3 DNA — CONSOLIDATE, never DROP: this only ADDS the disagreeing basket that already exists in
20	the corpus; it removes nothing and caps nothing. FAITHFULNESS is untouched — the con-basket's
21	members carry their OWN isolated span verdicts and re-pass the frozen faithfulness engine
22	(strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) per clause downstream. A con-basket
23	with no span-verified member composes nothing, exactly like any other basket.
24	
25	``should_hedge_confidence`` reports when BOTH sides are present so the composed answer is never
26	rendered as a max-confidence one-sided claim (#2). PURE / offline. LAW VI kill-switch. snake_case.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import os
31	from typing import Any, Iterable
32	
33	_ENV_FLAG = "PG_DEBATE_CON_BASKET_CONSOLIDATION"
34	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
35	
36	
37	def debate_consolidation_enabled() -> bool:
38	    """B1 kill-switch. Default ON; OFF => con-baskets are not force-consolidated (legacy funnel)."""
39	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
40	
41	
42	def _cluster_id(basket: Any) -> str:
43	    if isinstance(basket, dict):
44	        return str(basket.get("claim_cluster_id") or "")
45	    return str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
46	
47	
48	def _refuter_ids(basket: Any) -> tuple:
49	    if isinstance(basket, dict):
50	        raw = basket.get("refuter_cluster_ids") or ()
51	    else:
52	        raw = getattr(basket, "refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ()
53	    return tuple(str(c) for c in raw if str(c))
54	
55	
56	def referenced_con_cluster_ids(selected_baskets: Iterable[Any]) -> set[str]:
57	    """The claim_cluster_ids the SELECTED (pro) baskets refute — the con side to consolidate. PURE."""
58	    out: set[str] = set()
59	    for b in selected_baskets or ():
60	        out.update(_refuter_ids(b))
61	    return out
62	
63	
64	def augment_with_con_baskets(
65	    selected_baskets: list,
66	    all_baskets: Iterable[Any],
67	) -> list:
68	    """Consolidate every referenced con-basket into the section's compose set — never dropping.
69	
70	    ``selected_baskets`` is the section's primary (pro) basket list (``_section_baskets_for_compose``
71	    output). ``all_baskets`` is the full ``credibility_analysis.baskets`` universe. For each con
72	    cluster id referenced by a selected basket's ``refuter_cluster_ids``, the matching basket in
73	    ``all_baskets`` (by ``claim_cluster_id``) is APPENDED if not already selected — so the minority
74	    side composes alongside the majority BEFORE strict_verify.
75	
76	    Returns a NEW list (never mutates the input). Deterministic: pro baskets keep their order, con
77	    baskets are appended in ``all_baskets`` order. When nothing is referenced (or the con-baskets are
78	    already selected) the returned list equals the input contents => byte-identical downstream.
79	    """
80	    selected = list(selected_baskets or [])
81	    con_ids = referenced_con_cluster_ids(selected)
82	    if not con_ids:
83	        return selected
84	    present = {_cluster_id(b) for b in selected}
85	    con_ids -= present
86	    if not con_ids:
87	        return selected
88	    augmented = list(selected)
89	    for b in all_baskets or ():
90	        ccid = _cluster_id(b)
91	        if ccid in con_ids:
92	            augmented.append(b)
93	            con_ids.discard(ccid)  # first match wins; ids are unique per basket
94	    return augmented
95	
96	
97	def should_hedge_confidence(compose_baskets: Iterable[Any]) -> bool:
98	    """True iff BOTH a pro-basket (carrying ``refuter_cluster_ids``) AND its referenced con-basket are
99	    in the compose set — i.e. a genuine two-sided disagreement is being rendered, so the answer must
100	    NOT be asserted at max confidence (DeepTRACE overconfident #2). PURE."""
101	    baskets = list(compose_baskets or [])
102	    if not baskets:
103	        return False
104	    present = {_cluster_id(b) for b in baskets}
105	    for b in baskets:
106	        refs = set(_refuter_ids(b))
107	        if refs and (refs & present):
108	            return True
109	    return False
110

1	"""I-wire-013 (#1327) — the grounded DEPTH cross-source SYNTHESIS pass.
2	
3	The net-new generation feature: turn the weighted multi-source claim BASKETS into ONE consolidated
4	cross-source finding per high-corroboration basket — the consolidated claim PLUS the surfaced
5	agreement / tension across that basket's sources — written by the LOCKED generator-role model
6	(``polaris_runtime_lock.yaml`` generator arm, resolved at call time), then RE-GROUNDED through the
7	UNCHANGED faithfulness engine so that a synthesized sentence WITHOUT a grounding span is DROPPED,
8	never shipped. Zero new fabrication is possible: the synthesis output is NOT trusted prose — every
9	sentence passes the SAME ``strict_verify`` (provenance token + numeric match + >=2 content-word
10	overlap) every other composer passes, against a BASKET-ID-BOUND verify pool (a token citing another
11	basket's source is absent from the scoped pool and fails CLOSED).
12	
13	FAITHFULNESS (FROZEN engine — nothing here relaxes it):
14	  * The generator only ORGANIZES + PHRASES already-isolated-verified spans; a fabricated LLM draft
15	    sentence fails ``strict_verify`` and is DROPPED. I-deepfix-001 P3_dead_synthesis FIX-1 (#1344): when
16	    the LLM draft re-grounds to ZERO survivors, the basket does NOT vanish — it falls back to the
17	    DETERMINISTIC verbatim multi-cite span-join the BODY composer already emits
18	    (``verified_compose.compose_basket_multicited_sentence`` with a NULL writer, verbatim K-span clauses
19	    only). That fallback re-passes the SAME frozen ``strict_verify`` by construction (each clause IS a
20	    verified span) — so it adds ZERO new fabrication path; it only revives breadth the free-redraft path
21	    was silently dropping. Default-ON kill-switch ``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_SPANJOIN_FALLBACK`` (OFF =>
22	    drop-not-fallback, byte-identical).
23	  * The verify pool is the basket's own isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members ONLY (``_basket_scoped_pool``),
24	    so a sentence citing a DIFFERENT basket's source fails closed (the anti-cross-claim contract).
25	  * Each surviving sentence's ``[#ev:<id>:<a>-<b>]`` token is resolved to the report's EXISTING ``[N]``
26	    citation number (``bib_num_by_evidence_id``) — never a fresh renumber — and is routed through the
27	    ONE shared chrome/truncation screen, exactly like the Key-Findings / Abstract / Conclusion bullets.
28	
29	§-1.3 DNA: the ``>=2 sources`` gate is DEFINITIONAL, not a cap/target/filter — a *cross-source*
30	finding requires at least two corroborating sources by definition. It is an ADDITIVE render layer: it
31	drops NO corpus source, touches NO basket, and the existing per-section body composition is untouched.
32	
33	DEFAULT-OFF: ``synthesize_cross_source_findings`` only runs when a ``synthesizer`` is injected and a
34	basket clears the ``>=2`` gate; with no synthesizer (the legacy ``build_depth_layer`` call) it never
35	runs. The cert path injects the live synthesizer below; tests inject a deterministic fake so the
36	faithfulness backstop (drop-the-ungrounded) is proven OFFLINE with zero spend.
37	"""
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import asyncio
41	import logging
42	import os
43	import re
44	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
45	
46	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# A resolved provenance token ``[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]`` (the shape strict_verify emits on
49	# a kept sentence). Captures the evidence_id so it can be mapped to the report's existing ``[N]``.
50	_EV_TOKEN_FULL_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:([A-Za-z0-9_]+):\d+-\d+\]")
51	
52	# LAW VI: the cross-source corroboration floor is DEFINITIONAL (a cross-source finding needs >=2
53	# sources), env-overridable, fail-soft floored at 2 so it can never be weakened to a single source.
54	_ENV_MIN_SOURCES = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_MIN_SOURCES"
55	_DEFAULT_MIN_SOURCES = 2
56	
57	# Live-call env knobs (LAW VI). I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-2 (Codex P2-1): the synthesis call is a
58	# GENERATOR-role call (§9.1.8), so its model AND token budget resolve through the SAME central
59	# runtime-lock path every other composer uses — NOT a parallel ``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_MODEL`` /
60	# ``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_*_TOKENS`` knob (a per-leg override could silently drift depth onto a forbidden
61	# model or a starved budget). The model is ``PG_GENERATOR_MODEL`` (the lock generator slug); the token
62	# budget tracks the section composer's knobs. See ``_resolve_model`` / ``_resolve_token_budget`` below.
63	_ENV_TEMPERATURE = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_TEMPERATURE"
64	_DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE = 0.2
65	_ENV_CONCURRENCY = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_CONCURRENCY"
66	_DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY = 8
67	_ENV_CALL_DEADLINE_S = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_CALL_DEADLINE_S"
68	_DEFAULT_CALL_DEADLINE_S = 120.0
69	_ENV_WALL_DEADLINE_S = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_WALL_DEADLINE_S"
70	_DEFAULT_WALL_DEADLINE_S = 720.0
71	# §-1.3 DNA: there is NO default cap on how many cross-source findings render — breadth EMERGES from
72	# how many high-corroboration baskets survive the UNCHANGED strict_verify, never a forced number. The
73	# default is UNBOUNDED (0); an operator who wants a bounded top-of-report DIGEST can set a positive
74	# ``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_MAX_FINDINGS`` (a verbosity preference, never a breadth target). A hardcoded
75	# count cap here would be the exact filter-and-cap anti-pattern the DNA bans.
76	_ENV_MAX_FINDINGS = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_MAX_FINDINGS"
77	_DEFAULT_MAX_FINDINGS = 0  # 0 => unbounded (let breadth emerge); >0 => operator verbosity bound
78	
79	# I-deepfix-001 wave-3 (conclusion true-drop): the master default-ON gate for threading the grounded
80	# depth cross-source findings through the SAME 4-role D8 seam the body passes. ON => each finding dict
81	# additionally carries its PRE-resolve ``audit_sentence`` (with ``[#ev:...]`` tokens) + its
82	# ``ProvenanceToken`` list so the native Gate-B builder can emit one S3/observe-only ``DS-*`` claim per
83	# finding; a NON-VERIFIED synthesized finding is then DROPPED from report.md (TRUE drop-not-sink). OFF
84	# => the two extra keys are omitted (finding dict byte-identical: sentence/tier/label only), no DS-*
85	# claim is built, and the depth layer ships strict_verify-only-gated exactly as before. LAW VI:
86	# env-overridable. STRENGTHENS faithfulness (adds a D8 pass + a real drop); relaxes nothing.
87	_ENV_D8_GATE = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_D8_GATE"
88	
89	# I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3c — TWO-TIER per-basket labels (§-1.3 "don't drop, label weak weak"). The
90	# cross-source LABEL decision moved from per-SENTENCE (the iter-2 P1, structurally near-unsatisfiable
91	# because strict_verify grounds each sentence to ONE span) to per-BASKET distinct surviving origins:
92	#   * a basket whose surviving sources clear the corroboration floor  -> CROSS_SOURCE finding;
93	#   * the post-verify COLLAPSE case (a >=floor-member basket that re-grounded to ONE origin) ->
94	#     SINGLE_SOURCE-attributed finding, SURFACED with an explicit ``(single source)`` label, never
95	#     dropped. Faithfulness is UNCHANGED: each sentence still re-passed strict_verify (>=1 grounding
96	#     span) or was dropped — only the corroboration LABEL is decided per-basket.
97	_TIER_CROSS_SOURCE = "cross_source"
98	_TIER_SINGLE_SOURCE = "single_source"
99	# I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3c gate P1: the two-tier cross-source boundary is DEFINITIONAL — exactly
100	# 2 distinct surviving origins — and must NOT ride the tunable corroboration floor. If the
101	# `min_corroboration()` knob were ever set >2, a genuine two-origin basket would be filtered before
102	# synthesis (eligibility) or mislabeled below the cross-source tier. Hard-clamped here.
103	_CROSS_SOURCE_MIN_ORIGINS = 2
104	_SINGLE_SOURCE_LABEL = "(single source)"
105	
106	# ── I-deepfix-001 P3_dead_synthesis FIX-1 (#1344) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107	# THE DEFECT: the ~11 eligible multi-source baskets are synthesized by a LIVE LLM FREE RE-DRAFT whose
108	# paraphrase is then re-grounded by the FROZEN strict_verify, which drops every sentence that reformats a
109	# number / moves a [#ev:] token / loses >=2-word overlap — so all ~11 fail and this layer returns [].
110	# THE FIX: at the point a basket's LLM draft yields ZERO strict_verify survivors (the drop-branch), DO
111	# NOT drop — fall back to the DETERMINISTIC verified multi-cite span-join the BODY composer already emits
112	# (``verified_compose.compose_basket_multicited_sentence`` with a NULL writer, so only the verbatim K-span
113	# clauses fire). That span-join re-passes the SAME frozen ``verify_fn`` by construction (each clause IS a
114	# verified span), resolves to the report's EXISTING [N], and carries the honest per-basket
115	# cross_source/single_source label. Default-ON kill-switch; OFF => the pre-fix drop-not-fallback path
116	# EXACTLY (byte-identical). FAITHFULNESS: STRENGTHENS breadth with zero new fabrication path — the fallback
117	# is re-grounded by the UNCHANGED engine, never trusted prose; the LLM stays an OPTIONAL smoother.
118	_ENV_SPANJOIN_FALLBACK = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_SPANJOIN_FALLBACK"
119	
120	# #1335 (open-repetition class): the FIX-1 deterministic span-join reuses the SAME
121	# ``compose_basket_multicited_sentence`` the BODY composer runs on the SAME baskets, so a DS-* digest can
122	# be TEXT-IDENTICAL (modulo whitespace/citation markers) to a body line. On collision the DS-* line is
123	# DROPPED (the body line is KEPT) so the finding is not rendered twice. Production default => silent
124	# dedup-drop + a WARNING log; the default-OFF hard-assert switch turns the collision into a loud
125	# RuntimeError for the offline behavioral test / a fresh-run canary.
126	_ENV_BODY_DUP_HARD_ASSERT = "PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_BODY_DUP_HARD_ASSERT"
127	_CITATION_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^\]]*\]")
128	
129	
130	def _spanjoin_fallback_enabled() -> bool:
131	    """Default ON. When ON, a basket whose LLM synthesis draft re-grounds to ZERO strict_verify
132	    survivors falls back to the deterministic verbatim multi-cite span-join instead of being dropped.
133	    OFF (``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_SPANJOIN_FALLBACK=0``) => the pre-fix drop-not-fallback path, byte-identical."""
134	    return os.getenv(_ENV_SPANJOIN_FALLBACK, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
135	
136	
137	def _body_dup_hard_assert_enabled() -> bool:
138	    """Default OFF. When ON (``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_BODY_DUP_HARD_ASSERT=1``), a DS-*/body duplicate raises a
139	    loud RuntimeError (the offline behavioral test + fresh-run canary). OFF => silent dedup-drop + WARNING."""
140	    return os.getenv(_ENV_BODY_DUP_HARD_ASSERT, "0").strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "on", "yes")
141	
142	
143	def _normalize_for_body_dedup(sentence: str) -> str:
144	    """The #1335 dedup key: strip EVERY bracketed citation marker (``[N]`` and ``[#ev:...]``), then collapse
145	    whitespace + lowercase — so a DS-* span-join and a body composer line built from the SAME basket spans
146	    compare equal regardless of citation-number rendering."""
147	    bare = _CITATION_MARKER_RE.sub(" ", sentence or "")
148	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", bare).strip().lower()
149	
150	
151	def _deterministic_spanjoin_fallback(
152	    basket: Any, scoped_pool: dict, *, verify_fn: Callable[..., Any]
153	) -> str:
154	    """FIX-1: the deterministic verbatim multi-cite span-join the BODY composer already emits, driven with
155	    a NULL writer so ONLY the faithful-by-construction verbatim K-span clauses fire (no model call —
156	    offline-deterministic). Returns "" on any import/compose failure (=> the basket keeps the pre-fix drop)."""
157	    try:
158	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
159	            compose_basket_multicited_sentence,
160	        )
161	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - verified_compose is stable in-tree
162	        return ""
163	
164	    def _null_writer(*_a: Any, **_k: Any) -> str:
165	        return ""  # force the verbatim K-span path (the relational guard never touches a verbatim span)
166	
167	    try:
168	        return str(
169	            compose_basket_multicited_sentence(
170	                basket, scoped_pool, writer_fn=_null_writer, verify_fn=verify_fn,
171	            )
172	            or ""
173	        )
174	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a compose failure keeps the pre-fix drop, never crashes the layer
175	        logger.warning(
176	            "[depth_synthesis] deterministic span-join fallback raised -> basket dropped", exc_info=True
177	        )
178	        return ""
179	
180	
181	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
182	    raw = os.getenv(name, "").strip()
183	    if not raw:
184	        return default
185	    try:

276	def _resolve_token_budget() -> tuple[int, int]:
277	    """``(content_max_tokens, reasoning_max_tokens)`` for the depth generator call, resolved through the
278	    SAME generator-role knobs the section composer uses (``multi_section_generator`` section-writer leg):
279	    ``PG_SECTION_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS`` bounds the reasoning pool and CONTENT is floored strictly above
280	    it (+``PG_SECTION_CONTENT_HEADROOM_TOKENS``) so a reasoning-first generator (GLM-5.2) cannot starve
281	    content (§9.1.8 "never starve"). No parallel ``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_*`` token knob, no model-specific
282	    hardcoded default — the budget tracks the section composer."""
283	    reasoning = max(0, _env_int("PG_SECTION_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", 16384))
284	    content = max(
285	        _env_int("PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", 64000),
286	        reasoning + _env_int("PG_SECTION_CONTENT_HEADROOM_TOKENS", 8192),
287	    )
288	    return content, reasoning

291	def bib_num_by_evidence_id(bibliography: Any) -> dict[str, int]:
292	    """Build ``evidence_id -> [N]`` from the report's EXISTING bibliography (``multi.bibliography``).
293	
294	    The synthesized sentence cites the SAME sources the body already numbered, so its ``[#ev:...]``
295	    tokens resolve to the report's existing ``[N]`` — NEVER a fresh local renumber (which would
296	    mis-cite). A bibliography row is ``{num, evidence_id, ...}``; rows missing either key are skipped.
297	    """
298	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
299	    for row in bibliography or []:
300	        try:
301	            eid = str(row.get("evidence_id") or "")
302	            num = row.get("num")
303	        except AttributeError:
304	            continue
305	        if not eid or num is None:
306	            continue
307	        try:
308	            out[eid] = int(num)
309	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
310	            continue
311	    return out
312

314	def _resolve_tokens_to_citations(text: str, bib_map: dict[str, int]) -> Optional[str]:
315	    """Replace each ``[#ev:<id>:<a>-<b>]`` token with the report's existing ``[N]`` number.
316	
317	    Returns ``None`` (drop the sentence) if ANY token's evidence_id is absent from ``bib_map`` — a
318	    finding that cannot be cited consistently with the report's own numbering must not ship a dangling
319	    or fabricated citation. PURE."""
320	    missing = False
321	
322	    def _sub(m: re.Match[str]) -> str:
323	        nonlocal missing
324	        num = bib_map.get(m.group(1))
325	        if num is None:
326	            missing = True
327	            return m.group(0)
328	        return f"[{num}]"
329	
330	    out = _EV_TOKEN_FULL_RE.sub(_sub, text or "")
331	    if missing:
332	        return None
333	    return out

368	# ── I-deepfix-001 COV-DECHROME-BASKETS (#1344) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
369	# THE COVERAGE DEFECT (coverage forensic reconcile): a basket MEMBER whose claim-local span resolves to
370	# page furniture (cookie/consent banner, author/affiliation byline, ToC dot-leader, dead-fetch shell)
371	# still arrives here as an isolated-``SUPPORTS`` member — credibility_pass keeps it (correctly, §-1.3
372	# no-drop) but only excludes it from the strengthening COUNT, never from ``supporting_members``. The
373	# cross-source pre-pass then feeds that chrome span into the consolidation prompt / span-join; the
374	# clause fails ``_first_verified_clause`` / re-ground and the WHOLE basket collapses to zero — SILENTLY
375	# (the forensic had to reconstruct these drops because nothing logged them; the depth pre-pass went
376	# 3->0 and the one anchored cross-source pair died on chrome member spans). Screening chrome members
377	# OUT of the corroboration set BEFORE the eligibility gate keeps the pre-pass working on the basket's
378	# REAL members. §-1.3: page furniture is NOT a corroborating source, so holding a chrome member out of
379	# a corroboration count is not a DROP of a real source (the source stays in ``supporting_members`` +
380	# disclosure) — it is the consolidation layer keeping junk out of a count, never a breadth cap / target
381	# / thinner. Faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / D8 / provenance / span-grounding) is untouched.
382	# LAW VI: default-ON kill-switch (OFF => byte-identical legacy member selection).
383	_ENV_DECHROME_MEMBERS = "PG_DEPTH_DECHROME_MEMBERS"
384	
385	
386	def _dechrome_members_enabled() -> bool:
387	    """Default ON. Screen chrome/unrenderable basket members out of the cross-source corroboration set
388	    before eligibility (LAW VI kill-switch: ``PG_DEPTH_DECHROME_MEMBERS=0`` => byte-identical legacy)."""
389	    return os.getenv(_ENV_DECHROME_MEMBERS, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")

392	def _member_span_is_chrome(member: Any) -> bool:
393	    """True iff this member's claim-local span (``direct_quote``) is page furniture / a dead-fetch shell
394	    / a truncated fragment. Reads the DURABLE ``span_is_chrome`` flag credibility_pass stamps at basket
395	    build; falls back to re-screening ``direct_quote`` through the SAME shared render-seam predicate
396	    (``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable``) for a member built without the flag —
397	    called EXACTLY like the basket-build screen (no ``require_sentence_form``) so the flag and the
398	    re-screen never disagree. Fail-CONSERVATIVE: an empty span or any import/screen fault => not-chrome
399	    (never silently hold out a real member)."""

401	        return True

406	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
407	            is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable,
408	        )

414	def _dechrome_distinct_origin_supports(basket: Any, *, log_drops: bool = True) -> list[Any]:
415	    """The basket's distinct-origin isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members with chrome/unrenderable members held
416	    OUT of the cross-source corroboration set (the coverage-forensic root fix). §-1.3: page furniture is
417	    not a corroborating source — this keeps junk out of the corroboration count, it NEVER drops a real
418	    source (the source stays in ``supporting_members`` + disclosure) and NEVER caps/targets breadth. Each
419	    held-out member is logged LOUD per-basket (``log_drops``; the forensic flagged these drops were
420	    SILENT). ``PG_DEPTH_DECHROME_MEMBERS=0`` => ``_distinct_origin_supports`` unchanged."""
421	    members = _distinct_origin_supports(basket)
422	    if not _dechrome_members_enabled():
423	        return members

430	                    "(eid=%s, url=%s)",
431	                    str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or ""),
432	                    str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or ""),
433	                    str(getattr(m, "source_url", "") or ""),
434	                )
435	            continue
436	        kept.append(m)
437	    return kept

454	# ── I-deepfix-001 WS-8 (D4) THIRD headline path — basket-level recency RE-RANK ────────────────────────
455	# Codex found the depth cross-source SYNTHESIS pass orders/consumes ``credibility_analysis.baskets``
456	# DIRECTLY (the ``for basket in baskets`` loop below), bypassing the recency-ordered unbound-supports
457	# selection — so a very-old source can still anchor a TOP Analysis finding. This leg DEMOTES an older
458	# basket in the SYNTHESIS ORDER so it does not headline: a WEIGHT on ordering ONLY — every basket is KEPT
459	# and still synthesized (§-1.3 no-drop / no-cap / no-filter). It reuses the SAME env curve
460	# (``PG_M2_RECENCY_*``: grace 5, decay 0.02, floor 0.25) as the bibliography + composition legs, is gated
461	# on the SAME journal-class signal (``PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT``) AND a new default-ON
462	# ``PG_DEPTH_RECENCY_RERANK`` kill-switch; OFF / non-journal-class / no basket carries a parseable year =>
463	# the basket order is returned UNCHANGED (byte-identical). The faithfulness engine is untouched.
464	_DEPTH_RECENCY_RERANK_ENV = "PG_DEPTH_RECENCY_RERANK"
465	_DS_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:19|20)\d{2}\b")
466	
467	
468	def _depth_recency_rerank_enabled() -> bool:
469	    """Journal-class gate: the depth-synthesis recency re-rank fires only when ``PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT``
470	    is ON (the journal-class run signal, same gate as the bibliography + composition legs) AND
471	    ``PG_DEPTH_RECENCY_RERANK`` is not disabled (default-ON kill-switch)."""
472	    dtw = os.environ.get("PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT", "").strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "on", "yes")
473	    rerank = os.environ.get(_DEPTH_RECENCY_RERANK_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
474	    return dtw and rerank
