477	def _ds_publication_year(entry: "dict | None") -> "int | None":
478	    """Publication year of a member SOURCE dict (the evidence-pool row): an explicit year field, else the
479	    first plausible 4-digit year in title/statement/url/doi. None when none found (=> neutral, no penalty).
480	    Same parse shape as the other WS-8 legs (``weighted_enrichment._we_publication_year``)."""
481	    if not isinstance(entry, dict):
482	        return None
483	    for k in ("year", "publication_year", "pub_year"):
484	        v = entry.get(k)
485	        if v is None:
486	            continue
487	        try:
488	            y = int(str(v).strip()[:4])
489	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
490	            continue
491	        if 1500 <= y <= 2100:
492	            return y
493	    for k in ("title", "statement", "url", "doi"):
494	        m = _DS_YEAR_RE.search(str(entry.get(k) or ""))
495	        if m:
496	            y = int(m.group(0))
497	            if 1500 <= y <= 2100:
498	                return y
499	    return None
500	
501	
502	def _basket_newest_year(basket: Any, evidence_pool: dict) -> "int | None":
503	    """A basket's recency = the NEWEST publication year among its distinct-origin SUPPORTS member sources
504	    (each member's row looked up in ``evidence_pool`` by ``evidence_id``). None when NO member carries a
505	    parseable year (=> the basket is neutral and keeps its order)."""
506	    years: list[int] = []
507	    for m in _distinct_origin_supports(basket):
508	        eid = str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "")
509	        y = _ds_publication_year((evidence_pool or {}).get(eid))
510	        if y is not None:
511	            years.append(y)
512	    return max(years) if years else None
513	
514	
515	def _depth_recency_factor(year: "int | None", reference_year: "int | None") -> float:
516	    """A ``[floor, 1.0]`` ordering multiplier: 1.0 within ``grace`` years of the corpus-newest basket,
517	    decaying linearly by ``decay`` per year older, FLOORED (an old basket is DEMOTED in the synthesis
518	    order, never dropped). Same env curve as the bibliography + composition legs (``PG_M2_RECENCY_*``).
519	    Missing year / missing reference / disabled => 1.0 (byte-identical)."""
520	    if year is None or reference_year is None or not _depth_recency_rerank_enabled():
521	        return 1.0
522	    try:
523	        grace = int(os.getenv("PG_M2_RECENCY_GRACE_YEARS", "5"))
524	        decay = float(os.getenv("PG_M2_RECENCY_DECAY_PER_YEAR", "0.02"))
525	        floor = float(os.getenv("PG_M2_RECENCY_FLOOR", "0.25"))
526	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
527	        grace, decay, floor = 5, 0.02, 0.25
528	    age = max(0, int(reference_year) - int(year) - grace)
529	    return max(floor, 1.0 - decay * age)
530	
531	
532	def _order_baskets_by_recency(baskets: Any, evidence_pool: dict) -> list:
533	    """Re-order the baskets so an OLDER basket is DEMOTED below a NEWER one in the synthesis order (so a
534	    very-old source does not anchor a top Analysis finding) — a STABLE WEIGHT on ordering ONLY: every
535	    basket is KEPT (§-1.3 no-drop / no-cap / no-filter) and ties (including every basket when
536	    disabled/unknown-year) preserve their original relative order => byte-identical. OFF /
537	    non-journal-class / no basket carries a parseable year => the input order is returned UNCHANGED."""
538	    baskets_list = list(baskets or [])
539	    if not baskets_list or not _depth_recency_rerank_enabled():
540	        return baskets_list
541	    years = [_basket_newest_year(b, evidence_pool) for b in baskets_list]
542	    parseable = [y for y in years if y is not None]
543	    ref_year = max(parseable) if parseable else None
544	    if ref_year is None:
545	        return baskets_list  # no parseable year anywhere => byte-identical order
546	    factors = [_depth_recency_factor(y, ref_year) for y in years]
547	    # STABLE DESC sort by recency factor: an older basket (lower factor) sinks below a newer one; equal
548	    # factors (the newest baskets + unknown-year neutral baskets, all 1.0) keep their original relative
549	    # order (byte-identical). No basket is removed — this only permutes; the loop still visits them all.
550	    order = sorted(range(len(baskets_list)), key=lambda i: -factors[i])

551	    return [baskets_list[i] for i in order]
552	
553	
554	def synthesize_cross_source_findings(
555	    baskets: Any,
556	    evidence_pool: dict,
557	    *,
558	    synthesizer: Callable[[Any, dict], str],
559	    verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
560	    bib_num_by_evidence_id: Optional[dict[str, int]] = None,
561	    min_sources: Optional[int] = None,
562	    max_findings: Optional[int] = None,
563	    chrome_screen: Optional[Callable[[str], bool]] = None,
564	    body_sentences: Optional[Any] = None,
565	) -> list[dict]:
566	    """The grounded cross-source synthesis CORE (deterministic given ``synthesizer`` + ``verify_fn``).
567	
568	    For each basket carrying ``>= min_sources`` distinct-origin isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members:
569	      1. ``synthesizer(basket, evidence_pool)`` drafts ONE consolidated cross-source finding carrying
570	         ``[#ev:<id>:<a>-<b>]`` provenance tokens (the LOCKED generator on the cert path; an injected
571	         fake in tests).
572	      2. ``verify_fn(draft, scoped_pool)`` (= ``strict_verify``) RE-GROUNDS each sentence against the
573	         basket's OWN members and DROPS any that fail (numeric mismatch / overlap / no provenance /
574	         cross-claim). FIX-1 (#1344): when the LLM draft re-grounds to ZERO survivors the basket falls
575	         back to the DETERMINISTIC verbatim span-join (``_deterministic_spanjoin_fallback``) re-verified
576	         by the SAME ``verify_fn`` — no drop-to-empty (default-ON ``PG_DEPTH_SYNTHESIS_SPANJOIN_FALLBACK``;
577	         OFF => drop-not-fallback). ``scoped_pool`` is basket-id-bound so a cross-basket
578	         citation fails CLOSED. Each surviving sentence still carries ``>= 1`` grounding span (the
579	         UNCHANGED faithfulness floor); its ``[#ev:...]`` tokens resolve to the report's EXISTING
580	         ``[N]`` (never a renumber) — a raw / unmappable / unresolved token drops the WHOLE sentence;
581	         a chrome/truncated sentence is dropped.
582	      3. I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3c — TWO-TIER, decided PER BASKET, not per sentence (the iter-2 P1 was
583	         structurally near-unsatisfiable: strict_verify grounds each sentence to ONE span, so a single
584	         sentence almost never carries ``>=2`` distinct surviving sources -> every finding dropped ->
585	         depth=0). The cross-source LABEL is now decided on the basket's DISTINCT SURVIVING ORIGINS
586	         accumulated across its kept+resolved sentences (distinct report ``[N]`` numbers when a
587	         ``bib_num_by_evidence_id`` map is supplied — two evidence rows for the SAME source share one
588	         ``[N]`` and read as ONE origin; distinct evidence_ids otherwise):
589	           * ``>= min_sources`` distinct surviving origins -> ``cross_source`` finding;
590	           * exactly the COLLAPSE case (a ``>=floor``-member basket whose synthesis re-grounded to ONE
591	             surviving origin) -> ``single_source`` finding, SURFACED with a ``(single source)`` label,
592	             never dropped (§-1.3 "don't drop, label weak weak").
593	
594	    Returns the ordered, de-duplicated list of grounded findings as dicts
595	    ``{"sentence": <[N]-cited markdown sentence>, "tier": cross_source|single_source, "label": str}``
596	    (no header — ``build_depth_layer`` renders the two tiers under distinct subheads). FAITHFULNESS is
597	    the ``verify_fn`` — this orchestrator never relaxes it and never resurrects a dropped sentence.
598	    """
599	    # I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3c gate P1: the cross-source boundary (eligibility AND tier label) is the
600	    # DEFINITIONAL 2 distinct origins — decoupled from the tunable `min_corroboration()` floor /
601	    # `min_sources` so the two-tier guarantee holds even if that knob is raised. `min_sources` is
602	    # accepted for back-compat but never widens the boundary above 2.
603	    floor = _CROSS_SOURCE_MIN_ORIGINS
604	    cap = _env_int(_ENV_MAX_FINDINGS, _DEFAULT_MAX_FINDINGS) if max_findings is None else int(max_findings)
605	    screen = _default_chrome_screen if chrome_screen is None else chrome_screen
606	
607	    # I-deepfix-006-compose C1: ADDITIVE entailment verify. Wrap the incoming verify_fn (= the FROZEN
608	    # strict_verify — never edited) with the entailment UNION wrapper so a synthesized sentence the
609	    # >=2-verbatim-content-word leg drops but that (a) resolves a provenance token, (b) matches every
610	    # number in its span, and (c) is ENTAILED by that span is RESCUED. Union => strict_verify passes are
611	    # ALWAYS kept; the entailment leg only ADDS. OFF (PG_SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_VERIFY=0) => verify_fn unchanged.
612	    if synthesis_entailment_verify_enabled():
613	        try:
614	            from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.synthesis_entailment_verify import (  # noqa: PLC0415
615	                make_entailment_union_verify_fn,
616	            )
617	            verify_fn = make_entailment_union_verify_fn(verify_fn)
618	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — the entailment union is ADDITIVE; a wiring fault keeps strict_verify
619	            logger.warning("[depth_synthesis] C1 entailment union wrap failed => strict_verify only", exc_info=True)
620	
621	    # I-deepfix-006-compose C2: single-source synthesis. When C1 AND PG_SYNTH_SINGLE_SOURCE are both on,
622	    # a 1-distinct-origin basket is ALSO synthesized (the per-basket tier decision below labels it
623	    # "(single source)", never dropped — §-1.3 "don't drop, label weak weak"). The TIER boundary (cross
624	    # vs single) stays the DEFINITIONAL 2. OFF => the eligibility floor stays 2 (byte-identical).
625	    eligibility_floor = 1 if single_source_synthesis_active() else floor
626	
627	    # #1335: the BODY composer's already-rendered sentences, normalized for duplicate detection so a FIX-1
628	    # span-join digest that is text-identical to a body line is dropped (the body line is kept). None /
629	    # empty => no dedup (byte-identical to the pre-fix render — the guard is a no-op).
630	    body_norm: set[str] = {
631	        _normalize_for_body_dedup(str(s)) for s in (body_sentences or []) if str(s or "").strip()
632	    }
633	    body_norm.discard("")
634	
635	    def _collect(report: Any) -> "tuple[list[tuple[str, str, list, bool]], set[str]]":
636	        """Process a verify report's ``kept_sentences`` into (rendered/audit/token/is_entailment quads,
637	        distinct surviving report-``[N]`` origins). SHARED per-sentence resolution + chrome screen used
638	        for BOTH the LLM draft AND the FIX-1 deterministic span-join fallback, so the fallback re-grounds
639	        through the EXACT same faithfulness path (token->[N] resolution, origin identity, chrome screen)."""
640	        out_sentences: list[tuple[str, str, list, bool]] = []
641	        out_origins: set[str] = set()
642	        for sv in (getattr(report, "kept_sentences", None) or []):
643	            # PRE-resolve audit sentence + its provenance tokens (the D8 inputs), captured BEFORE the
644	            # token->[N] resolution below mutates ``sentence`` into the rendered form.
645	            audit_sentence = str(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "").strip()
646	            toks = list(getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or [])
647	            # C3: True when the C1 entailment leg RESCUED this sentence (the frozen >=2-verbatim leg
648	            # dropped it). Read from the SentenceVerification soft-warnings the union wrapper stamps.
649	            is_entailment = _SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_SOFT_WARNING in list(getattr(sv, "soft_warnings", None) or [])
650	            sentence = audit_sentence
651	            if not sentence:
652	                continue
653	            # The DISTINCT evidence_ids whose ``[#ev:...]`` token SURVIVED strict_verify on THIS sentence
654	            # (a kept sentence carries >=1 grounding span — the UNCHANGED faithfulness floor; a sentence
655	            # with zero surviving provenance tokens is not grounded and is skipped).
656	            surviving_ids = {m.group(1) for m in _EV_TOKEN_FULL_RE.finditer(sentence)}
657	            if not surviving_ids:
658	                continue
659	            if bib_num_by_evidence_id is not None:
660	                # Every surviving token must resolve to the report's EXISTING [N]; a raw / unmatched /
661	                # unresolved token returns None here -> DROP the whole sentence (no dangling [N]).
662	                resolved = _resolve_tokens_to_citations(sentence, bib_num_by_evidence_id)
663	                if resolved is None:
664	                    continue
665	                sentence = resolved.strip()
666	                # Defence-in-depth: no raw ``[#ev:...]`` token may survive resolution.
667	                if _EV_TOKEN_FULL_RE.search(sentence) or "[#ev:" in sentence:
668	                    continue
669	                # Origin identity = the report bibliography NUMBER (distinct SOURCES, not raw evidence
670	                # rows — two rows for the same source share one [N] and count as ONE origin). This is what
671	                # keeps the cross-source label honest (§-1.1 misstated corroboration is lethal).
672	                sentence_origins = {bib_num_by_evidence_id.get(eid) for eid in surviving_ids}
673	                sentence_origins.discard(None)
674	                if not sentence_origins:
675	                    continue
676	                origin_keys = {str(n) for n in sentence_origins}
677	            else:
678	                origin_keys = set(surviving_ids)
679	            if not sentence or screen(sentence):
680	                continue
681	            out_sentences.append((sentence, audit_sentence, toks, is_entailment))
682	            out_origins |= origin_keys
683	        return out_sentences, out_origins
684	
685	    findings: list[dict] = []
686	    seen: set[str] = set()
687	    # WS-8 (D4) THIRD headline path: DEMOTE an older basket in the synthesis ORDER so a very-old source
688	    # does not anchor a top Analysis finding — a WEIGHT on ordering ONLY (every basket kept + still
689	    # synthesized; §-1.3). OFF / non-journal-class / unknown-year => byte-identical order.
690	    # WS-8 P0 fix (Codex waveDE gate): reorder ONLY when there is NO synthesis cap (cap<=0, the default).
691	    # With a POSITIVE cap the loop below drops baskets past the cap line, so reordering by recency BEFORE
692	    # the cap would turn an old-basket demotion into an actual DROP (§-1.3 violation). When capped, keep
693	    # the original weight order for INCLUSION (byte-identical which baskets survive the cap); recency
694	    # reorders only in the uncapped case, where every basket is synthesized so nothing can be dropped.
695	    ordered_baskets = (
696	        _order_baskets_by_recency(baskets, evidence_pool) if cap <= 0 else list(baskets or [])
697	    )
698	    for basket in ordered_baskets:
699	        if cap > 0 and len(findings) >= cap:
700	            break
701	        # COV-DECHROME-BASKETS (#1344): hold chrome/unrenderable member spans OUT of the corroboration
702	        # set BEFORE the eligibility count (the coverage-forensic root; loud per-basket log). §-1.3: not
703	        # a corpus-source filter — the source stays in the basket + disclosure; only its chrome span is
704	        # kept out of the cross-source count. OFF => byte-identical ``_distinct_origin_supports``.
705	        members = _dechrome_distinct_origin_supports(basket)
706	        if len(members) < eligibility_floor:
707	            continue  # not a CANDIDATE basket (definitional, not a filter of corpus sources)
708	        scoped_pool = _scoped_pool(basket, evidence_pool)
709	        # (1) LLM draft -> re-ground through the UNCHANGED verify_fn. Each collected element is a QUAD
710	        # (rendered_[N]_sentence, audit_sentence_pre-resolve, tokens, is_entailment): the audit sentence
711	        # carries the ``[#ev:...]`` tokens for the D8 seam; the rendered sentence is the post-resolution
712	        # [N] form; is_entailment marks a C1 entailment-RESCUED sentence for the C3 D8-promote routing.
713	        basket_sentences: list[tuple[str, str, list, bool]] = []
714	        basket_origins: set[str] = set()
715	        draft = ""
716	        try:
717	            draft = str(synthesizer(basket, evidence_pool) or "")
718	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a per-basket synthesizer failure never aborts the layer
719	            logger.warning("[depth_synthesis] synthesizer raised for a basket", exc_info=True)
720	        if draft.strip():
721	            try:
722	                basket_sentences, basket_origins = _collect(verify_fn(draft, scoped_pool))
723	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a verify failure drops the LLM draft, never ships unverified
724	                logger.warning("[depth_synthesis] verify_fn raised on the LLM draft", exc_info=True)
725	        # (2) I-deepfix-001 P3_dead_synthesis FIX-1 (#1344): the LLM draft re-grounded to ZERO
726	        # strict_verify survivors -> DO NOT drop the basket. Fall back to the DETERMINISTIC verified
727	        # multi-cite span-join the BODY composer already emits (verbatim K-span clauses, NULL writer),
728	        # re-grounded by the SAME verify_fn. This revives the ~11 eligible multi-source baskets the
729	        # free-redraft-then-verify path drops to []. Default-ON kill-switch; OFF => the pre-fix
730	        # drop-not-fallback path (byte-identical: the fallback never runs).
731	        if not basket_sentences and _spanjoin_fallback_enabled():
732	            fallback = _deterministic_spanjoin_fallback(basket, scoped_pool, verify_fn=verify_fn)
733	            if fallback.strip():
734	                try:
735	                    basket_sentences, basket_origins = _collect(verify_fn(fallback, scoped_pool))
736	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a verify failure on the fallback keeps the drop
737	                    logger.warning(
738	                        "[depth_synthesis] verify_fn raised on the span-join fallback", exc_info=True
739	                    )
740	        if not basket_sentences:
741	            continue  # neither the LLM draft NOR the deterministic fallback re-grounded -> true drop
742	        # PER-BASKET two-tier decision: >=floor distinct surviving origins -> cross_source; the collapse
743	        # case (1 surviving origin) -> single_source-attributed (surfaced + labeled, §-1.3). The FIX-1
744	        # fallback flows through the SAME decision, so a fallback that re-grounds to ONE origin is
745	        # honestly labeled "(single source)", never a blanket "corroborated" (§-1.1 lethal-if-misstated).
746	        tier = _TIER_CROSS_SOURCE if len(basket_origins) >= floor else _TIER_SINGLE_SOURCE
747	        label = "" if tier == _TIER_CROSS_SOURCE else _SINGLE_SOURCE_LABEL
748	        # The legacy D8 gate attaches the seam inputs to EVERY finding (existing behavior). C3 ADDITIVELY
749	        # attaches them to a C1 entailment-RESCUED finding when the promote flag is on — so a rescued
750	        # paraphrase is never rendered as body prose without D8 adjudication even if the legacy gate is
751	        # off. A NON-entailment finding with the legacy gate off carries NO seam keys (byte-identical).
752	        carry_d8_legacy = depth_synthesis_d8_gate_enabled()
753	        promote_d8 = synth_d8_promote_enabled()
754	        for rendered, audit_sentence, toks, is_entailment in basket_sentences:
755	            # #1335 body-vs-DS duplicate guard: the FIX-1 deterministic span-join reuses the SAME
756	            # compose_basket_multicited_sentence the BODY composer runs on the SAME basket, so a DS-*
757	            # digest can be text-identical (modulo citation markers) to a body line. Drop the DS-*
758	            # duplicate (keep the body line) so the finding is not rendered twice; fail LOUD when the
759	            # hard-assert canary is on.
760	            if body_norm:
761	                bkey = _normalize_for_body_dedup(rendered)
762	                if bkey and bkey in body_norm:
763	                    if _body_dup_hard_assert_enabled():
764	                        raise RuntimeError(
765	                            "[depth_synthesis] #1335 DS-*/body duplicate: a synthesized digest is "
766	                            f"text-identical to a body composer sentence: {rendered!r}"
767	                        )
768	                    logger.warning(
769	                        "[depth_synthesis] #1335 DS-*/body duplicate dropped (kept body line): %.160s",
770	                        rendered,
771	                    )
772	                    continue
773	            key = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", rendered).strip().lower()
774	            if key in seen:
775	                continue
776	            seen.add(key)
777	            finding: dict = {"sentence": rendered, "tier": tier, "label": label}
778	            if carry_d8_legacy or (promote_d8 and is_entailment):
779	                # The PRE-resolve audit sentence (carries [#ev:...] tokens) + its ProvenanceToken list
780	                # are the D8 seam inputs. ``build_depth_layer`` reads ONLY sentence/tier/label, so these
781	                # extra keys are inert to the render; the native Gate-B builder consumes them. Legacy gate
782	                # OFF + not an entailment rescue => keys omitted (byte-identical to the pre-change shape).
783	                finding["audit_sentence"] = audit_sentence
784	                finding["tokens"] = toks
785	                # C3: mark a C1 entailment-RESCUED finding so the native Gate-B builder routes it into the
786	                # D8 4-role input set under PG_SYNTH_D8_PROMOTE (independent of the legacy D8 gate).
787	                if is_entailment:
788	                    finding["is_synth_entailment"] = True
789	            findings.append(finding)
790	            if cap > 0 and len(findings) >= cap:
791	                break
792	    return findings

795	# ── the live LLM synthesizer (cert path) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
796	_SYNTHESIS_SYSTEM = (
797	    "You consolidate several already-verified evidence spans that report the SAME finding into ONE "
798	    "clean, plain, declarative news-style sentence. You state the consolidated finding, then — only "
799	    "if the spans themselves show it — note where the sources AGREE or where they are in TENSION. You "
800	    "NEVER add a fact, number, or claim that is not in a provided span. You copy every number "
801	    "(decimal, percent, integer, dose) exactly as written; never round, never convert units. You end "
802	    "the sentence with the exact provenance token(s) supplied, copied character-for-character; you "
803	    "never invent or edit a token. You output EXACTLY one sentence, nothing else — no markdown, no "
804	    "bullets, no headings, no preamble."
805	)
806	
807	
808	def _build_synthesis_prompt(basket: Any, members: list, evidence_pool: dict) -> str:
809	    """One cross-source prompt: every distinct-origin SUPPORTS member's verified span + the EXACT
810	    canonical token (``_member_global_span``). Returns "" when no member resolves a global span."""
811	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import _member_global_span  # noqa: PLC0415
812	
813	    subject = str(getattr(basket, "claim_text", "") or getattr(basket, "subject", "") or "").strip()
814	    lines: list[str] = [
815	        "Consolidate the corroborating evidence spans below into ONE clean, plain, declarative "
816	        "news-style sentence that states the shared finding and (only if the spans show it) where "
817	        "the sources agree or are in tension. End the sentence with EVERY provenance token shown "
818	        "below, each copied character-for-character. Copy every number verbatim. Output one sentence.",
819	        "",
820	    ]
821	    if subject:
822	        lines.append(f"CLAIM UNDER CORROBORATION: {subject}")
823	        lines.append("")
824	    emitted = 0
825	    for i, m in enumerate(members, start=1):
826	        eid = str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "")
827	        gspan = _member_global_span(m, evidence_pool)
828	        quote = str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
829	        if not eid or gspan is None or not quote:
830	            continue
831	        token = f"[#ev:{eid}:{gspan[0]}-{gspan[1]}]"
832	        lines.append(f"SOURCE {i}: {quote}")
833	        lines.append(f"TOKEN {i} (append verbatim): {token}")
834	        lines.append("")
835	        emitted += 1
836	    if emitted == 0:
837	        return ""
838	    return "\n".join(lines)
839	
840	
841	async def _synthesize_one_basket(
842	    basket: Any,
843	    evidence_pool: dict,
844	    *,
845	    model: str,
846	    max_tokens: int,
847	    reasoning_max_tokens: int,
848	    temperature: float,
849	    call_deadline_s: float,
850	) -> str:
851	    """ONE live generator call: consolidate a basket's SUPPORTS spans into one cross-source sentence
852	    carrying the canonical tokens. Returns "" on any error/timeout (the basket is simply absent from
853	    the synthesis -> the layer omits it; never crashes the run). Chrome members are input-screened
854	    BEFORE the call (a paraphrase would mangle the multi-word markers, so only an input screen catches
855	    them)."""
856	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import _compose_junk_screen  # noqa: PLC0415
857	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
858	
859	    # COV-DECHROME-BASKETS (#1344): hold chrome/unrenderable member spans OUT of the SYNTHESIS input
860	    # BEFORE the prompt is built (the coverage-forensic root: a chrome member span mangles the multi-word
861	    # markers and collapses the pre-pass). The eligibility gate already logged the per-basket drop, so
862	    # ``log_drops=False`` here avoids double-logging the same member. The existing ``_compose_junk_screen``
863	    # allowlist pass is retained as an inner belt-and-suspenders filter (no regression).
864	    members = [
865	        m for m in _dechrome_distinct_origin_supports(basket, log_drops=False)
866	        if not _compose_junk_screen(str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or ""))
867	    ]
868	    # C2: when single-source synthesis is active (C1 + PG_SYNTH_SINGLE_SOURCE both on), a 1-member basket
869	    # IS drafted (labeled "(single source)" downstream, never dropped). OFF => the DEFINITIONAL >=2 floor
870	    # holds (byte-identical: a single-member basket is not drafted).
871	    _draft_floor = 1 if single_source_synthesis_active() else min_corroboration()
872	    if len(members) < _draft_floor:
873	        return ""
874	    prompt = _build_synthesis_prompt(basket, members, evidence_pool)
875	    if not prompt:
876	        return ""
877	    reasoning_arg = reasoning_max_tokens if reasoning_max_tokens and reasoning_max_tokens > 0 else None
878	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
879	    try:
880	        response = await asyncio.wait_for(
881	            client.generate(
882	                prompt=prompt,
883	                system=_SYNTHESIS_SYSTEM,
884	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
885	                temperature=temperature,
886	                reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_arg,
887	            ),
888	            timeout=call_deadline_s,
889	        )
890	        return str(getattr(response, "content", "") or "")
891	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any error/timeout degrades to absent (always-release)
892	        logger.warning("[depth_synthesis] basket synthesis call failed -> omitted", exc_info=True)
893	        return ""
894	    finally:
895	        try:
896	            await client.close()
897	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort teardown; never mask the result
898	            logger.debug("[depth_synthesis] client.close() raised on teardown", exc_info=True)
899

901	async def depth_synthesis_pre_pass(
902	    baskets: Any,
903	    evidence_pool: dict,
904	    *,
905	    min_sources: Optional[int] = None,
906	) -> dict:
907	    """ASYNC pre-pass: precompute one cross-source draft per high-corroboration basket, keyed by the
908	    basket's canonical ``claim_cluster_id``, under a bounded semaphore + a per-call deadline + an OUTER
909	    wall-deadline. Reuses the PROVEN abstractive-writer teardown-drain (abandon a wall-stuck task; never
910	    await it — the httpx-teardown hang class). A skipped/failed/abandoned basket is simply absent ->
911	    the sync synthesizer returns "" -> the layer omits that finding (always-release, fail-open)."""
912	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.abstractive_writer import (  # noqa: PLC0415
913	        _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS,
914	        _drain_detached_writer_task,
915	        _force_drop_detached_writer_task,
916	        install_teardown_drain_hook,
917	    )
918	
919	    # I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3c gate P1: the cross-source boundary (eligibility AND tier label) is the
920	    # DEFINITIONAL 2 distinct origins — decoupled from the tunable `min_corroboration()` floor /
921	    # `min_sources` so the two-tier guarantee holds even if that knob is raised. `min_sources` is
922	    # accepted for back-compat but never widens the boundary above 2.
923	    floor = _CROSS_SOURCE_MIN_ORIGINS
924	    # C2: single-source synthesis lowers the pre-pass DRAFT eligibility to 1 (a 1-origin basket is drafted
925	    # and labeled "(single source)" downstream, never dropped) when C1 + PG_SYNTH_SINGLE_SOURCE are both
926	    # on. OFF => the DEFINITIONAL >=2 floor holds (byte-identical: single-member baskets are not drafted).
927	    eligibility_floor = 1 if single_source_synthesis_active() else floor
928	    model = _resolve_model()
929	    max_tokens, reasoning_max_tokens = _resolve_token_budget()
930	    max_tokens = max(1, max_tokens)
931	    reasoning_max_tokens = max(0, reasoning_max_tokens)
932	    temperature = _env_float(_ENV_TEMPERATURE, _DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE)
933	    call_deadline_s = max(1.0, _env_float(_ENV_CALL_DEADLINE_S, _DEFAULT_CALL_DEADLINE_S))
934	    wall_deadline_s = max(1.0, _env_float(_ENV_WALL_DEADLINE_S, _DEFAULT_WALL_DEADLINE_S))
935	    concurrency = max(1, _env_int(_ENV_CONCURRENCY, _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY))
936	
937	    # COV-DECHROME-BASKETS (#1344): eligibility counts the DECHROMED corroboration set (chrome member
938	    # spans held out) so a basket whose only "second origin" is page furniture is not falsely eligible.
939	    # This is also the ONE per-basket LOUD log site for the async path (``log_drops`` default True);
940	    # ``_synthesize_one_basket`` re-derives with ``log_drops=False`` to avoid double-logging.
941	    eligible = [
942	        b for b in (baskets or [])
943	        if str(getattr(b, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
944	        and len(_dechrome_distinct_origin_supports(b)) >= eligibility_floor
945	    ]
946	    out: dict = {}
947	    if not eligible:
948	        logger.info("[depth_synthesis] pre-pass: 0 eligible high-corroboration baskets (model=%s)", model)
949	        return out
950	
951	    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
952	
953	    async def _one(basket: Any) -> None:
954	        key = str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
955	        async with sem:
956	            draft = await _synthesize_one_basket(
957	                basket, evidence_pool,
958	                model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens,
959	                reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_max_tokens, temperature=temperature,
960	                call_deadline_s=call_deadline_s,
961	            )
962	        if draft:
963	            out[key] = draft
964	
965	    tasks = [asyncio.ensure_future(_one(b)) for b in eligible]
966	    try:
967	        install_teardown_drain_hook(asyncio.get_running_loop())
968	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — hook install is best-effort
969	        logger.debug("[depth_synthesis] teardown-drain hook install skipped", exc_info=True)
970	
971	    done, pending = await asyncio.wait(tasks, timeout=wall_deadline_s)
972	    if pending:
973	        logger.warning(
974	            "[depth_synthesis] pre-pass WALL %.0fs hit: ABANDONING %d/%d pending basket task(s) "
975	            "(fail-open). %d drafted before the wall.", wall_deadline_s, len(pending), len(tasks), len(out),
976	        )
977	        for t in pending:
978	            _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.add(t)
979	            t.add_done_callback(_drain_detached_writer_task)
980	            t.cancel()
981	            _force_drop_detached_writer_task(t)
982	    for t in done:
983	        exc = t.exception()
984	        if exc is not None:
985	            logger.warning("[depth_synthesis] a pre-pass basket task raised: %r", exc)
986	    logger.info(
987	        "[depth_synthesis] pre-pass complete: %d/%d baskets drafted (model=%s, wall=%.0fs, abandoned=%d)",
988	        len(out), len(eligible), model, wall_deadline_s, len(pending),
989	    )
990	    return out
991	
992	
993	def make_depth_synthesizer(precomputed: dict) -> Callable[[Any, dict], str]:
994	    """The SYNC ``synthesizer`` ``synthesize_cross_source_findings`` reads: a pure dict lookup keyed by
995	    the basket's canonical ``claim_cluster_id``. A missing key returns "" -> that basket is omitted
996	    from the synthesis layer (never a crash). Mirrors ``abstractive_writer.make_abstractive_writer_fn``."""
997	
998	    def _synth(basket: Any, _pool: dict) -> str:
999	        return str(precomputed.get(str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or ""), "") or "")
1000

1	"""
2	Map-reduce evidence distiller â€” I-perm-016 (#1209) keystone generation fix.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	---------------
6	The legacy per-section generation path hands V4 Pro the raw ``<<<evidence:...>>>``
7	quote blocks plus an atom catalog and asks it to write cited prose in ONE pass.
8	The reasoning-first writer then has to (a) read every quote, (b) decide what is
9	section-relevant, (c) keep numbers exact, and (d) emit ``[#ev:...]`` provenance
10	tokens â€” all at once. The drb_72 / drb_76 forensic sweeps showed this conflated
11	job leaks: the writer over-claims, mis-binds numbers across sources, and
12	``strict_verify`` then DROPS the over-claimed sentences (drb_76 baseline: 40
13	verified / 41 dropped, 29 of the drops being entailment failures). The faithful
14	content is there in the corpus; the single-pass writer just cannot hold it.
15	
16	THE FIX (map-reduce)
17	--------------------
18	1. MAP â€” one LLM call PER SOURCE distills the source into atomic, span-grounded
19	   findings. Each finding is LOCALLY VALIDATED fail-closed: its support_quote must
20	   LOCATE a REAL source slice (exact/whitespace/fuzzy recovery, #1217), and a
21	   FUZZY (paraphrase-recovered) finding must additionally ENTAIL the claim via the
22	   production verifier (``verify_sentence_provenance``). Exact/whitespace findings
23	   are verbatim source text, so their per-finding entailment is non-blocking and
24	   the (slow) verifier call is skipped for them (#1217 perf). A finding that cannot
25	   be located, or a fuzzy finding the verifier does not entail, is rejected here
26	   before it can pollute the ledger. Every input source yields >=1 accepted finding
27	   OR a ``CoverageRow`` so no source ever silently disappears (LAW II fail-loud).
28	2. REDUCE â€” the section writer composes prose over the VALIDATED findings ledger
29	   only (reference-first). It never sees raw quotes; it cites findings with the
30	   same legacy ``[ev_XXX]`` markers used by the raw-evidence path. The downstream
31	   ``_rewrite_draft_with_spans`` (unchanged) binds those markers to spans for the
32	   FINAL sentence, then ``strict_verify`` (unchanged) re-checks every sentence
33	   exactly as today â€” the distiller TIGHTENS, it never relaxes, the faithfulness
34	   gates.
35	
36	FAITHFULNESS INVARIANT
37	----------------------
38	This module NEVER weakens a gate. A distilled finding is only ever a CANDIDATE
39	admitted by EXTRACTION-side checks (located in a real source slice; fuzzy
40	recoveries additionally entailment-gated); the FINAL section prose is re-verified
41	sentence-by-sentence by the unchanged ``strict_verify`` (numbers-in-span AND >=2
42	content-word overlap AND enforce-mode entailment) exactly as the legacy path is —
43	that final gate, NOT the per-finding extraction check, is the SOLE publication
44	authority. The distiller can only REDUCE what the writer is allowed to assert.
45	
46	Flag-gated entirely by ``PG_SECTION_DISTILL`` (read in the multi_section seam).
47	This module is only imported when that flag is ON; when OFF the legacy path is
48	byte-identical (no import, no call, no prompt change).
49	"""
50	from __future__ import annotations
51	
52	import asyncio
53	import hashlib
54	import json
55	import logging
56	import os
57	import re
58	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
59	from pathlib import Path
60	from typing import Any, Optional
61	
62	from src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor import (
63	    build_atom_catalog,
64	    filter_atoms_for_section,
65	)
66	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
67	    verify_sentence_provenance,
68	)
69	
70	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.evidence_distiller")
71	
72	# Bump when the prompt, validation, or dataclass shape changes so stale cache
73	# entries from an older distiller can never be replayed against a new contract.
74	DISTILLER_VERSION = "section_distiller_v7"  # bumped #1218: v5=MAP exhaustive+atomic-numeric extraction + REDUCE one-statistic-per-sentence/use-every-finding/[OR]->(OR) (thinness fix) — prompt change invalidates v4 cache
75	
76	
77	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
78	# Env knobs (LAW VI â€” named, env-overridable; no magic numbers)
79	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
80	
81	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
82	    """Read a positive-int env knob; fall back to default on missing/garbage."""
83	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
84	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
85	        return default
86	    try:
87	        return int(raw)
88	    except ValueError:
89	        logger.warning(
90	            "[evidence_distiller] %s=%r is not an int â€” using default %d",
91	            name, raw, default,
92	        )
93	        return default
94	
95	
96	def _map_max_tokens() -> int:
97	    return _env_int("PG_DISTILL_MAP_MAX_TOKENS", 8192)
98	
99	
100	def _map_reasoning_tokens() -> int:
101	    # BUG-2 (#1262): the MAP call is per-SOURCE atomic extraction — short,
102	    # bounded, NON-prose work (one source -> a few span-grounded findings). Its
103	    # modest 4096 reasoning budget is the DELIBERATE small-call reduction that
104	    # protects content per drb_76 (reasoning eating the whole completion ceiling
105	    # -> ZERO content -> ReasoningFirstTruncationError). It is NOT the starved
106	    # main-section call and is intentionally LEFT AS-IS by this fix.
107	    return _env_int("PG_DISTILL_MAP_REASONING_TOKENS", 4096)
108	
109	
110	def _map_text_mode() -> bool:
111	    # I-arch-007 distill_map chokepoint: deepseek-v4-pro (a reasoning-first model)
112	    # DUMPS the answer into the reasoning channel under
113	    # response_format=json_object, emitting EMPTY content and burning the full
114	    # 32768 ceiling (~410s) per call (openrouter_client.py:1817-1819,:1838). Text
115	    # mode (response_format=None) keeps the JSON in the CONTENT channel — proven by
116	    # the section_reduce control (multi_section_generator.py:6925, same model/same
117	    # 32768 floor, content populates). _parse_map_json already parses fenced/plain
118	    # JSON from content. Default OFF = byte-identical json_object behavior
119	    # (flag-OFF-byte-identical convention); set PG_DISTILL_MAP_TEXT_MODE=1 to
120	    # activate. NOT a faithfulness change: distill MAP is per-source consolidation,
121	    # not one of the 4 hard gates; output still flows through
122	    # _validate_and_store_one_source unchanged.
123	    return os.getenv("PG_DISTILL_MAP_TEXT_MODE", "0").strip().lower() in (
124	        "1", "true", "yes",
125	    )
126	
127	
128	def _reduce_max_tokens() -> int:
129	    return _env_int("PG_DISTILL_REDUCE_MAX_TOKENS", 8192)
130	
131	
132	# BUG-2 (#1262): sane reasoning FLOOR for the REDUCE call — the MAIN section-prose
133	# writer in the distillate keystone path (multi_section_generator
134	# `_reduce_reasoning_tokens()` site, call_type="section_reduce"). This is full
135	# section composition over the validated findings ledger, NOT a small extraction or
136	# <=3-sentence contract-slot call. The keystone's birth default (5000, #1209) was an
137	# initial guess that token-STARVES this call vs CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 ("reasoning always
138	# max"): deepseek-v4-pro emits ~5-18k reasoning tokens for full prose composition
139	# (openrouter_client.py:1820-1837 in-code evidence), and the equivalent LEGACY
140	# section writer (multi_section_generator.py:2802, no explicit reasoning cap) gets
141	# ~0.4*32768 ≈ 13k reasoning headroom for the SAME job. A 5000 hint sits BELOW that
142	# band, so a provider that DOES honor reasoning.max_tokens could truncate V4 Pro's
143	# plan mid-stream -> the REDUCE ReasoningFirstTruncationError guard returns an empty
144	# draft -> that section DROPS. FAITHFULNESS IS SAFE: raising the reasoning floor can
145	# only let MORE sections finish composing (never fewer, never a relaxed gate) — the
146	# content ceiling is independently floored to 32768 (openrouter_client.py:1838) so
147	# wider reasoning does not starve content, and every REDUCE sentence is still
148	# re-verified by the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI gates downstream. Env-overridable
149	# (LAW VI); the floor only guarantees the MAIN section call is never capped below a
150	# sane value — operators may set the env knob HIGHER. The small-call reductions
151	# (_map_reasoning_tokens above; PG_CONTRACT_SLOT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS) are untouched.
152	PG_DISTILL_REDUCE_REASONING_MIN_TOKENS: int = _env_int(
153	    "PG_DISTILL_REDUCE_REASONING_MIN_TOKENS", 16384
154	)
155	
156	
157	def _reduce_reasoning_tokens() -> int:
158	    # BUG-2 (#1262): clamp the configured REDUCE reasoning budget UP to the sane
159	    # main-section floor so the keystone section writer can never be token-starved
160	    # below it (default raised 5000 -> floor). max() keeps a deliberately HIGHER
161	    # operator override intact; it only prevents an accidentally-tiny value.
162	    configured = _env_int(
163	        "PG_DISTILL_REDUCE_REASONING_TOKENS",
164	        PG_DISTILL_REDUCE_REASONING_MIN_TOKENS,
165	    )
166	    return max(configured, PG_DISTILL_REDUCE_REASONING_MIN_TOKENS)
167	
168	
169	def _max_parallel() -> int:
170	    # B19/I-arch-011 (KEYSTONE): raise the MAP fan-out default 4 -> 8 so a wide
171	    # corpus distills with more sources in flight (breadth-positive; the per-call
172	    # wall below bounds each one). DNA = consolidate-keep-all, never a throttle.
173	    return max(1, _env_int("PG_DISTILL_MAX_PARALLEL", 8))
174	
175	
176	def _distill_map_call_wall_s() -> int:
177	    # B19/I-arch-011 (KEYSTONE — root cause of the B15 generator hang): bound EACH
178	    # distill_map LLM call with an EXPLICIT per-call wall, mirroring the sibling
179	    # PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S in multi_section_generator.py. Without an explicit
180	    # timeout, openrouter_client._call_impl resolves a reasoning-first model
181	    # (deepseek-v4-pro) to ~6530s (GENERATOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS + 30); a half-open SSE
182	    # socket then hangs the asyncio loop for ~1.8h until only the 10800s run-wall
183	    # backstops. DEFAULT 1800: telemetry shows a HEALTHY distill call ran 1475s and
184	    # COMPLETED, so 1800 sits safely above the healthy band yet well under the
185	    # 10800s run-wall. An explicit timeout always wins (_resolve_call_timeout /
186	    # _call_impl: actual_timeout = timeout or default). LAW VI: env-overridable.
187	    return _env_int("PG_DISTILL_MAP_CALL_WALL_S", 1800)
188	
189	
190	async def _call_distill_map_with_wall(client, **call_kwargs):
191	    """Run ONE distill_map LLM call bounded END-TO-END by PG_DISTILL_MAP_CALL_WALL_S.
192	
193	    B19/I-arch-011 iter-2 (Codex P1): ``openrouter_client._call`` applies its ``timeout=``
194	    PER retry attempt and retries up to MAX_RETRIES, so a half-open SSE socket could occupy
195	    the map fan-out for ~3*(wall+grace)s before a coverage row appears — not the single
196	    ``wall`` the B19 contract promises, and enough (under many hung sources) to push the
197	    distill stage past the 10800s run-wall and lose the render. Wrap the whole call in an
198	    OUTER ``asyncio.wait_for(wall)`` so the TOTAL across internal retries is bounded at
199	    ``wall``: a FAST transient failure still retries within the remaining budget, but a hung /
200	    pathologically-slow call is cancelled at ``wall`` and surfaces as the caller's loud
201	    ``map_failed`` coverage row instead of retry-hanging. The inner per-attempt ``timeout=`` is
202	    kept as a defensive bound. A wall breach raises ``asyncio.TimeoutError`` (caught by the
203	    caller). No source is silently dropped; no faithfulness gate is touched.
204	    """
205	    wall = float(_distill_map_call_wall_s())
206	    return await asyncio.wait_for(
207	        client._call(call_type="distill_map", timeout=wall, **call_kwargs),
208	        timeout=wall,
209	    )
210	
211	
212	def _microbatch_size() -> int:
213	    """MAP micro-batch size, clamped to 1..3 (per spec: per-source by default,
214	    allow 1..3 only)."""
215	    n = _env_int("PG_DISTILL_MICROBATCH_SIZE", 1)
216	    return min(3, max(1, n))
217	
218	
219	def _default_cache_dir() -> Path:
220	    return Path(".cache") / "polaris" / "evidence_distiller"
221	
222	
223	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
224	# Dataclasses (spec Â§1)
225	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
226	
227	@dataclass(frozen=True)
228	class DistilledFinding:
229	    finding_id: str
230	    evidence_id: str
231	    claim: str
232	    span_start: int
233	    span_end: int
234	    support_quote: str
235	    numbers: list[str]
236	    entities: list[str]
237	    caveat: str
238	    contradiction_key: str
239	    source_tier: str
240	    atom_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
241	
242	
243	@dataclass(frozen=True)
244	class CoverageRow:
245	    evidence_id: str
246	    status: str  # mapped | no_relevant_findings | map_failed | validation_failed
247	    n_findings: int
248	    reason: str = ""
249	
250	
251	@dataclass(frozen=True)
252	class ContradictionCluster:
253	    contradiction_key: str
254	    finding_ids: list[str]
255	    summary: str = ""
256	
257	
258	@dataclass(frozen=True)
259	class SectionDistillate:
260	    section_title: str
261	    section_focus: str
262	    findings: list[DistilledFinding]
263	    coverage: list[CoverageRow]
264	    contradiction_clusters: list[ContradictionCluster]
265	    atom_catalog: dict[str, Any]
266	    input_tokens: int = 0
267	    output_tokens: int = 0
268	    cache_hits: int = 0
269	    version: str = DISTILLER_VERSION
270	
271	
272	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
273	# Prompts (spec Â§3 â€” verbatim)
274	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
275	
276	_MAP_SYSTEM = (
277	    "You extract atomic, provenance-preserving findings for one report section.\n"
278	    "Return JSON only. Use only the supplied evidence text. Do not infer beyond it.\n"
279	    "Each finding must be one atomic claim grounded in one exact substring of "
280	    "direct_quote.\n"
281	    "EXHAUSTIVE BUT STRICTLY ON-TOPIC (#1218): first decide what THIS section is about "
282	    "from SECTION_TITLE/SECTION_FOCUS, then extract EVERY distinct finding that is "
283	    "GENUINELY ABOUT THAT TOPIC as its own atomic finding — including every on-topic "
284	    "numeric outcome (incidence, rate, fatality, odds ratio, relative risk, hazard "
285	    "ratio, confidence interval, count, percentage), and every on-topic qualifier or "
286	    "caveat. Do NOT stop at the most salient few WITHIN the topic; but do NOT pad with "
287	    "OFF-TOPIC findings. For a SAFETY / ADVERSE-EVENT / CONTRAINDICATION section, "
288	    "ON-TOPIC = harms, adverse events, contraindications, toxicity, warnings, "
289	    "infections, and risks attributable to THE INTERVENTION itself (with their numbers); "
290	    "OFF-TOPIC = general disease-causation or carcinogenesis MECHANISMS not framed as a "
291	    "risk of the intervention — return no_relevant_findings=true for a source that only "
292	    "describes such mechanisms.\n"
293	    "ONE STATISTIC PER FINDING (#1218): each finding's support_quote MUST be an EXACT, "
294	    "CONTIGUOUS substring of direct_quote that contains EVERY number in the claim. If "
295	    "the numbers for one result are NOT contiguous in the source (e.g. a count in one "
296	    "sentence and an odds ratio in another), SPLIT into separate findings, each with "
297	    "its own contiguous support_quote. Never bundle numbers from different source "
298	    "locations into one finding.\n"
299	    "If no section-relevant finding exists, return no_relevant_findings=true with "
300	    "a reason."
301	)
302	
303	_REDUCE_SYSTEM = (
304	    "Write the section using only the validated findings ledger.\n"
305	    "Every factual sentence must cite at least one [[finding:f...]] marker and the "
306	    "evidence marker copied EXACTLY from that finding's ledger 'cite=[...]' field — "
307	    "reproduce the bracketed id verbatim; do NOT add or remove an 'ev_' prefix.\n"
308	    "CRITICAL marker placement: put ALL markers INSIDE the sentence they support, "
309	    "immediately BEFORE that sentence's terminal period. NEVER place markers on their "
310	    "own line, in a separate sentence, or after the period. Correct: "
311	    "'Colibactin induces double-strand breaks in cultured cells [[finding:f002_000]] "
312	    "[ev_colibactin_pks].' Wrong: 'Colibactin induces double-strand breaks in cultured "
313	    "cells. [[finding:f002_000]] [ev_colibactin_pks]'\n"
314	    "USE EVERY validated finding (#1218): emit AT LEAST ONE sentence for each finding "
315	    "in the ledger (co-cite true duplicates together); do not summarize findings away.\n"
316	    "ONE STATISTIC PER SENTENCE (#1218): write each NUMERIC result in its OWN sentence; "
317	    "NEVER combine two distinct statistics (e.g. an odds ratio from one finding and a "
318	    "fatality rate or sample count from another) into a single sentence. Every number "
319	    "in a sentence must come from ONE finding's quote so it binds to one source span.\n"
320	    "Rewrite any source bracket abbreviation (e.g. [OR], [CI], [RR]) as PARENTHESES — "
321	    "(OR), (CI), (RR) — so it is never mistaken for a citation marker.\n"
322	    "A sentence must be exactly one type: single-source claim, multi-source "
323	    "conjunction (NON-numeric only), or conflict-limitation.\n"
324	    "For numeric outcome/incidence/effect claims, copy the finding's atom_ids as "
325	    "(atom_NNN) before the evidence marker.\n"
326	    "Do not emit [#ev:...] span tokens; spans are computed after drafting. "
327	    "No uncited prose. No headings."
328	)
329	
330	
331	def _render_map_user(
332	    *,
333	    section_title: str,
334	    section_focus: str,
335	    evidence_id: str,
336	    tier: str,
337	    statement: str,
338	    direct_quote: str,
339	    atom_rows: str,
340	    research_question: str = "",
341	) -> str:
342	    """Render the MAP user prompt (spec Â§3).
343	
344	    I-arch-004 F21 (#1255): when `research_question` is supplied, a FRAMING-ONLY
345	    line is prepended so the per-source extractor can decide on-topic relevance
346	    against the actual question (the _MAP_SYSTEM rules already turn on
347	    "what THIS section is about"). It is NOT a citable source: findings are still
348	    extracted ONLY from DIRECT_QUOTE and validated against it. Empty (the default)
349	    => byte-identical to pre-F21."""
350	    _rq = (research_question or "").strip()
351	    rq_line = (
352	        "RESEARCH_QUESTION (framing only — extract findings ONLY from "
353	        f"DIRECT_QUOTE, never from this line): {_rq}\n"
354	        if _rq
355	        else ""
356	    )
357	    return (
358	        f"{rq_line}"
359	        f"SECTION_TITLE: {section_title}\n"
360	        f"SECTION_FOCUS: {section_focus}\n"
361	        f"EVIDENCE_ID: {evidence_id}\n"
362	        f"SOURCE_TIER: {tier}\n"
363	        f"STATEMENT: {statement}\n"
364	        f"DIRECT_QUOTE:\n"
365	        f"{direct_quote}\n\n"
366	        f"NUMERIC_ATOMS_AVAILABLE:\n"
367	        f"{atom_rows}\n\n"

400	def _render_reduce_narrative_context(
401	    advisory_text: str = "",
402	    cross_trial_summaries: list[str] | None = None,
403	) -> str:
404	    """I-perm-018 (#1210): render the domain advisory + cross-trial inferences as
405	    FRAMING-ONLY narrative context for the REDUCE prompt.
406	
407	    The legacy path threads `advisory_text` (domain tone/emphasis guidance) and the
408	    M-72 cross-trial synthesis block into the section prompt; the distill REDUCE
409	    branch previously dropped both, so the distilled section lost that narrative
410	    richness. This block restores it WITHOUT touching faithfulness: it is explicitly
411	    framing-only — the REDUCE writer must still produce every sentence from the
412	    VALIDATED_FINDINGS_LEDGER and cite its [[finding:]] marker; nothing here is a
413	    finding or a citable source. (We deliberately do NOT reuse
414	    `render_cross_trial_synthesis_block`, whose 'Cite the contributing [ev_XXX]
415	    markers' instruction is incompatible with the distill filter that drops any
416	    sentence lacking a [[finding:]] ledger marker.) Empty inputs → "" → the prompt
417	    is byte-identical to pre-#1210."""
418	    advisory = (advisory_text or "").strip()
419	    summaries = [s.strip() for s in (cross_trial_summaries or []) if s and s.strip()]
420	    if not advisory and not summaries:
421	        return ""
422	    parts = [
423	        "NARRATIVE FRAMING CONTEXT (framing ONLY — NOT findings, NOT sources):",
424	        "Use the guidance below to shape TONE, EMPHASIS, and the ORDER in which you "
425	        "present the ledger findings. You must NOT write or cite any sentence FROM "
426	        "this block: every sentence still comes from the VALIDATED_FINDINGS_LEDGER "
427	        "above and carries its [[finding:]] marker. Do NOT introduce numbers, claims, "
428	        "or sources that are not in the ledger.",
429	    ]
430	    if advisory:
431	        parts.append(f"\nDOMAIN_ADVISORY:\n{advisory}")
432	    if summaries:
433	        bullets = "\n".join(f"  - {s}" for s in summaries)
434	        parts.append(
435	            "\nCROSS_TRIAL_CONNECTIONS (relationships among ledger findings you may "
436	            f"emphasise when ordering your sentences):\n{bullets}"
437	        )
438	    return "\n".join(parts)
439	
440	
441	def render_reduce_user(
442	    distillate: SectionDistillate,
443	    *,
444	    advisory_text: str = "",
445	    cross_trial_summaries: list[str] | None = None,
446	    research_question: str = "",
447	) -> str:
448	    """Render the REDUCE user prompt (spec Â§3): the validated findings ledger,
449	    one line per finding, plus contradiction clusters.
450	
451	    I-perm-018 (#1210): when `advisory_text` / `cross_trial_summaries` are supplied,
452	    a FRAMING-ONLY narrative-context block is appended (see
453	    `_render_reduce_narrative_context`). Both empty (the default) → byte-identical.
454	
455	    I-arch-004 F21 (#1255): when `research_question` is supplied, a single
456	    FRAMING-ONLY line is prepended so the REDUCE writer knows what the report is
457	    about. It is NOT a finding and NOT a citable source — every sentence still
458	    comes from the VALIDATED_FINDINGS_LEDGER and carries its [[finding:]] marker
459	    (the distill filter drops any sentence lacking one, and strict_verify is
460	    unchanged), so the question text can never publish as an unsupported claim.
461	    Empty (the default) => byte-identical to pre-F21."""
462	    ledger_lines: list[str] = []
463	    for f in distillate.findings:
464	        atom_ids = ",".join(f.atom_ids)
465	        quote = f.support_quote.replace("\n", " ")
466	        ledger_lines.append(
467	            f"{f.finding_id} | {f.evidence_id} | {f.source_tier} | "
468	            f"cite=[{f.evidence_id}] | atom_ids={atom_ids} | "
469	            f"claim={f.claim} | caveat={f.caveat} | "
470	            f"contradiction_key={f.contradiction_key} | quote={quote}"
471	        )
472	    ledger = "\n".join(ledger_lines) if ledger_lines else "(no findings)"
473	
474	    cluster_lines: list[str] = []
475	    for c in distillate.contradiction_clusters:
476	        ids = ",".join(c.finding_ids)
477	        cluster_lines.append(
478	            f"{c.contradiction_key}: {ids}"
479	            + (f" â€” {c.summary}" if c.summary else "")
480	        )
481	    clusters = "\n".join(cluster_lines) if cluster_lines else "(none)"
482	
483	    narrative = _render_reduce_narrative_context(advisory_text, cross_trial_summaries)
484	    narrative_section = f"{narrative}\n\n" if narrative else ""
485	
486	    # F21 (#1255): framing-only research-question line. Empty => byte-identical.
487	    _rq = (research_question or "").strip()
488	    rq_section = (
489	        "RESEARCH_QUESTION (framing only — NOT a finding, NOT a citable source): "
490	        f"{_rq}\n\n"
491	        if _rq
492	        else ""
493	    )
494	
495	    return (
496	        f"{rq_section}"
497	        f"SECTION_TITLE: {distillate.section_title}\n"
498	        f"SECTION_FOCUS: {distillate.section_focus}\n\n"
499	        f"VALIDATED_FINDINGS_LEDGER:\n{ledger}\n\n"
500	        f"CONTRADICTION_CLUSTERS:\n{clusters}\n\n"

511	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€

513	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€

515	# Match the first balanced-looking top-level JSON object in a noisy string.
516	# Reasoning-first models sometimes wrap the JSON in ```json fences or prose.
517	_JSON_OBJECT_RE = re.compile(r"\{.*\}", re.DOTALL)
518	
519	
520	def _parse_map_json(content: str | None, reasoning: str | None) -> Optional[dict]:
521	    """Parse the MAP JSON defensively from content OR reasoning.
522	
523	    OpenRouter's reasoning-first behaviour (per openrouter_client) can route the
524	    JSON object into either the content field or the reasoning field. Try content
525	    first (the response_format target), then reasoning, then a regex-extracted
526	    object from either. Returns None if nothing parses â€” the caller treats that as
527	    a map failure and emits a CoverageRow (no source disappears).
528	    """
529	    for raw in (content, reasoning):
530	        if not raw or not raw.strip():
531	            continue
532	        # Strip code fences if present.
533	        candidate = raw.strip()
534	        if candidate.startswith("```"):
535	            candidate = candidate.strip("`")
536	            # drop a leading "json" language tag
537	            if candidate.lstrip().lower().startswith("json"):
538	                candidate = candidate.lstrip()[4:]
539	        try:
540	            obj = json.loads(candidate)
541	            if isinstance(obj, dict):
542	                return obj
543	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
544	            pass
545	        # Last resort: extract the first {...} block.
546	        m = _JSON_OBJECT_RE.search(raw)
547	        if m:
548	            try:
549	                obj = json.loads(m.group(0))
550	                if isinstance(obj, dict):
551	                    return obj
552	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
553	                continue
554	    return None

561	def _cache_key(
562	    *,
563	    section_title: str,
564	    section_focus: str,
565	    evidence_id: str,
566	    direct_quote: str,
567	) -> str:
568	    """sha256(distiller_version + fuzzy_threshold + title + focus + evidence_id +
569	    sha256(direct_quote)).
570	
571	    #1217 Codex diff-gate P2: the key includes ``PG_DISTILL_FUZZY_MIN_OVERLAP`` so
572	    that retuning the fuzzy-recovery threshold after a cache is populated cannot
573	    replay stale fuzzy ACCEPTS at the old threshold — a tighter/looser threshold is
574	    a different validation outcome and must miss the cache."""
575	    dq_hash = hashlib.sha256(direct_quote.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
576	    payload = "\n".join(
577	        [
578	            DISTILLER_VERSION,
579	            f"fuzzy_min_overlap={_distill_fuzzy_min_overlap_frac()}",
580	            section_title, section_focus, evidence_id, dq_hash,
581	        ]
582	    )
583	    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()

591	    """Load a cached list of VALIDATED finding dicts, or None on miss/corruption."""
592	    path = _cache_path(cache_dir, key)
593	    if not path.is_file():
594	        return None
595	    try:
596	        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
597	        if isinstance(data, list):
598	            return data
599	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc:
600	        logger.warning(
601	            "[evidence_distiller] cache read failed for %s: %s â€” treating as miss",
602	            path, exc,
603	        )
604	    return None

621	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
622	# Numeric helpers â€” mirror strict_verify's definitions (spec Â§1 validation)
623	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
624	
625	def _numbers_in_text(text: str) -> set[str]:
626	    """Numeric tokens under the boundary-aware definition shared with the
627	    downstream verifier (spec section 1 check 4: "the same numeric definitions
628	    as strict_verify plus claim_atom_extractor").
629	
630	    Uses claim_atom_extractor._NUMBER_ATOM_RE, whose ``(?<![A-Za-z0-9_.])``
631	    lookbehind correctly excludes the ``1`` embedded in "HbA1c" (preceded by a
632	    letter) while still catching ``-1.86``, ``99.9``, and ranges. The bare
633	    strict_verify._decimals pattern (no boundary) over-tokenizes "HbA1c" into a
634	    junk ``1`` that would both over-strict the numbers-in-span pre-filter AND let
635	    a non-numeric claim spuriously satisfy the numeric->atom map (via
636	    ``"1" in atom_literal``). The boundary-aware regex is therefore the
637	    authoritative tokenizer here. The AUTHORITATIVE numeric verdict is still
638	    delegated to verify_sentence_provenance(require_number_match=True) downstream
639	    (check 5), which runs strict_verify's full numbers-in-span machinery — this
640	    pre-filter is a cheap, boundary-correct subset of it.
641	
642	    claim_atom_extractor is imported lazily so this module does not couple to its
643	    import graph at load. claim_atom_extractor + strict_verify are UNCHANGED.
644	    """
645	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor import _NUMBER_ATOM_RE
646	
647	    nums: set[str] = set()
648	    for m in _NUMBER_ATOM_RE.finditer(text):
649	        raw = m.group("value").strip()
650	        if not raw:
651	            continue
652	        nums.add(raw)
653	        # sign-normalize the unicode minus so "-1.86" and the unicode-minus form
654	        # both count as the same number.
655	        nums.add(raw.replace("−", "-"))
656	    return nums
657	
658	
659	def _all_numbers_in_span(
660	    claim: str, numbers: list[str], support_quote: str,
661	) -> bool:
662	    """Every number asserted in the claim (and the declared `numbers` list) must
663	    appear in the support span. One-way constraint, identical in spirit to
664	    strict_verify's numeric_mismatch check. The authoritative numeric verdict is
665	    still delegated to verify_sentence_provenance(require_number_match=True); this
666	    is the cheap local pre-filter so an out-of-span number is rejected before the
667	    (more expensive) entailment call."""
668	    span_numbers = _numbers_in_text(support_quote)
669	    claim_numbers = _numbers_in_text(claim)
670	    declared = {str(n) for n in numbers}
671	    declared_numeric = {n for n in declared if _numbers_in_text(n)}
672	    required = claim_numbers | declared_numeric
673	    return required.issubset(span_numbers)

676	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
677	# Atom matching (spec Â§1: numeric findings must map to a section-local atom)
678	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
679	
680	def _match_atom_ids(
681	    *,
682	    evidence_id: str,
683	    numbers: set[str],
684	    section_atoms: dict[str, Any],
685	) -> list[str]:
686	    """Deterministic atom match rule:
687	
688	    A finding maps to a section-local atom iff the atom shares the finding's
689	    evidence_id AND one of the finding's numbers equals the atom's `value` OR
690	    appears in the atom's `literal_text`. Returns the sorted matched atom_ids.
691	
692	    Numeric findings with NO matching section-local atom are rejected upstream,
693	    because the downstream atom validator only sees the section catalog (spec Â§1).
694	    """
695	    matched: list[str] = []
696	    for aid, atom in sorted(section_atoms.items()):
697	        if getattr(atom, "evidence_id", "") != evidence_id:
698	            continue
699	        atom_value = str(getattr(atom, "value", "") or "")
700	        atom_literal = str(getattr(atom, "literal_text", "") or "")
701	        # Tokenize BOTH sides with the boundary-aware tokenizer so a finding
702	        # number matches an atom only on a WHOLE-number basis (e.g. "2.30" does
703	        # NOT match inside "12.301"). Same boundary class as the tokenizer fix.
704	        atom_numbers = _numbers_in_text(atom_value) | _numbers_in_text(atom_literal)
705	        if numbers & atom_numbers:
706	            matched.append(aid)
707	    return matched
708	
709	
710	def _distill_fuzzy_min_overlap_frac() -> float:
711	    """Min fraction of the quote's content words that must appear in a candidate
712	    source window for #1217 fuzzy span recovery to ACCEPT it (LAW VI: env-tunable,
713	    default 0.6). Higher = stricter recovery."""
714	    try:
715	        return float(os.getenv("PG_DISTILL_FUZZY_MIN_OVERLAP", "0.6"))
716	    except ValueError:
717	        return 0.6
718	
719	
720	def _fuzzy_locate_span(quote: str, source: str) -> Optional[tuple[int, int]]:
721	    """#1217 Bug B fix (Codex pre-approved candidate (c), CONSTRAINED): recover the
722	    REAL source window that best matches a PARAPHRASED MAP support_quote, or None.
723	
724	    The MAP model frequently paraphrases (drops markdown italics like ``_S.
725	    cerevisiae_``; atomizes one source sentence into several) so the quote is not a
726	    verbatim or whitespace-flexible substring — the live probe on the CDC safety
727	    source rejected all 3 contraindication findings here ("3 proposed, 0 validated"),
728	    the exact claims the legacy arm verified. This finds the source window with the
729	    highest content-word overlap with the quote and ACCEPTS it only if the overlap
730	    meets a threshold, then SHRINKS to the tight span between the first and last
731	    matched content word. Faithfulness-safe (Codex constraint): it returns a GENUINE
732	    source slice — NEVER the model's paraphrase text — and the claim is still
733	    entailment-checked against it, then the final REDUCE prose is re-checked by the
734	    UNCHANGED strict_verify, so a wrong recovery can never publish an unsupported
735	    claim. It only RAISES recall to parity with the final gate's own span binder."""
736	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import _content_words
737	
738	    qwords = _content_words(quote)
739	    if len(qwords) < 2:
740	        return None  # too little signal to recover safely
741	    need = max(2, int(_distill_fuzzy_min_overlap_frac() * len(qwords) + 0.9999))
742	
743	    n = len(source)
744	    window = min(n, max(200, len(quote) * 2))
745	    stride = max(20, window // 8)
746	
747	    best: Optional[tuple[int, int]] = None
748	    best_ov = 0
749	    starts = list(range(0, max(1, n - window + 1), stride))
750	    starts.append(max(0, n - window))  # always include the tail window
751	    for i in starts:
752	        end = min(i + window, n)
753	        ov = len(qwords & _content_words(source[i:end]))
754	        if ov > best_ov:
755	            best_ov = ov
756	            best = (i, end)
757	    if best is None or best_ov < need:
758	        return None
759	
760	    # Localize to the matched content-word region, then EXPAND to the enclosing
761	    # clause/sentence boundary — do NOT shrink to the first/last content word.
762	    # #1217 Codex diff-gate P2 (clinical faithfulness): a tight content-word shrink
763	    # drops leading FUNCTION words, so a source clause "... are not recommended for
764	    # immunocompromised patients" would shrink to "recommended for immunocompromised
765	    # patients" — flipping the negation BEFORE the per-finding entailment check sees
766	    # it. Widening each side to the nearest sentence terminator keeps "not"/"no"/
767	    # "without"/qualifier words inside the span so the entailment gate judges the TRUE
768	    # meaning. (The final strict_verify still rebinds against the original evidence
769	    # row, but the fuzzy gate must not be blind to negation.)
770	    wstart, wend = best
771	    win = source[wstart:wend]
772	    first_off: Optional[int] = None
773	    last_off: Optional[int] = None
774	    for m in re.finditer(r"[A-Za-z0-9]+", win):
775	        if m.group(0).lower() in qwords:
776	            if first_off is None:
777	                first_off = m.start()
778	            last_off = m.end()
779	    if first_off is None or last_off is None:
780	        return best
781	    # Left boundary: just after the previous sentence terminator (. ! ? or newline).
782	    left = max(win.rfind(".", 0, first_off), win.rfind("\n", 0, first_off),
783	               win.rfind("!", 0, first_off), win.rfind("?", 0, first_off))
784	    start_off = left + 1 if left >= 0 else 0
785	    while start_off < first_off and win[start_off] in " \t\r\n":
786	        start_off += 1
787	    # Right boundary: through the next sentence terminator at/after the last match.
788	    m_end = re.search(r"[.\n!?]", win[last_off:])
789	    end_off = last_off + m_end.end() if m_end else len(win)
790	    return wstart + start_off, wstart + end_off
791

793	def _locate_span_with_method(quote: str, source: str) -> Optional[tuple[int, int, str]]:
794	    """Locate ``quote`` inside ``source``, returning (start, end, method) of the
795	    REAL source slice, or None. ``method`` ∈ {"exact", "stripped", "whitespace",
796	    "fuzzy"} — the caller uses it to apply an EXTRA entailment gate to the riskier
797	    "fuzzy" (paraphrase-recovered) case (#1217).
798	
799	    I-perm-016 / #1217: the MAP model rarely copies a span VERBATIM — it reformats
800	    whitespace/newlines AND paraphrases (drops markdown italics, atomizes a source
801	    sentence). An exact-substring-only check rejected ~most findings, collapsing the
802	    distillate to empty. We recover the real span at four escalating tolerances; the
803	    output gates (entailment + the final strict_verify) remain the authority on the
804	    prose, and "fuzzy" recoveries get an additional blocking entailment check in
805	    `_validate_finding` so a meaning-changing paraphrase (e.g. "all" -> "some",
806	    negation flips) cannot enter the ledger on content-word overlap alone."""
807	    if not quote or not source:
808	        return None
809	    i = source.find(quote)
810	    if i >= 0:
811	        return i, i + len(quote), "exact"
812	    stripped = quote.strip()
813	    if stripped and stripped != quote:
814	        i = source.find(stripped)
815	        if i >= 0:
816	            return i, i + len(stripped), "stripped"
817	    toks = stripped.split()
818	    if not toks:
819	        return None
820	    # Same tokens, any whitespace run between them (newline/space reformatting).
821	    pattern = r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in toks)
822	    try:
823	        m = re.search(pattern, source)
824	    except re.error:
825	        m = None
826	    if m:
827	        return m.start(), m.end(), "whitespace"
828	    # #1217 Bug B: exact + whitespace-flexible failed -> the MAP paraphrased the
829	    # quote (markdown italics dropped, source sentence atomized). Recover the REAL
830	    # source window by content-word overlap (CONSTRAINED, threshold-gated). Returns a
831	    # genuine source slice or None; the caller adds a blocking entailment gate.
832	    fz = _fuzzy_locate_span(stripped, source)
833	    if fz is not None:
834	        return fz[0], fz[1], "fuzzy"
835	    return None
836	
837	
838	def _locate_span_in_source(quote: str, source: str) -> Optional[tuple[int, int]]:
839	    """Backward-compatible wrapper: (start, end) of the REAL source slice, or None.
840	    See `_locate_span_with_method` for the recovery tolerances."""
841	    r = _locate_span_with_method(quote, source)
842	    return None if r is None else (r[0], r[1])

845	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
846	# MAP validation â€” fail-closed (spec Â§1)
847	# â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
