699	def build_groups(
700	    sections: dict[str, list[str]],
701	    section_order: Optional[list[str]] = None,
702	) -> list[RedundancyGroup]:
703	    """Group sentences across sections by overlap-matched FactSignature.
704	
705	    Per Codex Phase 3 diagnosis (`.codex/I-gen-002-phase3/`): the
706	    earlier exact-FactSignature grouping was too strict — incidental
707	    years/dollars added in one section but not another broke the match.
708	    Now uses `_signatures_overlap` core-fact matcher to group
709	    semantically equivalent sentences even when contextual numerics
710	    differ.
711	
712	    Args:
713	        sections: dict mapping section_title -> list of sentence strings.
714	        section_order: optional explicit ordering for PRIMARY selection.
715	            If None, falls back to insertion order of `sections` keys.
716	
717	    Returns:
718	        List of RedundancyGroup, one per overlap-cluster appearing in
719	        2+ section-distinct sentences. Empty signatures are excluded.
720	        Sentences within the same section that share a signature are
721	        NOT counted as redundancy.
722	    """
723	    if section_order is None:
724	        section_order = list(sections.keys())
725	
726	    # Collect all locations with their signatures
727	    all_locations: list[SentenceLocation] = []
728	    for section_title in section_order:
729	        if section_title not in sections:
730	            continue
731	        for idx, sentence in enumerate(sections[section_title]):
732	            sig = extract_signature(sentence)
733	            if sig.is_empty():
734	                continue
735	            all_locations.append(SentenceLocation(
736	                section=section_title, index=idx,
737	                sentence=sentence, signature=sig,
738	            ))
739	
740	    # Transitive clustering by compatibility (overlap + no-conflict):
741	    # each location is compared against ALL members of each existing
742	    # cluster. The candidate joins a cluster iff it has at least one
743	    # overlapping member AND no conflicting member. When the candidate is
744	    # compatible with multiple clusters, those clusters are merged into
745	    # the lowest-index target ONLY IF the clusters themselves are
746	    # pairwise-compatible (no cross-cluster member conflict). This
747	    # prevents contextless-bridge merges (Codex brief-iter-1 P1 fix).
748	    clusters: list[list[SentenceLocation]] = []
749	    for loc in all_locations:
750	        compat_idx = [
751	            i for i, cluster in enumerate(clusters)
752	            if _cluster_is_compatible(loc, cluster)
753	        ]
754	        if not compat_idx:
755	            clusters.append([loc])
756	            continue
757	        target = clusters[compat_idx[0]]
758	        target.append(loc)
759	        # Codex brief-iter-2 P1 fix: check compatibility against the
760	        # GROWING target (not the original), so two non-target clusters
761	        # that conflict with each other cannot both be folded in via the
762	        # same candidate bridge. Iteration order is the natural compat_idx
763	        # order; for each follow-on candidate, the test is against the
764	        # target's CURRENT membership.
765	        merged_idx: list[int] = []
766	        for j in compat_idx[1:]:
767	            if _clusters_pairwise_compatible(target, clusters[j]):
768	                target.extend(clusters[j])
769	                merged_idx.append(j)
770	        for j in sorted(merged_idx, reverse=True):
771	            del clusters[j]
772	
773	    groups: list[RedundancyGroup] = []
774	    _exact_relax = _fact_dedup_exact_intrasection_enabled()
775	    for cluster in clusters:
776	        # Filter: at least 2 distinct sections must share this cluster
777	        distinct_sections = {loc.section for loc in cluster}
778	        if len(distinct_sections) < 2:
779	            # I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 (#1344, finding #10): the >=2-distinct-section gate excludes an
780	            # INTRA-section duplicate — the exact drb_72 case where the identical Acemoglu-Restrepo
781	            # figure appears twice in ONE section (one twin carrying a leaked contract-field label
782	            # prefix). Consolidate EXACT duplicates (identical text after prefix-strip + normalize)
783	            # even within one section, so the twins collapse to one representative + cross-references.
784	            # SCOPED to exact dups only (a mere overlap-signature match within a section is NOT
785	            # consolidated — that would over-collapse distinct-but-related sentences). Kill-switch OFF
786	            # => byte-identical to the pre-fix >=2-section gate.
787	            if not _exact_relax:
788	                continue
789	            by_norm: dict[str, list[SentenceLocation]] = {}
790	            for loc in cluster:
791	                by_norm.setdefault(_exact_dup_norm(loc.sentence), []).append(loc)
792	            for _norm, members in by_norm.items():
793	                if _norm and len(members) >= 2:
794	                    groups.append(RedundancyGroup(
795	                        signature=members[0].signature,
796	                        primary=members[0],
797	                        redundants=members[1:],
798	                    ))
799	            continue
800	        # Within the cluster, primary = first location in section_order;
801	        # redundants = all other locations (even same section as primary,
802	        # if they appear after — but typically these will be from
803	        # different sections).
804	        primary = cluster[0]
805	        redundants = cluster[1:]
806	        groups.append(RedundancyGroup(
807	            signature=primary.signature,
808	            primary=primary,
809	            redundants=redundants,
810	        ))
811	
812	    # I-beatboth-011 §3.3 (#1289): PROSE-repetition pass — clusters the EMPTY-numeric-signature
813	    # sentences (skipped above at the `sig.is_empty(): continue`) by content-word shingle Jaccard.
814	    # GATED behind PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE (default OFF => this block is a no-op and `groups` is exactly
815	    # the numeric-path result, byte-identical to today). Prose groups append to the SAME `groups` list
816	    # so they route through the UNCHANGED keep-all cross-ref rewrite downstream (§-1.3 consolidate).
817	    if _prose_dedup_enabled():
818	        groups.extend(_build_prose_groups(
819	            sections, section_order, _read_prose_jaccard(),
820	        ))
821	
822	    # I-wire-001 W1 (#1306): CONSOLIDATION-NLI winner — clusters the SAME empty-numeric
823	    # sentences by BIDIRECTIONAL NLI (same-claim paraphrases the literal/Jaccard floor
824	    # leaves separate). GATED behind PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI (default OFF => no-op, byte-
825	    # identical). Appends to the SAME `groups` list so it routes through the UNCHANGED
826	    # keep-all cross-ref rewrite downstream (§-1.3 consolidate; faithfulness FROZEN).
827	    # NOTE: the primary behavioral seam for this winner is `finding_dedup.dedup_by_finding`
828	    # (the source-basket corroboration path the §-1.4 canary asserts on); this block is the
829	    # plan-listed companion seam in the SENTENCE-redundancy path.
830	    if _consolidation_nli_enabled_factdedup():
831	        groups.extend(_build_nli_prose_groups(sections, section_order))
832	
833	    return groups
834	
835	
836	def count_redundancy(groups: list[RedundancyGroup]) -> int:
837	    """Total redundant sentences across all groups."""
838	    return sum(len(g.redundants) for g in groups)
839	
840	
841	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
842	# Rewrite — LLM call
843	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
844	
845	
846	REWRITE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are rewriting redundant sentences from a multi-section research report so each redundant sentence becomes a brief CROSS-REFERENCE back to the section where the fact was first established (the PRIMARY section).
847	
848	INPUT: A list of (REDUNDANT sentence, PRIMARY section name) pairs. The redundant sentence currently restates a fact that already appears in the PRIMARY section.
849	
850	OUTPUT: A JSON object {"rewrites": [...]} with one rewrite per input. Each rewrite is the new sentence to replace the redundant one.
851	
852	RULES:
853	1. Keep at least ONE evidence-ID citation marker `[ev_XXX]` from the original sentence in the rewrite.
854	2. Rewrites should be ONE sentence, 6-20 words.
855	3. Use the natural cross-reference idiom: "as noted under {PRIMARY_SECTION} [ev_X]", "see the {PRIMARY_SECTION} section [ev_X]", "the same finding is detailed in {PRIMARY_SECTION} [ev_X]", etc.
856	4. PRESERVE the topical anchor in the rewrite — what the fact is ABOUT — even though the decimals/amounts move to the PRIMARY section. Example: a redundant sentence about regressivity-by-income gets rewritten as "Regressivity by income is detailed under {PRIMARY_SECTION} [ev_X]" — readers still see what the cross-ref points to.
857	5. Do NOT invent new claims. Do NOT cite evidence-IDs that weren't in the original sentence.
858	6. If you cannot construct a valid cross-reference (e.g., the sentence has no [ev_XXX] markers, or the topical anchor is unclear), return null for that rewrite.
859	
860	OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON):
861	{"rewrites": ["new sentence 1", "new sentence 2", null, "new sentence 4"]}
862	"""
863	
864	
865	def _build_rewrite_prompt(
866	    redundancy_groups: list[RedundancyGroup],
867	) -> tuple[str, list[tuple[RedundancyGroup, SentenceLocation]]]:
868	    """Build the user prompt + a flat list of (group, redundant_loc) for
869	    aligning the rewrite outputs back to source.
870	
871	    Returns:
872	        (prompt_text, flat_list_of_input_locations_in_output_order)
873	    """
874	    lines = ["INPUTS (redundant sentence, primary section name):", ""]
875	    flat: list[tuple[RedundancyGroup, SentenceLocation]] = []
876	    counter = 0
877	    for group in redundancy_groups:
878	        for redundant in group.redundants:
879	            counter += 1
880	            lines.append(f"{counter}. REDUNDANT: {redundant.sentence}")
881	            lines.append(f"   PRIMARY_SECTION: {group.primary.section}")
882	            lines.append("")
883	            flat.append((group, redundant))
884	    lines.append(
885	        f"Return JSON {{\"rewrites\": [...]}} with exactly "
886	        f"{counter} entries in the same order as INPUTS."
887	    )
888	    return "\n".join(lines), flat
889	
890	
891	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
892	# I-deepfix-001 V6 (#1344): provenance-token carry-through for the rewrite.
893	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
894	# ROOT (grounded in drb_72_ai_labor/reasoning_trace.jsonl _fact_dedup call): the
895	# anti-restatement rewrite LLM DELIBERATELY converts the span-bearing canonical
896	# provenance token `[#ev:ev_109:839-961]` into a BARE `[ev_109]` marker — its own
897	# reasoning: "the original uses [#ev:ev_109:839-961]. The rule says keep at least one
898	# [ev_XXX] citation marker. Let me use ... [ev_X]." The re-verify strict_verify then
899	# parses provenance tokens from the rewrite, finds NO valid `[#ev:id:start-end]` token,
900	# and DROPS every rewrite with `no_provenance_token` (manifest fact_dedup:
901	# n_rewrites_strict_verify_pass=0 / n_rewrites_strict_verify_drop=3).
902	#
903	# FIX: carry the ORIGINAL redundant sentence's REAL span-bearing tokens through the
904	# rewrite so the UNCHANGED strict_verify can re-check it (shell / numeric / content /
905	# entailment) instead of auto-failing on a stripped token. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL
906	# (§-1.3): we only restore the sentence's OWN real grounding that the LLM stripped —
907	# never fabricate a token, never relax a gate. A genuinely ungroundable rewrite (junk
908	# shell span, no content overlap) STILL drops in strict_verify. LAW VI kill-switch:
909	# PG_FACT_DEDUP_CARRY_PROVENANCE=0 restores the byte-identical pre-fix behaviour.
910	_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE_FACTDEDUP = re.compile(
911	    r"\[#ev:(?P<ev_id>[A-Za-z0-9_]+):(?P<start>\d+)-(?P<end>\d+)\]"
912	)
913	
914	
915	def _carry_provenance_enabled() -> bool:
916	    """PG_FACT_DEDUP_CARRY_PROVENANCE gate (default ON)."""
917	    return os.getenv("PG_FACT_DEDUP_CARRY_PROVENANCE", "1").strip().lower() not in (
918	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
919	    )
920	
921	
922	def _restore_provenance_tokens(rewrite_text: str, original_sentence: str) -> str:
923	    """Carry the ORIGINAL sentence's span-bearing `[#ev:id:start-end]` tokens onto the
924	    LLM rewrite so strict_verify can re-verify it.
925	
926	    For each canonical token id present in ``original_sentence``: if the rewrite already
927	    carries that exact canonical token, leave it; else, if the rewrite carries a BARE /
928	    span-less marker for the SAME id (``[ev_id]`` / ``[#ev:id]`` / ``[ev_id:s-e]``),
929	    replace it with the canonical token(s); else APPEND the canonical token(s)
930	    (KEEP-ALL, §-1.3 — a corroborating source is never dropped). Ids never in the
931	    original are left untouched (strict_verify still polices them). Deterministic,
932	    stdlib-only; returns ``rewrite_text`` unchanged when the original carries no
933	    canonical token."""
934	    original_tokens = list(_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE_FACTDEDUP.finditer(original_sentence or ""))
935	    if not original_tokens:
936	        return rewrite_text
937	    # ev_id -> ordered, de-duplicated list of its canonical span-bearing tokens.
938	    canonical_by_id: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
939	    for m in original_tokens:
940	        toks = canonical_by_id.setdefault(m.group("ev_id"), [])
941	        if m.group(0) not in toks:
942	            toks.append(m.group(0))
943	
944	    text = rewrite_text
945	    for ev_id, toks in canonical_by_id.items():
946	        canonical = "".join(toks)  # all spans for this id (KEEP-ALL)
947	        if canonical in text:
948	            continue  # rewrite already carries the exact span-bearing grounding
949	        # A bare / span-less marker for THIS specific id (matched literally, never a
950	        # different id or a numeric `[12]` marker).
951	        bare_re = re.compile(r"\[(?:#ev:)?" + re.escape(ev_id) + r"(?::\d+-\d+)?\]")
952	        if bare_re.search(text):
953	            _state = {"done": False}
954	
955	            def _sub(_m: "re.Match[str]") -> str:
956	                if _state["done"]:
957	                    return ""  # collapse duplicate bare markers for the same id
958	                _state["done"] = True
959	                return canonical
960	
961	            text = bare_re.sub(_sub, text)
962	        else:
963	            # No marker for this id at all -> append the canonical token(s) (KEEP-ALL),
964	            # keeping any trailing sentence period after the citation.
965	            stripped = text.rstrip()
966	            if stripped.endswith("."):
967	                text = stripped[:-1].rstrip() + " " + canonical + "."
968	            else:
969	                text = stripped + " " + canonical
970	    return text
971	
972	
973	async def rewrite_redundant_sentences(
974	    redundancy_groups: list[RedundancyGroup],
975	    llm_callable: Callable[[str, str], Any],
976	) -> dict[tuple[str, int], Optional[str]]:
977	    """Issue a single LLM call to rewrite all redundant sentences as
978	    cross-references.
979	
980	    Args:
981	        redundancy_groups: from build_groups().
982	        llm_callable: async callable(system, prompt) -> response object
983	            with .content attribute. Decoupled from openrouter_client
984	            to keep this module test-friendly.
985	
986	    Returns:
987	        dict mapping (section, index) -> merged multi-citation sentence string.
988	        A redundant whose rewrite FAILED or came back null/empty is OMITTED from
989	        the dict (no key) so apply_rewrites keeps its ORIGINAL cited sentence —
990	        consolidate-keep-all (§-1.3): a failed merge never deletes a corroborating
991	        source. (The None value is still honored as an explicit drop by
992	        apply_rewrites, but the failure/null fallbacks no longer emit it.)
993	    """
994	    import json
995	
996	    if not redundancy_groups:
997	        return {}
998	
999	    prompt, flat = _build_rewrite_prompt(redundancy_groups)
1000	    try:
1001	        response = await llm_callable(REWRITE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, prompt)
1002	        content = getattr(response, "content", None) or response
1003	        # Parse JSON; tolerate code fences
1004	        text = str(content).strip()
1005	        if text.startswith("```"):
1006	            # strip ```json … ``` or ``` … ```
1007	            lines = text.splitlines()
1008	            text = "\n".join(
1009	                line for line in lines
1010	                if not line.strip().startswith("```")
1011	            )
1012	        parsed = json.loads(text)
1013	        rewrites = parsed.get("rewrites", [])
1014	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError, KeyError, TypeError) as e:
1015	        logger.warning(
1016	            "[fact_dedup] rewrite call failed (%s); falling back to "
1017	            "KEEP-redundants (consolidate-keep-all, §-1.3)", e,
1018	        )
1019	        # Safe fallback (§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-DON'T-DROP): the anti-restatement rewrite is an
1020	        # OPTIMIZATION (merge corroborating sentences into one multi-citation sentence). When it
1021	        # fails we must NOT delete the corroborating cited sentences — emit NO drop keys so
1022	        # apply_rewrites keeps every original sentence verbatim (repetition = corroboration).
1023	        return {}
1024	
1025	    if not isinstance(rewrites, list) or len(rewrites) != len(flat):
1026	        logger.warning(
1027	            "[fact_dedup] rewrite response shape mismatch "
1028	            "(expected %d items, got %r); falling back to KEEP (consolidate-keep-all)",
1029	            len(flat), type(rewrites).__name__,
1030	        )
1031	        return {}
1032	
1033	    out: dict[tuple[str, int], Optional[str]] = {}
1034	    for (_group, loc), rewrite in zip(flat, rewrites):
1035	        if isinstance(rewrite, str) and rewrite.strip():
1036	            rewrite_text = rewrite.strip()
1037	            # I-deepfix-001 V6 (#1344): carry the ORIGINAL sentence's span-bearing
1038	            # `[#ev:id:start-end]` provenance token(s) through the rewrite BEFORE the
1039	            # KEEP-ALL check + return, so the re-verify strict_verify can re-check the
1040	            # rewrite instead of auto-dropping it on the LLM-stripped bare `[ev_id]`
1041	            # marker (`no_provenance_token`). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: restores the
1042	            # sentence's OWN real grounding; never fabricates, never relaxes a gate.
1043	            if _carry_provenance_enabled():
1044	                rewrite_text = _restore_provenance_tokens(rewrite_text, loc.sentence)
1045	            # KEEP-ALL ENFORCEMENT (§-1.3; Codex #1289 iter-1 NOVEL-P0): a *successful* rewrite must NOT
1046	            # silently DROP a corroborating source. Keep-all is a GROUP-GLOBAL invariant: the redundant
1047	            # is cross-reffed to the PRIMARY (which is kept verbatim and carries ALL its own citations),
1048	            # so the redundant may safely shed a token it SHARES with the primary — but every source
1049	            # UNIQUE to this redundant (a citation token NOT in the primary) MUST survive the rewrite, or
1050	            # it vanishes from the report entirely. Require those unique-vs-primary tokens to be present;
1051	            # if any is missing, DISCARD the rewrite and KEEP the original cited sentence (emit no key —
1052	            # the absent-key branch in apply_rewrites preserves it). A false reject only forgoes an
1053	            # optimization; it can never drop a source. (The numeric path shares this guard, harmlessly.)
1054	            original_cites = set(_CITATION_TOKEN_RE.findall(loc.sentence or ""))
1055	            primary_cites = set(_CITATION_TOKEN_RE.findall(getattr(_group.primary, "sentence", "") or ""))
1056	            must_survive = original_cites - primary_cites  # sources unique to this redundant
1057	            rewrite_cites = set(_CITATION_TOKEN_RE.findall(rewrite_text))
1058	            if must_survive and not must_survive <= rewrite_cites:
1059	                logger.warning(
1060	                    "[fact_dedup] rewrite at (%s,%d) dropped redundant-unique citation token(s) %s "
1061	                    "(not carried by the primary); KEEPing the original cited sentence "
1062	                    "(consolidate-keep-all, §-1.3)",
1063	                    loc.section, loc.index, sorted(must_survive - rewrite_cites),
1064	                )
1065	                continue
1066	            out[(loc.section, loc.index)] = rewrite_text
1067	        # else: a null/empty rewrite for THIS item means the merge produced nothing — KEEP the
1068	        # original cited sentence (consolidate-keep-all, §-1.3) by emitting NO key for it, so
1069	        # apply_rewrites' absent-key branch preserves the corroborating sentence rather than
1070	        # dropping it. Never delete a corroborating source on a failed rewrite.
1071	    return out
1072	
1073	
1074	def apply_rewrites(
1075	    sections: dict[str, list[str]],
1076	    rewrites: dict[tuple[str, int], Optional[str]],
1077	) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
1078	    """Apply rewrites in-place-equivalent (returns new dict).
1079	
1080	    None rewrites = drop the sentence from its section.
1081	    String rewrites = replace the sentence at that (section, index).
1082	    """
1083	    new_sections: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
1084	    for section_title, sentences in sections.items():
1085	        new_sentences: list[str] = []
1086	        for idx, sentence in enumerate(sentences):
1087	            key = (section_title, idx)
1088	            if key in rewrites:
1089	                replacement = rewrites[key]
1090	                if replacement is None:
1091	                    # Drop redundant entirely
1092	                    continue
1093	                new_sentences.append(replacement)
1094	            else:
1095	                new_sentences.append(sentence)
1096	        new_sections[section_title] = new_sentences
1097	    return new_sections
1098	
1099	
1100	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1101	# Top-level entry point
1102	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1103	
1104	
1105	async def dedup_pass(
1106	    sections: dict[str, list[str]],
1107	    llm_callable: Callable[[str, str], Any],
1108	    section_order: Optional[list[str]] = None,
1109	) -> tuple[dict[str, list[str]], dict[str, Any]]:
1110	    """End-to-end dedup pass.
1111	
1112	    Args:
1113	        sections: dict mapping section_title -> list of sentence strings.
1114	            Typically the verified sentences from each section after
1115	            multi_section generation.
1116	        llm_callable: async callable(system, prompt) -> response.
1117	        section_order: explicit PRIMARY ordering; defaults to insertion.
1118	
1119	    Returns:
1120	        (new_sections, telemetry) where telemetry includes:
1121	            - n_groups: number of duplicate-fact groups detected
1122	            - n_redundants: total redundant sentences flagged
1123	            - n_rewrites_applied: successful rewrites
1124	            - n_drops: sentences dropped because rewrite returned None
1125	    """
1126	    groups = build_groups(sections, section_order=section_order)
1127	    n_redundants = count_redundancy(groups)
1128	    telemetry: dict[str, Any] = {
1129	        "n_groups": len(groups),
1130	        "n_redundants": n_redundants,
1131	        "n_rewrites_applied": 0,
1132	        "n_drops": 0,
1133	    }
1134	    if not groups:
1135	        return sections, telemetry
1136	
1137	    logger.info(
1138	        "[fact_dedup] %d duplicate-fact groups detected, "
1139	        "%d redundant sentences to rewrite",
1140	        len(groups), n_redundants,
1141	    )
1142	    rewrites = await rewrite_redundant_sentences(groups, llm_callable)
1143	    for _k, v in rewrites.items():
1144	        if v is None:
1145	            telemetry["n_drops"] += 1
1146	        else:
1147	            telemetry["n_rewrites_applied"] += 1
1148	
1149	    new_sections = apply_rewrites(sections, rewrites)
1150	    logger.info(

1151	        "[fact_dedup] applied=%d, dropped=%d",
1152	        telemetry["n_rewrites_applied"], telemetry["n_drops"],
1153	    )
1154	    return new_sections, telemetry
1155

1	"""GH #1259 (FIX B) — POST-FETCH checkpoint for resume-from-nearest durability.
2	
3	The honest-sweep pipeline has ONE checkpoint today: F04 ``corpus_snapshot.json``
4	(``corpus_snapshot.py``), written at the single pre-GENERATION seam — AFTER STORM +
5	fetch + every merge lane AND after the slow embedding-rerank / evidence selection. A
6	run killed DURING fetch or DURING the embedding-rerank (the costliest, network- and
7	CPU-bound stretch) lands BEFORE that single late checkpoint, so a 72-minute run is
8	lost entirely and ``--resume`` has nothing to reload.
9	
10	This module adds the highest-value EARLIER checkpoint: it persists the RAW FETCHED
11	CORPUS at the post-fetch / post-merge seam — after ``run_live_retrieval`` returns and
12	the additive deepener/agentic/STORM merge lanes have folded in, but BEFORE the
13	embedding-rerank + ``select_evidence_for_generation``. On ``--resume`` the caller
14	reconstructs ``retrieval`` from this snapshot, SKIPS STORM + fetch + every merge lane,
15	and RE-RUNS the embedding-rerank + selection + generation on the reloaded corpus.
16	
17	RESUME-FROM-NEAREST: the two checkpoints form a stage order
18	    post-fetch (fetch_snapshot)  <  post-selection (corpus_snapshot).
19	The runner prefers the LATER checkpoint when both exist (it skips strictly more work);
20	it falls back to the earlier fetch_snapshot when only that one was written (the kill
21	landed before selection completed). The two modules are independent files with
22	independent schema versions; this one is intentionally NARROWER (no ``evidence_for_gen``,
23	since selection has not run yet).
24	
25	HARD INVARIANT (CLAUDE.md §-1.3, ABSOLUTE — enforced by design here):
26	    A checkpoint carries DATA, NEVER A VERDICT.
27	    The snapshot stores ONLY the retrieved/merged EVIDENCE corpus (classified sources +
28	    evidence rows as plain dict data) and the retrieval COUNTS for the manifest
29	    envelope. It stores NO faithfulness verdict, NO strict_verify result, NO
30	    NLI/4-role/D8 decision, NO "verified" flag, NO selected/billed pool (selection has
31	    not happened). On ``--resume`` the caller RE-RUNS selection + strict_verify / NLI /
32	    4-role / D8 on this DATA from scratch — exactly as a fresh run does. Restoring a
33	    cached faithfulness verdict would be a RELAXATION of the only hard gate and is an
34	    auto-P0; this module makes that impossible by never serializing a verdict.
35	
36	The snapshot is a plain JSON document (schema_version pinned) so it is human-auditable
37	and forward/backward inspectable. The retrieval payload shape is IDENTICAL to the one
38	``corpus_snapshot._retrieval_payload`` writes, so ``corpus_snapshot.reconstruct_retrieval``
39	rebuilds the ``LiveRetrievalResult`` from either snapshot (shared, not duplicated). No
40	pickle, no code execution on load.
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import json
46	import os
47	from dataclasses import asdict
48	from pathlib import Path
49	from typing import Any
50	
51	# Bump on any incompatible schema change. A reload that sees a different version FAILS
52	# LOUD (refuses to resume) rather than silently feeding a stale-shaped corpus to the
53	# downstream selection — LAW II no-silent-downgrade. Independent of corpus_snapshot's
54	# version: the two files evolve separately.
55	FETCH_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
56	
57	# The canonical post-fetch snapshot filename inside a per-query run_dir. Distinct from
58	# corpus_snapshot.json so resume-from-nearest can detect each stage independently.
59	FETCH_SNAPSHOT_FILENAME = "fetch_snapshot.json"
60	
61	# Stage pointer value. Only ``post_fetch`` is persisted by this module (the seam after
62	# fetch + merge, before embedding-rerank/selection).
63	STAGE_POST_FETCH = "post_fetch"
64	
65	
66	def fetch_snapshot_path(run_dir: Path) -> Path:
67	    """Deterministic post-fetch snapshot location for a per-query run_dir."""
68	    return Path(run_dir) / FETCH_SNAPSHOT_FILENAME
69	
70	
71	def _retrieval_payload(retrieval: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
72	    """Serialize the retrieval fields the resume path + manifest envelope need.
73	
74	    DATA ONLY — counts + the two evidence collections. ``classified_sources`` is a list
75	    of flat ``CorpusSource`` dataclasses (``asdict``-safe); ``evidence_rows`` is already
76	    ``list[dict]``. Every count is read with a default so a partial/test retrieval object
77	    round-trips. NO verdict, NO gate result. Field-for-field IDENTICAL to
78	    ``corpus_snapshot._retrieval_payload`` so ``reconstruct_retrieval`` is shared.
79	    """
80	    # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311) P1-1: corpus_asdict omits the W2 keys at default so the
81	    # fetch snapshot is byte-identical when W2 OFF (kill-switch contract).
82	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.corpus_approval_gate import corpus_asdict
83	    return {
84	        "classified_sources": [corpus_asdict(s) for s in getattr(retrieval, "classified_sources", []) or []],
85	        "evidence_rows": list(getattr(retrieval, "evidence_rows", []) or []),
86	        "notes": list(getattr(retrieval, "notes", []) or []),
87	        "counts": {
88	            "total_candidates_pre_filter": getattr(retrieval, "total_candidates_pre_filter", 0),
89	            "candidates_kept_by_scope": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_kept_by_scope", 0),
90	            "candidates_kept_by_offtopic": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_kept_by_offtopic", 0),
91	            "candidates_fetched": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_fetched", 0),
92	            "candidates_failed_fetch": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_failed_fetch", 0),
93	            "candidates_total": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_total", 0),
94	            "candidates_processed": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_processed", 0),
95	            "extraction_finding_rows": getattr(retrieval, "extraction_finding_rows", 0),
96	            "corpus_truncated": bool(getattr(retrieval, "corpus_truncated", False)),
97	        },
98	        "api_calls": dict(getattr(retrieval, "api_calls", {}) or {}),
99	    }
100	
101	
102	def save_fetch_snapshot(
103	    run_dir: Path,
104	    *,
105	    run_id: str,
106	    question: str,
107	    slug: str,
108	    domain: str,
109	    retrieval: Any,
110	    journal_metadata_sidecar: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
111	    stage: str = STAGE_POST_FETCH,
112	) -> Path:
113	    """Persist the post-fetch corpus snapshot. Returns the written path.
114	
115	    Atomic write (temp + os.replace) so a kill DURING the snapshot write never leaves a
116	    truncated/half-parsed file that a later --resume would choke on.
117	
118	    NOTE: there is NO ``evidence_for_gen`` argument — selection has NOT run at this seam.
119	    On resume the caller RE-RUNS ``select_evidence_for_generation`` on the reconstructed
120	    corpus. Storing a billed/selected pool here would be wrong (it does not exist yet).
121	
122	    GH #1259 (Codex diff-gate P1): ``journal_metadata_sidecar`` is the MERGED per-stage
123	    journal-article metadata (``dict[canonical_url -> meta_entry]``) the runner builds from
124	    the retrieval + expansion + deepener + agentic stages just before the journal_only
125	    filter. It is persisted as DATA so a ``journal_only`` fetch-resume reconstructs the SAME
126	    metadata a fresh run had; without it the reconstructed corpus carries no sidecar and the
127	    journal_only filter would reject every row as ``no_journal_metadata`` and ABORT instead
128	    of round-tripping. This is metadata ABOUT sources (peer-review/source-type/DOI signals),
129	    NOT a faithfulness verdict — it carries no strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / D8 decision and
130	    no ``verified`` flag. It defaults empty so a non-journal_only run round-trips unchanged.
131	    """
132	    run_dir = Path(run_dir)
133	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
134	        "schema_version": FETCH_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION,
135	        "stage": stage,
136	        "run_id": run_id,
137	        "question": question,
138	        "slug": slug,
139	        "domain": domain,
140	        # DATA: the raw fetched + merged retrieval corpus + counts for manifest
141	        # reconstruction. NO selected pool, NO verdict.
142	        "retrieval": _retrieval_payload(retrieval),
143	        # DATA: the MERGED journal-article metadata sidecar (keyed by canonical URL) so a
144	        # journal_only fetch-resume sees identical per-source signals to a fresh run. This
145	        # is source metadata, NOT a faithfulness verdict.
146	        "journal_metadata_sidecar": dict(journal_metadata_sidecar or {}),
147	    }
148	    path = fetch_snapshot_path(run_dir)
149	    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + ".tmp")
150	    tmp.write_text(
151	        json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
152	        encoding="utf-8",
153	    )
154	    os.replace(tmp, path)
155	    return path
156	
157	
158	class FetchSnapshotError(RuntimeError):
159	    """Raised when a --resume reload cannot trust the on-disk post-fetch snapshot.
160	
161	    FAIL LOUD (LAW II): a corrupt/version-mismatched/empty-corpus snapshot must NOT
162	    silently fall back to a fresh retrieval under --resume (the operator asked to resume
163	    an interrupted run; a silent restart would re-bill fetch and mask the interruption).
164	    The caller surfaces this as a clean abort.
165	    """
166	
167	
168	def load_fetch_snapshot(run_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
169	    """Reload + validate the post-fetch snapshot for --resume. Returns the parsed payload.
170	
171	    Raises FetchSnapshotError on absent / malformed / version-mismatched / empty-corpus
172	    snapshots so the caller fails loud instead of resuming on bad data. Returns DATA ONLY
173	    — the caller MUST re-run selection + every faithfulness gate on it.
174	
175	    The "non-empty corpus" predicate here is ``retrieval.evidence_rows`` (NOT
176	    ``evidence_for_gen`` — that key does not exist in a post-fetch snapshot, by design):
177	    a fetch snapshot with no evidence rows is nothing to resume from.
178	    """
179	    path = fetch_snapshot_path(run_dir)
180	    if not path.exists():
181	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
182	            f"--resume: no post-fetch snapshot at {path} (nothing to resume; run without "
183	            f"--resume for a fresh run)"
184	        )
185	    try:
186	        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
187	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
188	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
189	            f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot at {path} is unreadable/corrupt: {exc}"
190	        ) from exc
191	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
192	        raise FetchSnapshotError(f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot at {path} is not a JSON object")
193	    version = payload.get("schema_version")
194	    if version != FETCH_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION:
195	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
196	            f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot schema_version {version!r} != expected "
197	            f"{FETCH_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION} at {path}; refusing to resume on a stale-shaped "
198	            f"corpus (re-run fresh)"
199	        )
200	    # Codex diff-gate P2-2: a malformed snapshot must FAIL LOUD at LOAD (LAW II), not later
201	    # as a generic no-source error deep in the resume path. Validate the stage pointer is the
202	    # one this module writes, so a corpus_snapshot (or any other staged checkpoint) handed to
203	    # the post-fetch loader is rejected explicitly rather than silently mis-reconstructed.
204	    stage = payload.get("stage")
205	    if stage != STAGE_POST_FETCH:
206	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
207	            f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot at {path} has stage {stage!r} != expected "
208	            f"{STAGE_POST_FETCH!r}; refusing to resume on a non-post-fetch checkpoint"
209	        )
210	    retrieval = payload.get("retrieval") or {}
211	    if not (isinstance(retrieval, dict) and retrieval.get("evidence_rows")):
212	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
213	            f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot at {path} has an empty retrieval corpus; refusing "
214	            f"to resume a run with no fetched evidence"
215	        )
216	    # Codex diff-gate P2-2: also require a non-empty, well-shaped ``classified_sources`` list.
217	    # reconstruct_retrieval rehydrates CorpusSource(**row) from these, so each entry must be a
218	    # mapping; an empty/malformed list would yield a source-less corpus that the adequacy +
219	    # journal_only gates can only reject downstream — fail loud here instead.
220	    sources = retrieval.get("classified_sources")
221	    if not (isinstance(sources, list) and sources):
222	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
223	            f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot at {path} has an empty/malformed classified_sources; "
224	            f"refusing to resume a run with no source corpus"
225	        )
226	    if not all(isinstance(s, dict) for s in sources):
227	        raise FetchSnapshotError(
228	            f"--resume: post-fetch snapshot at {path} has a malformed classified_sources entry "
229	            f"(every source must be a JSON object); refusing to resume on corrupt source data"
230	        )
231	    return payload
232
